speakers.name: Karsten Wildberger
speakers.title: Federal Minister for Digital Transformation and Government Modernisation
speakers.bio: Germany's Federal Minister for Digital Transformation and Government Modernisation

speakers.name: Markus Blume
speakers.title: Bavarian State Minister for Science and the Arts
speakers.bio: Markus Blume has served as Bavarian State Minister for Science and the Arts since February 2022. Prior to this, he was Secretary General of the CSU from 2018 to 2022. He has been a member of the Bavarian State Parliament since 2008, representing the Munich-Ramersdorf constituency as a directly elected representative. A father of three, he lives in Munich. Before entering politics, Blume worked as a founder and board member of a medium-sized enterprise.

speakers.name: Thomas Jarzombek
speakers.title: Parliamentary State Secretary to the Federal Minister for Digital Transformation and Government Modernisation
speakers.bio: Thomas Jarzombek was born in Düsseldorf and has been active in politics for more than 25 years. He served as a member of the Düsseldorf City Council and as a member of the State Parliament of North Rhine-Westphalia. Since 2009, he has been a Member of the German Bundestag. From 2013 to 2018, he acted as the spokesperson for the Digital Agenda of the CDU/CSU parliamentary group. Between 2018 and 2021, Thomas Jarzombek served as the Federal Government Coordinator for Aerospace, and from 2019 to 2021, as the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs’ Representative for the Digital Economy and Start-ups. Most recently, from 2021 to 2025, he was the spokesperson for Education and Research of the CDU/CSU parliamentary group and a member of the Committee on Digital Affairs. Since May 2025, he has served as Parliamentary State Secretary to the Federal Minister for Digital Transformation and Government Modernisation.

speakers.name: Michael Kagan
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Michael Kagan serves as a Chief Technology Officer (CTO) of NVIDIA since May 2020, following the company’s acquisition of Mellanox Technologies, the company which he co-founded. With over 40 years in the tech industry, Michael’s career began at Intel, where he contributed to 80387 math co-processor design, led 8060XP processor architecture and managed Pentium MMX design team. At Mellanox he played a pivotal role in developing accelerated networking technologies that enhanced cloud data center efficiency. Today, as Nvidia’s CTO, Michael is focused on architecting solutions for evolving demands of artificial intelligence, developing architecture of data center as a single unit of computing – the AI factory. Michael focuses on full-stack solution, emphasizing importance of software in conjunction with hardware, advocating for the future where AI computing is accessible across diverse platforms from mega-scale data centers to mobile devices.

speakers.name: Werner Vogels
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Dr. Werner Vogels is Chief Technology Officer at Amazon.com where he is responsible for driving the company's customer-centric technology vision. As one of the forces behind Amazon's approach to cloud computing, he is passionate about helping young businesses reach global scale, and transforming enterprises into fast-moving digital organizations. Vogels joined Amazon in 2004 from Cornell University where he was a distributed systems researcher. He has held technology leadership positions in companies that handle the transition of academic technology into industry. Vogels holds a PhD from the Vrije Universiteit in Amsterdam and has authored many articles on distributed systems technologies for enterprise computing. On his blog, all things distributed, Werner shares insights and thought leadership content on a range of topics, such as cloud computing, machine learning, artificial intelligence, as well as his annual technology predictions.

speakers.name: Thomas Dohmke
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Fascinated by software development since his childhood in Germany, Thomas Dohmke has built a career creating tools developers love. He is currently working on a new startup in this space. Formerly, Thomas was Chief Executive Officer of GitHub, where he led the company’s growth to over 150 million developers and oversaw the rise of GitHub Copilot — the world’s most widely adopted AI developer tool — now with over 20 million users and counting. Thomas is a celebrated TED speaker and holds a PhD in mechanical engineering from the University of Glasgow, UK.

speakers.name: Dana Lawson
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Dana Lawson is the Chief Technology Officer at Netlify, where she leads the Engineering, Product, and Design teams to innovate and deliver transformative digital experiences. With over two decades of leadership at industry-leading companies like GitHub, Heptio, and New Relic, Dana has a proven track record of scaling teams, fostering collaboration, and navigating complex technical landscapes. Throughout her career, she’s worn many hats across the product lifecycle, showcasing her versatility and expertise building impactful technology solutions. Driven by a genuine passion for people, Dana infuses her leadership with a blend of technical expertise and a spirit of fun, creating an inspiring and dynamic work environment.

speakers.name: Jeff Atwood
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: Jeff Atwood has spent his career building places where developers learn together at scale. He co-founded Stack Overflow and the Stack Exchange network, transforming Q&A into a global, searchable knowledge base that reshaped how programmers solve problems. He later co-founded Discourse, an open-source discussion platform used by companies, open-source projects, and public communities to host high-signal conversations on the modern web. Jeff is also the author of the influential Coding Horror blog, known for pragmatic (and occasionally contrarian) perspectives on software engineering, product decisions, and the incentives that shape human behaviour online. His work sits at the intersection of technology and community: how you design systems that are not only technically scalable, but socially sustainable, especially as AI changes how we search, learn, and collaborate.

speakers.name: Malte Ubl
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Malte Ubl is the CTO of Vercel, where he leads the development and delivery of the company’s suite of tools and features to enable developers to create at the moment of inspiration. Prior to joining Vercel, Malte was the Principal Engineer for Google Search Rendering and Engineering Director for Google’s Search on Laptops, Tablets, and Desktop. Malte has also created the frontend infrastructure for a number of Google Web Apps and the web at large. He is also the founder and curator of JSConf EU.

speakers.name: Markus Rautert
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Markus Rautert is Chief Technology Officer at adidas, where he leads global technology strategy across enterprise architecture, platform engineering, IT infrastructure, cybersecurity, and Data & AI. With nearly two decades at adidas, Markus has helped build the company’s digital backbone—scaling platforms, securing operations, and enabling data-driven decisions across brand, supply chain, and finance. His current focus is on strengthening cybersecurity and unlocking business value through advanced analytics. Markus speaks regularly at internal and industry events, connecting with developers and senior leaders alike. Offstage, he’s a lifelong gamer and passionate tech enthusiast.

speakers.name: Des Traynor
speakers.title: Co-Founder and Chief Strategy Officer
speakers.bio: Des Traynor is a Co-founder and the Chief Strategy Officer of Intercom and sits on Intercom’s board of directors. Des has led many teams within the company, including Product, Marketing, Customer Support, and Content, and has been directly responsible for building out even more functions within Intercom as well. Today, Des oversees Intercom's R&D team, based in the Dublin and London offices. Before being part of Intercom’s founding team, he was a UX consultant, a university lecturer in computer science, and also a Ph.D. researcher studying how to improve the quality of computer science education. Des also previously co-founded Exceptional. Des is an author and speaker on the topics of starting a startup, scaling a business, creating a product strategy and more. He shares his ideas and expertise in books, podcasts, and conference presentations. He also invests in many startups, some of which include Stripe, Miro, Notion, and Hopin.

speakers.name: Taroon Mandhana
speakers.title: CTO AI and Teamwork
speakers.bio: Taroon Mandhana is CTO of AI and Teamwork at Atlassian, a leading provider of productivity and collaboration software. In his role, Taroon oversees engineering teams responsible for Atlassian’s flagship products, including Jira, Confluence, and Loom—which are bundled within the Teamwork Collection. He also leads the development of Rovo, Atlassian's AI, which includes Search, Chat, Agents, and Studio. Outside of Atlassian, Taroon holds nearly 15 years of AI experience. In his previous roles, he led the Search team at Microsoft Bing and later joined Meta to lead their Facebook Search and Marketplace teams. In his second stint at Microsoft, he led the company’s content teams and built the personalized recommendation feed, Microsoft Start. Taroon graduated from the Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi, and received his Master’s from the University of Texas at Austin. In his free time, he enjoys going on hikes around the Pacific Northwest with his family.

speakers.name: Victor Savkin
speakers.title: CTO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Co-founder and CTO of Nx, an agentic build platform that scales CI and automatically fixes failed PRs. A former member of the Angular core team at Google. Victor has spent 15+ years developing high-performance frameworks and build tools for large-scale JavaScript applications, with a focus on enterprise developer experience.

speakers.name: Avani Prabhakar
speakers.title: Chief People  and AI Enablement Officer
speakers.bio: Atlassian Chief People and AI Enablement Officer, Avani Prabhakar, leads Atlassian's People and Transformations organisation with a sharp focus on unlocking potential in the AI era – blending purpose, systems thinking, and modern leadership to shape the future of work. Avani’s focus is on enabling the cultural transformation required alongside the technology shift – to build AI-fluent teams and unlock opportunity. She has been instrumental in pioneering the distributed work models and ways of working that underpin Atlassian’s Team Anywhere approach, and is actively embedding AI up-skilling, scaling, and innovation across the organisation. With 20+ years across tech, aviation, engineering, professional services and consulting, Avani keeps Atlassian's people-first approach practical and grounded, to build human + AI collaboration and capability in complex, high-growth environments.

speakers.name: Michael "Monty" Widenius
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Michael “Monty” Widenius is the co-founder of MariaDB, a renowned advocate for the open source software movement and one of the original developers of MySQL, the predecessor to MariaDB. He is currently serving as the CTO at MariaDB Plc., and was previously co-founder at SkySQL, and the CTO of MySQL AB until its sale to Sun Microsystems (now Oracle). Monty was also the founder of TCX DataKonsult AB, a Swedish data warehousing company. He was awarded the Finnish Software Entrepreneur of the Year prize in 2003. In 2015, Monty was selected as one of the 100 most influential persons in the Finnish IT market. Monty studied at the Helsinki University of Technology and lives in Finland.

speakers.name: Milin Desai
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Milin Desai currently serves as the CEO of Sentry. Used by more than 4 million developers and 150,000 organizations worldwide, Sentry helps every developer detect, understand, and fix broken code, fast. Milin joined Sentry from VMware where he helped build out multiple lines of business from networking (0->1b+) to multi cloud (0->100M+ SAAS). Earlier in his career, he held engineering and product roles at Veritas and Riverbed.

speakers.name: Agur Jõgi
speakers.title: Tech leader and former CTO
speakers.bio: Agur Jõgi is an experienced technology leader with extensive experience in building and scaling SaaS platforms. He most recently served as Chief Technology Officer at Pipedrive, a leading sales CRM platform used by over 100,000 companies globally. With his 35 years of experience across Skype, Bigbank, Elion, and askRobin, Agur shares insights into his leadership through engineering and product innovations. At Pipedrive, he has championed the adoption of data-driven product strategies, blending deep technical execution with a user-first mindset.

speakers.name: Dr. Ramin Hasani
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Dr. Ramin Hasani is the co-founder and CEO of Liquid AI. Previously, he was jointly appointed as a Principal Machine Learning Scientist at the Vanguard Group and a Research Affiliate at MIT CSAIL. Ramin’s research focuses on robust deep learning and decision-making in complex dynamical systems. Prior to that, he was a Postdoctoral Associate at CSAIL MIT, leading research on modeling intelligence and sequential decision-making. He received his Ph.D. degree with distinction in Computer Science from the Vienna University of Technology, Austria. His Ph.D. dissertation and continued research on Liquid Neural Networks have been recognized internationally with numerous nominations and awards.

speakers.name: Stephanie Cohen
speakers.title: Chief Strategy Officer
speakers.bio: Stephanie Cohen joined Cloudflare in 2024 as the company’s first-ever Chief Strategy Officer. A seasoned operating executive, she brings more than two decades of experience at Goldman Sachs where she advised the leadership and boards of some of the world's most important companies and institutions. In addition to her time in Investment Banking, she was Goldman Sachs's Chief Strategy Officer, ran two multi-billion dollar global segments and served as a member of their Management Committee. In her role at Cloudflare, Stephanie leads the company's efforts in expanding the breadth and depth of its presence in boardrooms and the C-suite on its path to $5B in revenue and beyond. She focuses on the most important initiatives driving Cloudflare's growth, including the future of the network, international expansion, AI and the developer platform. Stephanie has a Bachelors of Science in Finance from the University of Illinois. She is a trustee for the Economic Club of New York and Park City Day School, a member of the Board of Quill.org and an Ambassador for US Ski & Snowboard. She is also a member of the Board of Directors of Fiserv.

speakers.name: Daniel Geisel
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Daniel Geisel is CEO of Mercedes‑Benz Tech Innovation and Head of Home of Tech. In this role, he is responsible for the global software organization within Mercedes‑Benz IT, driving technological development and innovation in close collaboration with the business functions. Under his leadership, the organization is evolving from individual tech hubs into a globally integrated software tech organization, combining software engineering excellence, digital solutions and strong execution capabilities. He works closely with international teams across locations including Spain, South Africa, India and Malaysia.

speakers.name: Daniel Gebler
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Daniel Gebler is CTO of Picnic, the world’s fastest growing online supermarket that makes grocery shopping simple, fun, and affordable for everyone. Previously, he was Director R&D of Fredhopper, responsible for the product and technology roadmap, and led engineering teams located in Amsterdam and Sofia. Daniel holds a PhD in Computer Science and an MBA. He published papers, edited journals, and chaired international workshops on software correctness.

speakers.name: Loredana Crisan
speakers.title: Chief Design Officer
speakers.bio: Loredana Crisan is the Chief Design Officer at Figma, where she leads design, UX writing, brand studio and research. Prior to joining Figma, she spent nine years at Meta, where she held product and design leadership roles across Messenger, Instagram and GenAI initiatives. Before Loredana’s career in product and design, she was a musician and sound engineer.

speakers.name: Stefan Ostwald
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: Stefan Ostwald is Chief AI Officer and Co-Founder of Parloa, a leading AI company redefining customer service through AI agents. Stefan's passion for conversational AI dates back to 2016, when he co-founded Future of Voice — one of Europe's first voice AI agencies. In 2018, he went on to found Parloa, where he now leads the agentic AI research team, bridging state-of-the-art research with enterprise-grade solutions. His mission: making AI so reliable and intuitive that every conversation deepens the customer relationship.

speakers.name: Christian Trummer
speakers.title: Co-founder and Chief Scientist
speakers.bio: Christian Trummer is the founder and Chief Scientist of Bitpanda. Previously serving as CTO for nearly a decade, he scaled the engineering team to over 400 people, building the company's core trading platform and in-house banking system. He now leads a specialized team focused on embedding AI across the organization to enhance products and efficiency. Christian combines deep technical expertise with a vision for AI-driven transformation in fintech.

speakers.name: Philipp Schmid
speakers.title: Senior Engineer Dev Rel
speakers.bio: Philipp Schmid is a Senior AI Developer Relations Engineer at Google DeepMind working on Gemini, Gemma with the mission to help every developer and builder to create and benefit from AI in a responsible way.

speakers.name: Axel Schell
speakers.title: CTTO
speakers.bio: Axel Schell is driving the AI adoption at Allianz Technology and transforming the company into an innovative and customer centric tech company. He joined the Executive Management Team of Allianz Technology, the IT company of the Allianz group, as the Chief Technology and Transformation Officer in January 2021. His mission and passion to modernize and transform the IT landscape as well as the ways of working was rewarded with the “CIO of the Year 2021” award by the German speaking CIO Magazine and the “European Digital Leader of the Year 2023” award by CIONET. He started this transformation journey already in his previous roles: Since his start at Allianz in 2002 he has demonstrated not just a specialist knowledge on IT management but also a distinct feeling for the needs of the business and future Tech trends. He is a techie with knowledge for economy and vice versa: Axel completed his studies in Business Administration and obtained a doctoral degree in Information Systems from the University of Augsburg. He is fluent in German, English, and French. Axel is married and has two children. In his free time, he enjoys spending time with his family on a tennis court, coding and watching American basketball.

speakers.name: Raffi Krikorian
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Raffi Krikorian is Chief Technology Officer at Mozilla, where he leads efforts to build trustworthy technology that serves the public interest and strengthens human agency. A long-time member of the Mozilla community - serving on the Mozilla Foundation Board since 2023, Mozilla.ai’s Board since 2024, and the Mozilla.org Board since its inception - Raffi brings a record of impact across technology, politics, media, and philanthropy. He previously served as CTO of Emerson Collective, where he focused on how technology and data can be used to drive solutions that promote social good; as the first CTO of the Democratic National Committee, where he built the technology, data, and security infrastructure that supported Democratic candidates nationwide; as Director of Uber’s Advanced Technologies Center, where he led the rollout of the first passenger-carrying self-driving car fleet; and as Vice President of Platform Engineering at Twitter, where he designed and scaled the systems that powered Twitter’s global platform. He also created Technically Optimistic - a podcast and Substack exploring technology’s impact on society - which during its run reached #2 on Apple’s tech podcast charts and featured conversations with leaders including Sal Khan, Nobel Peace Prize laureate Maria Ressa, Cory Doctorow, and Congressman Jay Obernolte.

speakers.name: Cédric Gégout
speakers.title: VP of Product
speakers.bio: Cédric Gégout, Canonical's VP of Product, is a strong believer in open source as both the foundation of major platform shifts and the primary source of innovation. Before Canonical, he founded Streamezzo, later led cloud-native strategy at Amdocs, and holds a PhD in AI. He has spent his career adopting new technologies early, from AI and mobile platforms to cloud-native systems, Kubernetes, secure containers, and edge computing with the same passion for helping developers turn open innovation into enterprise-ready solutions.

speakers.name: Maxim Schepelin
speakers.title: Engineering Manager
speakers.bio: Maxim Schepelin has been an engineering leader in various industries, including Game Dev, e-commerce, and travel building teams capable of launching products from idea to world-scale. With over a decade of experience leading engineering teams, Maxim has mastered the art and science of management, as well as how to exit both Vim and Emacs. Maxim continues exploring ways to build effective engineering teams that deliver value at a sustainable pace.

speakers.name: Alexander Glätzle
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Dr. Alexander Glätzle is CEO and co-founder of planqc, the company building Germany’s first quantum computer based on neutral atoms. A quantum physicist turned entrepreneur, he is on a mission to turn quantum technology from a scientific breakthrough into a powerful industrial tool. After leading research on ultracold atoms and quantum annealing at the University of Oxford and the Centre for Quantum Technologies in Singapore, Glätzle moved into industry to shape strategies for quantum computing and blockchain at a leading global consultancy. In 2018, he took the leap to build the future himself. Today, he is driving Europe’s quantum ambition with planqc. Backed by €50 million in funding, the company is developing a 1,000-qubit quantum computer for the Leibniz Supercomputing Centre and building quantum computing capabilities for research and industry. His vision is bold and clear: quantum computers made in Europe, solving real-world problems worth billions.

speakers.name: Annabella Bassler
speakers.title: CFO
speakers.bio: Dr. Annabella Bassler is Group CFO of Ringier, board member and Initiator of the EqualVoice initiative. She combines financial leadership with a strong commitment to advancing gender equality, driving initiatives that address gender stereotypes and unconscious bias through the EqualVoice-Assistant. With extensive experience in corporate governance and strategy, she stands for modern, responsible leadership. In her keynote, she shares insights on leading large organizations through cultural change and company-wide transformation.

speakers.name: Gülnaz Öneş
speakers.title: Group CIO
speakers.bio: As Group CIO at RWE, Gülnaz Öneş spearheads the energy transition through technology, with a strong emphasis on Data & AI and business agility. She brings a distinguished track record across the automotive, financial services, and energy sectors. Gülnaz spent more than two decades within the Daimler Group, holding executive roles including CTO at Daimler Truck and CIO at Mercedes-Benz Mobility. Known for her people-first, integrative leadership style, Gülnaz turns technology and data into tangible business outcomes, strengthening collaboration and delivering measurable impact across global organisations.

speakers.name: Benjamin Mann
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Benjamin was appointed Chief Technology Officer (CTO) at Delivery Hero in June 2024. He leads the engineering teams driving the technological expertise, operations, and processes supporting Delivery Hero’s platform applications across all its markets. Prior to joining Delivery Hero in January 2023 as Senior Vice President of Engineering, Benjamin was CTO at foodpanda, one of Asia’s leading food delivery apps. With a career spanning 25 years in the technology industry, Benjamin has been and continues to be at the forefront of innovation across the globe.

speakers.name: Vinesh Sukumar
speakers.title: VP Product Management and AI/Gen AI
speakers.bio: Dr. Vinesh Sukumar currently serves as Vice President of Product Management at Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. Vinesh acts as head of AI/ML, leading AI product definition, strategy and solution deployment across multiple business units. He has about 20 years of industry experience spread across research, engineering and application deployment. He holds a doctorate degree specializing in imaging and vision systems and also holds an MBA focused on strategy and marketing. He is a regular speaker in many AI industry forums and has authored several journal papers and two technical books.

speakers.name: George Hantzaras
speakers.title: Director Engineering
speakers.bio: George is a distributed systems expert and a hands-on engineering leader. He is a Director of Engineering at MongoDB, focusing on implementing cloud native technologies at enterprise scale. He is an Ambassador of the Data on Kubernetes community and the author of The Platform Engineering Playbook, by Packt. Most recently, he has been a speaker at global events like Kubecon, OpenSource Summit, Hashiconf, LeadDev, SaaStr, and more.

speakers.name: Lilia Gargouri
speakers.title: Board Member and Head of QA
speakers.bio: Lilia Gargouri stands for sustainable, scalable, and efficient quality assurance in complex eGovernment projects. She is committed to accessibility, trains career changers, and passionately advocates for greater visibility and equal opportunities for women in IT. As the inventor of a model-based, low-code test automation tool, she has significantly simplified and accelerated quality assurance in critical eGovernment projects. A member of the German Testing Board, she actively drives the development of industry quality standards.

speakers.name: Martin Remmelgas
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Martin is CEO of Codemagic. Codemagic started as a CI/CD product for mobile developers and now offers and over-the-air (OTA) updates as well. Fun fact: Martin studied to become a chemist, but after graduation, he began his career in technology in 2017.

speakers.name: Guy Korland
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: The speaker is Guy Korland, PhD. in C.S. (From TAU) FalkorDB’s CEO and Co-founder, whose experience spans more than 20 years in the database and software engineering industry. Guy was previously SVP of Innovation and CTO of Incubation in Redis.

speakers.name: Josip Stuhli
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: As the CTO of Sofascore, a leading live score service that provides real-time sports data, Josip is responsible for overseeing the architecture, development, and scaling their web and mobile platforms, as well as leading a team of talented engineers and developers. Since joining in 2010, he has overseen the architecture, development, and scaling of the company's web and mobile platforms, guiding a talented team of engineers and developers. His journey at Sofascore began as a software engineer, progressing to system architect and ultimately CTO, reflecting a deep understanding of the company's evolution and a significant contribution to its success. Josip’s passion for technology finds its roots in a youthful curiosity for programming languages, ultimately leading to a formal academic background in computer science. With extensive experience and expertise in web technologies and distributed systems, he thrives on tackling complex challenges that demand creative and innovative solutions. His unwavering mission is to deliver exceptional, reliable solutions that not only meet the needs of users and partners but also continuously improve and optimize Sofascore's performance and functionality.

speakers.name: Ross Kukulinski
speakers.title: VP of Product
speakers.bio: Ross Kukulinski is Vice President of Product at Tailscale, where he leads product strategy and execution across the company's secure connectivity platform. Ross is a technical product leader who builds businesses at the intersection of open source, cloud infrastructure, and developer experience. He has spent nearly two decades working on cloud-native technologies, helping organizations move from early adoption to reliable, large-scale production use. His work has focused on turning complex distributed systems into products that are easier to adopt, operate, and trust—without hiding the underlying engineering or limiting flexibility. Before joining Tailscale, Ross was VP of Product Management at Kong, where he led the transformation from packaged on-prem software to a cloud-first platform. Prior to Kong, he spent four years at VMware following the acquisition of Heptio, where he was an initial employee. At Heptio and VMware, he led product for Kubernetes platforms including Tanzu Kubernetes Grid, working closely with engineering teams and customers to shape how modern infrastructure is packaged, delivered, and operated. Ross is passionate about building products that balance strong technical foundations with clear customer value, mentoring future product leaders, and earning developer trust through great experiences.

speakers.name: Max Fischer
speakers.title: Director Engineering
speakers.bio: Director Engineering Trade Republic, responsible for the Engineering, Data and Cyber Security organisation. Earlier CTO Bluestep Bank, VP Klarna, Executive VP Nordea, Chief Business Architect SEB, Chief Architect TeliaSonera

speakers.name: Anastasiia Stefanska
speakers.title: Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: I’m a data professional with over decade of experience in analytics and consulting, specializing in data, storytelling and machine learning to unlock the data value. With five Tableau certifications under my belt, one Snowflake and two GCP Cloud certifications, and hands-on expertise in data architecture and enablement, I thrive at the intersection of data, business strategy, and community. I am a Snowflake Data Superhero recognized for my contributions to data community. I am a Solutions Architect at TUI, where I design tools, programs, enablement paths, and events to empower data-driven culture. Beyond work, I organize meetups and have founded Women on Snowflake and LGBTQIA+ communities.

speakers.name: Carlos Sanchez
speakers.title: Principal Scientist
speakers.bio: Carlos Sanchez is a Principal Scientist at Adobe Experience Manager, specializing in software automation, from build tools to Continuous Delivery and Progressive Delivery. Involved in Open Source for over 20 years, he is the author of the Jenkins Kubernetes plugin and a member of the Apache Software Foundation amongst other open source groups, contributing to several projects, such as Kubernetes, Jenkins or Apache Maven.

speakers.name: Ivan Burazin
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Ivan Burazin co-founded Codeanywhere, the first cloud IDE, in 2009, and later created Shift, Europe’s leading dev conference (acquired by Infobip in 2021). He then joined Infobip’s executive board as Chief Developer Experience Officer. In 2023, he co-founded Daytona, an open-source platform enabling AI agents to securely interact with runtime environments. Backed by $7M, Daytona serves startups to Fortune 500s, helping agents reach their full potential.

speakers.name: Anja Kunkel
speakers.title: Principal Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Anja is a Principal Fullstack Engineer based in Berlin. She loves systems thinking - but doesn't love buzzwords all that much. Her aim is to understand and influence human dynamics behind software and organizations. She's equally passionate about hard and soft skills. Over time, she's become more and more convinced that soft skills are the true hard skills. Off stage, she's a sports enthusiast, current local champion in wakeskating, and a fan of pushing limits – whether on the water or in the workplace.

speakers.name: Johannes Kopf
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Johannes Kopf, LL.M. is the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Public Employment Service Austria, AMS, where he continues to shape Austria's policymaking for labor market and employment. Prior to becoming executive chairman in July 2023, Johannes served as a member of the Executive Board of the Public Employment Service Austria from July 2006 to June 2023, as well as Chairman of the Network of European Public Employment Services (PES) from July 2019 to June 2023. Born and raised in Vienna, Austria, Johannes holds a doctorate in law and an LL.M. in European law. He is a well-respected labor market expert, having gained valuable professional experience in the cabinet of former Minister for Economics and Labour, Dr. Martin Bartenstein, and as a member of the Board of Directors of the AMS Austria, both from 2003 to mid-2006. Before his time in the ministry, he was a consultant for the Federation of Austrian Industries, also with a focus on labour market and employment policies. In addition to his ongoing work at AMS Austria, Johannes was awarded the Humanity Prize of the Heinrich Treichl Foundation by the Austrian Red Cross in 2017. He is also an award-winning photographer and highly regarded DJ.

speakers.name: Markus Hofmarcher
speakers.title: Research Director
speakers.bio: Markus Hofmarcher develops AI workflows at ExtensityAI using autonomous agents and modern language models, particularly for complex multi-stage processes. Previously, he conducted research for several years at the Institute for Machine Learning at JKU Linz, where he focused extensively on deep learning and reinforcement learning.

speakers.name: David vonThenen
speakers.title: Senior AI/ML Engineer
speakers.bio: David is a Senior AI/ML Engineer within the Office of the CTO at NetApp, where he’s dedicated to empowering developers to build, scale, and deploy AI/ML solutions in production environments. He brings deep expertise in building and training models for applications such as NLP, vision, real-time analytics, and even classifying debilitating diseases. His mission is to help users build, train, and deploy AI models efficiently, making advanced machine learning accessible to users of all levels. Before NetApp, he was heavily involved in the AI/ML community, specifically in conversational AI solutions and driving AI platform growth in a DevRel and pre-sales role. David frequently shares his insights at industry conferences and events, offering hands-on guidance for implementing AI/ML in cloud environments. David's prior experience includes contributing to the Kubernetes and CNCF ecosystems, working hands-on with VMware virtualization, implementing backup/recovery solutions, and developing hardware storage adapter firmware and drivers.

speakers.name: Etienne Bernard
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Etienne is an AI/ML expert, co-founder & CEO of NuMind — a startup developing LLMs specialized in information extraction. Etienne holds a physics PhD (ENS+MIT), led the ML group of Wolfram Research, and wrote Introduction to Machine Learning.

speakers.name: Bjarne Valentin Rentz
speakers.title: SRE
speakers.bio: Bjarne Rentz is a Site Reliability Engineer with a robust background in Software Engineering at the Hamburg Port Authority. He possesses extensive hands-on experience building resilient, cloud-native applications. Beyond his daily role, Bjarne is deeply fascinated by the underlying mechanics of technological and always tries to understand one level under the abstraction.

speakers.name: Piotr Zaniewski
speakers.title: Head of Engineering Enablement
speakers.bio: An active contributor to open source, content creator on Medium and YouTube, focusing on practical, scalable solutions in cloud-native environments. DevOps and Platform Engineering practitioner and advocate.

speakers.name: James Cha-Earley
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: I'm a developer advocate at Snowflake who gets excited about helping developers do cool things with AI and data in the cloud. I found my happy place bridging the gap between complex tech and the developers who want to use it. Think of me as a technical matchmaker – connecting developers with the right tools and watching the magic happen. When I'm not geeking out about AI, you'll find me spending time with my son and collecting new hobbies (because why stick to just one?)

speakers.name: Daniel Schley
speakers.title: Head of Data Engineering
speakers.bio: Daniel Schley is a senior engineering leader with over 20 years of experience building and scaling software, data, and platform teams in complex, fast-growing organizations. He has led teams responsible for production systems through periods of low maturity, high autonomy, and structural ambiguity, helping turn recurring incidents and operational chaos into clearer decision-making, stronger technical foundations, and more effective engineering practices. His work sits at the intersection of decision-making under pressure, incident-driven learning, and modern platform engineering. With a growing focus on how AI-assisted development amplifies existing strengths and weaknesses in engineering organizations, Daniel speaks from hard-earned experience, sharing concrete failure modes, real incident patterns, and pragmatic approaches teams can apply immediately to improve reliability and learning at scale.

speakers.name: Julian Totzek-Hallhuber
speakers.title: Lead Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Julian Totzek-Hallhuber is Lead Solutions Architect at XBOW and has spent nearly two decades on the front lines of application security. Before XBOW, he led the solution architect team at Veracode. From application development and penetration testing to web application security, Julian helps enterprises ship code that holds up under real-world attack. He's the author of the book Mastering the Art of Application Security Testing, a regular voice on the international conference, trade fair, and webinar circuit, and a familiar name in tech PR, quoted across numerous articles and trade publications. He has also contributed to projects under www.webappsec.org. Between keynotes, you'll find him writing and sharing field notes at julz.de. Equal parts technologist, strategist, and storyteller, Julian turns deep security expertise into clear, actionable guidance that resonates with developers, security leaders, and executives alike.

speakers.name: Helmut Schönenberger
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Scalable tech-startups are Helmut Schönenberger’s passion as co-founder and CEO of UnternehmerTUM, the Center for Innovation and Business Creation at Technische Universität München (TUM). He also serves as TUM’s Vice President Entrepreneurship, as a professor at TUM School of Management and as a member of the advisory board of tech companies. With his background as a founder, aerospace engineer and venture capitalist at UVC Partners, he loves to help new ventures solve strategy, recruiting, business development and future financing challenges.

speakers.name: Frank Schlesinger
speakers.title: CIO DACH Platforms
speakers.bio: Frank Schlesinger is CIO DACH Platforms and a member of the management team at Nexi Germany. In his role, he is responsible for IT and infrastructure in Germany and the entire DACH region. In previous positions, he was Head of Technology at ImmobilienScout24 and, prior to that, Head of Agile and Project Management, where he was responsible for cloud migration and a major organisational restructuring. After graduating with a degree in computer science, Frank began his career as a Java software developer, helping German banks and savings banks equip their teams with modern technology for banking processes.

speakers.name: Aurélie Vache
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Aurélie Vache is a Developer Advocate at OVHcloud in Toulouse, France. She is Docker Captain, CNCF ambassador, Google Cloud Developer Expert, Women techmarkers Ambassador & GitPod Hero. She has been working as a Developer and Ops for over 18 years. Cloud enthusiast and advocates DevOps/Cloud/Golang best practices. Conferences and meetups organizer since 2016. Technical writer (dev.to/aurelievache), a book author & reviewer, a sketchnoter and a speaker at international conferences. Mentor and promote diversity and accessibility in technology. She created a new visual way for people to learn and understand Cloud technologies: "Understanding Kubernetes/Istio/Docker in a visual way" in sketchnotes, books and videos. Blog: https://dev.to/aurelievache/ YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/c/AurelieVache

speakers.name: Ahmed Tikiwa
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Ahmed Tikiwa is a Senior Software Engineer at Disney and a Google Developer Expert for Android with over 12 years of software engineering experience and possesses a strong passion for developing world-class applications that can help make a difference. He has experience in frontend and backend systems, where he has built enterprise solutions for major telecommunication companies. He is currently a Senior Software Engineer based in Utrecht, The Netherlands, and an official contributor to Google Developers Dev Library.

speakers.name: Glenn F. Henriksen
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Glenn F. Henriksen is a mentor and developer from Norway. As the co-founder and CTO of Justify, he gets to build new legal tools for everyone to use, helping to create better communication and less conflict in relationships. He's continuously exploring new tools, processes and technologies, and improving how he and his fellow developers work with code, tasks and projects. He has been a Microsoft Development MVP, a part of the Microsoft Regional Director program and is an ASP.NET Insider and an Azure Advisor. In the past 25+ years he has co-owned two companies, worked as a consultant, manager, support tech, network admin, developer, architect, technical lead and more, but his favourite things are still swapping code for food and building stuff that makes a difference in people’s lives.

speakers.name: Awi Lifshitz
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Awi Lifshitz is a passionate and visionary leader in the fields of innovation and digital transformation with more than 20 years of professional experience. His core competencies include innovation consulting, digitalization, and the development of new business strategies. As CEO of weXelerate, he leads Austria’s most significant innovation ecosystem, which provides space for innovators, fosters collaboration, and empowers individuals and organizations to unlock their full innovation potential. Driven by the mission to holistically support companies in innovation and transformation, Awi Lifshitz brings diverse perspectives and experiences gained across various industries, regions, and roles.

speakers.name: André Alves
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: André is a software engineer on the quantum firmware team at eleQtron, Germany’s first commercial quantum computer manufacturer. He previously worked as a software engineer and team lead at freiheit.com technologies and on the quantum firmware team at IBM Quantum. He is a Qiskit Advocate and a former Quantum Ambassador at the Unitary Foundation.

speakers.name: Michael O'Toole
speakers.title: Data Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: Michael O'Toole is a Staff Engineer at Trade Republic. Over the past decade he's been building, breaking, and migrating data platforms across Europe and across the pong. When he's not obsessing over query performance, he plays in TwoFoxOut. He brings the same energy to both — loud, fast, and no unnecessary downtime.

speakers.name: Ohans Emmanuel
speakers.title: Former Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: Staff software engineer and technical author with 5+ published books. Since 2017, he’s written extensively about software and product development, with his work reaching 5M+ readers. He specialises in turning complex problems into simple, reliable systems and building delightful user interfaces that hold up in the real world.

speakers.name: Sandeep Pal
speakers.title: Principal Member of Technical Staff
speakers.bio: Sandeep is an engineering leader with 11+ years of experience in distributed systems, storage engines, infrastructure security and cloud-native platforms. Currently Principal Engineer at Salesforce, driving multi-cloud standards and strategy across AWS, GCP, and Alibaba Cloud. Previously, I worked on planet-scale HBase deployments at Salesforce and delivered Spark optimizations at Snapchat that saved tens of millions in infrastructure costs. I am the founding member of MultiCloudJ, Salesforce’s open-source Java SDK for multi-cloud development, and a contributor to Apache HBase, Apache Phoenix, Apache Spark, and GoCloud.dev. Passionate about building resilient, cloud-agnostic, and data-driven systems that create real-world impact.

speakers.name: Ondřej Gróf
speakers.title: Senior QA Engineer
speakers.bio: Ondřej is a Senior QA Engineer at Canva who has spent 9 years in testing, always with an eye toward what's next. He has a knack for finding innovative approaches that others might consider 'out of the box' for QA, specializing in visual testing, quality measurement, and AI applications in quality.

speakers.name: Phil Leggetter
speakers.title: Head of Developer Experience
speakers.bio: Phil Leggetter is an experienced Developer Experience, Developer Relations, and DevTools PLG leader with a background guiding startups through growth, acquisition, and scale within a publicly traded enterprise. He has spent his career building software experiences that maximize developer productivity at companies such as Pusher (acquired by MessageBird), Nexmo (acquired by Vonage), and, most recently, Hookdeck, where he helps build platforms and tools that empower developers to create interoperable and dependable event-driven systems.

speakers.name: Radostina (Ina) Tsvetkova
speakers.title: Senior Advisor
speakers.bio: Radostina (Ina) Tsvetkova is a Senior Advisor in Digital Accessibility and Inclusive Design at the Norwegian Directorate of Labour and Welfare (NAV). She is an Invited Expert in the W3C Accessibility Guidelines Working Group, a PhD candidate in Computer Science, a Certified Professional in Web Accessibility (CPWA), a DHS Trusted Tester, and an ISTQB Advanced Level Test Manager and Test Analyst. Ina provides guidance on digital accessibility throughout the product development lifecycle, from early planning and design through development, testing, and procurement. She has participated in the development of two Norwegian standards on accessibility in procurement and accessibility in the workplace, as well as the European standard “Accessible Systems for Living Independently.” Ina is a passionate advocate for an accessible and inclusive world for all, regardless of ability. She has presented at conferences including Agile Testing Days 2025, a11yCamp 2025, a11yTO 2025, M-Enabling 2025, BdKCSE 2025, Social Media Days 2025, Web4All 2025, EuroSTAR 2024, Odin 2023, and Mangfold i Mai (Diversity in May) 2023 and 2025.

speakers.name: Ali Alp
speakers.title: Distinguished Software Architect
speakers.bio: I am a seasoned Distinguished Software Engineer and Software Architect with over 15 years of experience in IT solutions, spanning both on-premise infrastructures and advanced cloud environments. My passion lies in containerization, virtualization, Kubernetes, and software architecture, and I have been deeply involved in driving cloud transformation projects. As a strong advocate for Linux, I bring extensive knowledge of IT systems, specializing in deploying containerization tools and Kubernetes to improve operational efficiency and scalability. My work focuses on designing resilient, cutting-edge systems that stay ahead of the technological curve.

speakers.name: Sasha Denisov
speakers.title: Chief Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Sasha is CTO at Brainform.ai with over 20 years of experience architecting scalable enterprise systems. With a strong engineering background, his expertise spans frontend, backend, cloud infrastructure, mobile development, and AI — from cloud-based generative AI to on-device solutions. He specializes in building robust, production-ready products using a variety of technologies and frameworks. Sasha has delivered solutions across fintech, digital media, and entertainment. He is a Google Developer Expert for Cloud, AI, Firebase, Flutter, and Dart, co-organizes the Flutter Berlin Community, and is a recognized international speaker and writer, having presented at 30+ conferences worldwide.

speakers.name: Michael Coté
speakers.title: Senior Member of Technical Staff
speakers.bio: Michael Coté studies how large organizations improve how they build software to run and grow their business. His books Changing Mindsets, Monolithic Transformation, and The Business Bottleneck cover these topics. He’s been an industry analyst at RedMonk and 451 Research, done corporate strategy and M&A, and was a programmer. He also co-hosts several podcasts, including Software Defined Talk. His daily-ish newsletter is at newsletter.cote.io.

speakers.name: Sonja Nesic
speakers.title: Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: Entered the world of testing in 2014 and loving it ever since! Upon entering the world of software development, I learned quickly that in order to bring the right product to customers, having people with the best tech knowledge with you is simply not enough – there is so much more to it. This is why besides learning new tools and techniques I always take the time to work on my communication and organizational skills as well as on my understanding of human interactions. Supporting multiple teams working in an e-commerce, I had the opportunity to take my coaching skills to a serious test and prove that being a tester or later on a staff engineer doesn’t have to be a lonely position in the organization. Always interested in sharing my experience and knowledge as well as learning about yours! System quality, chaos engineering, observability and testing in isolation are just a few of the topics close to my heart. I was fortunate enough to be able to take the stage at Agile Testing Days, HUSTEF and AutomationSTAR and become a program committee member of HUSTEF this year!

speakers.name: Stefan Mesquita
speakers.title: Platform Experience Lead
speakers.bio: Stefan Mesquita leads the Platform Experience team for Corporate Bank platforms at Deutsche Bank. He is responsible for scaling the bank’s core API, Kafka, and BigQuery infrastructure to drive organizational efficiency. With over 15 years of experience in the financial sector, Stefan specializes in Platform Engineering and defining the architectural patterns that allow product teams to deliver faster. By decoupling infrastructure complexity from product development, he enables the organization to focus on business value and innovation. Stefan is a strategic leader dedicated to bridging the gap between large-scale systems engineering and digital business strategy.

speakers.name: Viktoria Semaan
speakers.title: Principal AI Evangelist
speakers.bio: Viktoria Semaan is a Principal AI Evangelist at Databricks, specializing in AI agents, data engineering, and enterprise AI implementation. She has spent years helping organizations build production-ready AI systems, previously as a Senior Developer Advocate at AWS. With 15+ years of experience spanning technology, engineering, and management consulting, Viktoria has led Build and Go-to-Market efforts with top software companies. She holds an MBA from UCLA Anderson and an entrepreneurship program at HEC Paris. Named an AI/ML Top Voice on LinkedIn, Viktoria is deeply passionate about coaching, talent development, and mentoring. She regularly shares educational content with her audience of over 630,000 tech professionals, fostering a collaborative and inclusive community across her platforms.

speakers.name: Aeneas Rekkas
speakers.title: Founder and CTO
speakers.bio: Aeneas Rekkas is the Founder and CTO of Ory Corp, the modern customer identity and access management platform trusted by developers worldwide. In 2010, he co-founded Serlo Education e.V., the German non-profit behind the open education platform serlo.org, which serves over one million active users per month and is the largest e-learning platform in German-speaking countries. Aeneas has contributed to open source since 2004 and has authored numerous widely adopted projects.

speakers.name: Mats Kuuskemaa
speakers.title: Country Director
speakers.bio: Mats Kuuskemaa started his career as a diplomat for Estonia, working with trade and investment policy at the Estonian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (2011-14). He was Consul & Economic Officer at Estonian Embassy in New Delhi (2014-18) and Political Counsellor at Estonian Embassy in Beijing (2018-19). In 2018 Mats joined Government of Estonia’s e-Residency programme, initially as Head of Business Strategy and from 2022 as Country Director for Germany, Poland, Austria and Switzerland based in Berlin. On two occasions he has also been part of startup teams as Co-Founder and Advisor. As a hobby, he has occasionally taken up journalism.

speakers.name: Edith Harbaugh
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Edith Harbaugh is the CEO and Co-founder of LaunchDarkly, the comprehensive feature management platform that equips software teams to proactively reduce the risk of shipping bad software and AI applications while accelerating their release velocity. LaunchDarkly serves over 5,500 of the most innovative enterprises, including a quarter of the Fortune 500. Edith holds two patents in deployment and earned a B.S. in Engineering from Harvey Mudd College.

speakers.name: Deivids Vilkinsons
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Dei is the CEO of HASH, the open-source platform for data integration and simulation. HASH develops ultra high-accuracy AI solutions for firms in safety-critical, error-sensitive, and regulated industries. HASH has been developing "agentic" simulation tooling since 2019, when Dei established the firm with Joel Spolsky (the former founder of Stack Overflow, Trello, and Glitch). Today, HASH is profitable and has offices in New York, London, and Berlin. Dei also leads one of the technical areas of the UK Advanced Research + Invention Agency's "Safeguarded AI" program, focused on developing high-trust interfaces for effective human-AI collaboration.

speakers.name: Eric Bowman
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Eric Bowman, Chief Technology Officer at King, brings over 30 years of visionary leadership and technical expertise to his role, guiding the company’s technological future with clarity and creativity. Known for transforming complex technical ideas into actionable strategies, Eric shapes King's long-term vision and spearheads innovative solutions that redefine gaming experiences. At King, Eric leads a dynamic, multidisciplinary tech team looking after the foundational technologies underpinning all King games, including the cutting-edge AI and Machine Learning group. He fosters a culture of collaboration, innovation, and continuous improvement. His leadership extends throughout the organization, empowering teams and individuals to leverage technology effectively and maximize their creative potential. Passionate about nurturing talent, Eric champions leadership development, cultivating a culture of inclusion and growth. Eric’s work positions King at the forefront of a rapidly evolving technological landscape, driving innovations that captivate new players, delight dedicated fans, and advance King’s core mission: Making the World Playful. Before King, Eric was CTO at TomTom, overseeing global technology initiatives. He also made significant contributions as Zalando’s first Vice President of Engineering, and as the technical programming lead for Electronic Arts' iconic game, The Sims 1.0. Eric’s extensive track record underscores his unique ability to envision and execute transformative technology strategies, cementing his reputation as a thought leader and influential figure in the industry.

speakers.name: Francesco Ciulla
speakers.title: Computer Scientist and Content Creator
speakers.bio: Computer Scientist (Rome, Italy) who turned into a Content Creator in 2020. Recognized as a Docker Captain since 2021 and an International Public Speaker since 2022. He currently focuses on producing in-depth YouTube videos and content, specializing in the Rust programming language.

speakers.name: Jason Bloomfield
speakers.title: Global Head of TA Transformation at Ericsson
speakers.bio: Jason has a 20+ year track record of strategic impact & meaningful innovation across geographies and industries alike. A hands-on leader who inspires those around him towards a shared sense of purpose, vision and outcome. Jason joined Ericsson in 2022 as its Global Head of People Change & Experience Design focused on transforming the employee experience for its workforce of 100,000 around the globe. His experience includes digital transformation, driving new customer centric journeys, multi-billion-dollar merger & acquisition integrations, accelerating sustainability and spearheading new ways of working. More recently, he has also taken on a secondment role as Global Head of Talent Acquisition Transformation to improve the experience of candidates, recruiters and hiring managers alike through AI enabled journeys. Jason’s expertise has garnered industry recognition; expertise he’s shared with not-for-profits as a member of their Board of Directors.

speakers.name: Prince Kohli
speakers.title: President and CEO
speakers.bio: Prince Kohli is a distinguished technology executive and visionary leader with three decades of experience in building, scaling, and transforming solutions across critical sectors, including enterprise software, cloud computing, telecoms, and security. As the current Chief Executive Officer of Sauce Labs, he spearheads an AI-driven end- to-end continuous quality platform trusted by a distinguished list of large enterprise clients. For the past decade, Mr. Kohli's focus has been at the forefront of Artificial Intelligence platforms, including the development of one of the industry's first agentic platforms. Throughout his career, he has cultivated a strong reputation for operational excellence and for building high-performing, globally distributed teams within companies of all sizes, from startups to large enterprises. His entrepreneurial background includes co-founding a successful, VC-backed company. Committed to responsible innovation, he is also a valued member of the Ethical AI Governance Group, collaborating with practitioners who share similar values. Mr. Kohli holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science from the Georgia Institute of Technology and a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science and Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), New Delhi, India.

speakers.name: Thomas Eriksson
speakers.title: CEO and Founder
speakers.bio: Thomas Eriksson, Founder and CEO of SASHA- Safe Share, is a Danish technology leader at the forefront of watermarking technology. Driven by the mission to end online image abuse, Thomas developed SASHA’s patented "in-pixel" signature technology that embeds ownership and consent directly into digital content. As a serial entrepreneur with a background in edtech and medtech, Thomas has scaled SASHA from a vision to a robust product presented at the EU Presidency Meeting for Digital Affairs and awarded the 2025 World Summit Award. SASHA tech provides platforms with the tools to streamline moderation and neutralize image abuse at the source.

speakers.name: Nils Berger
speakers.title: Founder and CEO
speakers.bio: Nils Berger is the Founder and CEO of Viewpointsystem, a deep-tech company developing and producing internationally awarded smart glasses with integrated eye tracking. With its “Digital Iris” integration platform, Viewpointsystem enables seamless eye tracking in third-party wearables. By capturing gaze in real time, eye tracking enables more intuitive interaction and a deeper connection between users and digital environments. Nils is an experienced founder and serial entrepreneur with a proven track record in information services, technology consultancy, and the automation industry.

speakers.name: Klaus-Michael Schremser
speakers.title: Co-founder and Head of Growth
speakers.bio: Klaus-M. Schremser is the Co-Founder and Head of Growth at OtterlyAI, a leading AI search optimisation platform. A serial entrepreneur and MarTech enthusiast, he co-founded four software companies - three acquired by Atlassian, APA and saas.group. With 25+ years in SaaS, growth, and product development, Klaus-M. combines startup grit with an Ironman mindset to build the next wave of AI-driven marketing solutions.

speakers.name: Chris Daden
speakers.title: CTO and Head of AI
speakers.bio: Chris Daden is the Chief Technology Officer at Criteria, a global Talent Success platform operating at the center of the Future of Work. Criteria helps organizations measure real talent signals through more than 80 million assessments delivered worldwide in over 25 languages, enabling fairer, more predictive hiring and development decisions. Backed by over $65 million in private growth equity investment, Criteria works with 4,000+ leading global organizations to move beyond static credentials toward dynamic, skills-based insights that reflect true capability and potential. Chris is a multi-time founder and technology executive with deep expertise in artificial intelligence, including applied AI systems, model fine-tuning, and frontier model development. His work focuses on how AI is reshaping work, education, and human capital strategy at scale. He regularly advises leaders on the intersection of technology, workforce transformation, and organizational design, and frequently speaks globally as a keynote presenter, podcast guest, and industry contributor. He is a contributor to the Forbes Technology Council and Forbes Young Executives Council, and serves as the founding technology advisor for The Hungry Lab, a global initiative working alongside the United Nations to advise and support startups worldwide. Chris also leads SoCal Tech Forum, one of Southern California’s largest technology non-profits, dedicated to educating companies and communities on emerging and disruptive technologies. Chris brings a data-driven perspective on Work 4.0, the future of education, and how organizations can design talent systems that prioritize adaptability, equity, and long-term human potential in an AI-driven world.

speakers.name: Marcin Olichwirowicz
speakers.title: Engineering Manager
speakers.bio: Marcin is an Engineering Manager with over a decade of experience spanning small startups to big enterprises. Having served in multiple roles - from Senior Engineer to Tech Lead and Manager - he treats his career as a laboratory for leadership and philosophy. Most recently, Marcin navigated a unique "Pendulum" career arc: transitioning from management roles in large organizations (like William Hill and NetEnt) to a Staff Engineer role at Qonto, before returning to leadership in Big Tech. He uses this cross-functional experience to help teams bridge the gap between backend infrastructure and organizational strategy, advocating for "Radical Career Ownership" along the way.

speakers.name: Michele Zuccala
speakers.title: Senior Vice President of Engineering
speakers.bio: Michele Zuccala is Senior Vice President of Engineering at Sysdig, leading the global engineering organization across open source R&D, runtime security, monitoring, AI, and core platform areas. He drives technical execution and innovation across both commercial and open source initiatives. Michele joined Sysdig to lead the open source team and helped scale Falco, guiding the CNCF runtime security project to graduation and broad industry adoption. He later unified open source and commercial development for runtime security before expanding his leadership across the wider engineering organization. Before Sysdig, he was CTO at ProntoPro and held leadership roles at several high-growth startups.

speakers.name: Benjamin Dumke-von der Ehe
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Ben is an AI-skeptic software developer in Berlin who believes in a healthy balance between over-engineered creativity and creative over-engineering. He was an early hire at Stack Overflow and spent over 12 years there, in addition to working at other startups like Alloy, Wonder, and most recently Stacks Labs.

speakers.name: Max Tschochohei
speakers.title: Director Customer Engineering
speakers.bio: Max works with Google Cloud customers to realize their AI ambitions. As the Director for Customer Engineering for AI in EMEA, he leads Google's teams of expert AI engineers in the region in deploying the Gemini model family, Gemini Enterprise and Vertex AI, as well as Google Cloud's AI infrastructure offerings. Besides his role at Google, Max is also the co-lead for the AI in Women's Health research group at TUM University Hospital in Munich, where he and his team work on improving treatment outcomes for patients with rare gynecological tumors through AI for clinical decision support systems and patient digital twins.

speakers.name: Ana Mitrasevic
speakers.title: CPSO
speakers.bio: Ana Mitrasevic joined Delivery Hero in November 2021 and was named Chief People and Sustainability Officer in May 2023. She is responsible for driving Delivery Hero’s global strategy to find and retain the world’s best tech talent, pioneer an inclusive environment, and positively impact the communities that we serve. Before joining, Ana spent 8 years at Expedia Group where she was focused on the global tech talent strategy and growth across 21 brands, 200 travel sites and 60+ locations, and led the people function at Vrbo, Expedia’s vacation rentals entity. During her time at Expedia, Ana spent 2.5 years in the Product Management team, focusing on global Product Operations and excellence. Prior to joining Expedia, she was a CPO at Thomas Cook Digital and OdigeO, European travel operators and marketplaces. Ana holds a bachelor's degree in Business Management and IT from University of Hull, Post Graduate degree in Human Resources from University of Kingston, UK and has completed Harvard Business School Executive Education.

speakers.name: Wolfgang Oels
speakers.title: COO
speakers.bio: Dr. Wolfgang Oels studied engineering and business and started his career at McKinsey & Company before taking senior leadership positions in the solar industry in Germany and the US. Since 2016, he has served as COO of Ecosia, the largest European search engine, and as Director of European Search Perspective, building a sovereign European web index.

speakers.name: Grisha Pavlotskyi
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Grisha Pavlotskyi is the Chief Transformation Officer of Miro, the world’s leading visual workspace for innovation. Grisha spends most of his time with customers, helping them think through AI transformation topics - be it the technology side of it, ways of working, or how to re-engineer processes from an AI-first principles basis. Together with Miro's CIO, Grisha also drives Miro's own internal AI transformation. Prior to Miro, Grisha served as a Managing Partner at Deloitte for 19 years, focusing on helping organisations find ways to accelerate innovation.

speakers.name: Mirko Novakovic
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Mirko Novakovic is the CEO and co-founder of Dash0, an observability platform built on OpenTelemetry and AI. Previously, he co-founded Instana, a leading application performance monitoring company acquired by IBM, and codecentric. As an active angel investor, he has backed more than 70 B2B SaaS startups. Mirko is passionate about building developer-first products and shaping the future of observability in the age of AI

speakers.name: Misha Bragin
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Misha Bragin is a Berlin-based tech entrepreneur, software engineer, and open source contributor who is the co-founder and CEO of NetBird, an open source cybersecurity company focused on Zero Trust networking.

speakers.name: Mark Pollok
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: As CEO of Trenkwalder Group AG, Mark had the opportunity to lead the development of an innovative solution for automating recruitment. His focus was on leveraging technology to optimize and enhance the recruitment process, ensure to remain industry leaders and offer the best possible service to their clients. In addition, Mark and his team successfully built an outsourced customer service solution in which transactions are handled by AI agents. With revenues of €550 million and 16,000 employees across 15 countries, they are proud to deliver first-class services to their clients worldwide.

speakers.name: Jakobus Schuster
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Jakobus Schuster is the founder and CEO of Notarity, the leading platform for digital notary appointments in Europe. He studied law at Bucerius Law School and worked in international law firms during his studies. Already as a student, he developed a strong interest in legal tech, taught himself fundamental coding skills, and implemented his first legal data science projects. After completing his legal clerkship in Hamburg, he began building Notarity, which is now used worldwide to handle notarial processes digitally, efficiently, and independently of location.

speakers.name: Alexandre Guenoun
speakers.title: CEO and Founder
speakers.bio: Alexandre is the founder and CEO of Kiro. He defines Kiro’s vision and strategic direction and serves as the guardian of the company’s culture. He began his career in private equity at L Catterton (LVMH) and in management consulting at Oliver Wyman. Alexandre graduated from ESSEC in Paris (Management) and from UC Berkeley in California (Innovation and Law), and specialized in innovation at the Luigi Bocconi University in Milan. He was recently named among Forbes’ 30 Under 30 and Choiseul’s 100. Passionate about the contribution of new technologies to improving healthcare at scale, Alexandre decided to create a leading company to help every patient benefit from technology and access better care.

speakers.name: Daniele Tonella
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Daniele Tonella is the chief technology officer at ING. A lifelong technologist, he describes himself as “a mechanical engineer by mistake” with an enduring passion for tech. He started coding when he was 10 years old, and at 18, “with the self‑confidence of that age”, he believed he already understood technology well enough to study something different, so he pursued mechanical engineering. Despite that, he never stopped tinkering and coding on the side, eventually moving fully into the technology world. Daniele has over 20 years of experience in senior technology and leadership roles in the financial industry and currently serves in advisory and board positions for several companies. Before joining ING, he worked at UniCredit, including as Group Chief Information Officer and CEO of UniCredit Services. Prior to that he held roles at AXA Group, Evalueserve, and Swiss Life. He began his career as a consultant at Mercer and later McKinsey & Company in Switzerland. He holds a master’s degree in industrial engineering from ETH Zürich and completed executive education programmes at Harvard, IMD, INSEAD, and MIT Sloan.

speakers.name: Oliver Krizek
speakers.title: CEO and Founder
speakers.bio: Oliver Krizek is the Founder and CEO of the NAVAX Group. Mid-sized and large enterprises rely on the company’s expertise in digital business processes and automation. With his vision and strong focus on innovation, Krizek has grown NAVAX from its entrepreneurial beginnings in the 1990s into an international group with more than 330 employees. He combines technical know-how and entrepreneurial experience with clear, decisive leadership. Off the business stage, the father of three is an enthusiastic skier, golfer, and triathlete. His energy and positive attitude towards life shape not only his athletic ambitions but also the future of NAVAX.

speakers.name: Peter Bakenecker
speakers.title: President Central Europe
speakers.bio: Peter leads Mastercard’s Central Europe Division, driving strategy and tech-driven innovations across 12 markets in Europe, including Germany, Poland, and Switzerland. With nearly two decades at Mastercard, he has shaped digital payments, business development, and innovative solutions like wallets and tokenization in Europe, AP, and MEA. He held prior roles in Mastercard Advisors, Roland Berger and Batten & Company. Peter holds an MBA in Banking & Finance, studying in Australia, Germany and the U.K.

speakers.name: Peter Kürpick
speakers.title: Senior VP Germany and Austria and CTO
speakers.bio: Mr. Kürpick holds a Ph.D. in Theoretical Physics from the University of Kassel and has conducted post-doctorate research at Kansas State University and the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. He also holds a Masters in Environmental Sciences from the University of Kassel as well as a Corporate MBA from INSEAD. Mr. Kürpick is Senior Vice President at EPAM Systems in Berlin. In a dual role he is both responsible for the German entity of EPAM and global CTO Enterprise Software. He joined EPAM Systems from BainCapital where he oversaw the adoption of latest IT strategies in the BainCapital portfolio companies in the role of a Technology Operating Partner. He also is a Non-Executive Director of PTV and DualFluid. Previously, he has held numerous technology leadership roles at HERE Technologies, Unify, Software AG and SAP, where he was instrumental in the conception and development of SAP NetWeaver.

speakers.name: Marcus Ross
speakers.title: Platform Engineering and Process Framework
speakers.bio: Marcus Ross is a Member of the Platform Engineering & Process Framework team. He's developing the next step in scalable telematics and healthcare digitalization by building platforms with Kubernetes, GitOps, and platform tooling that put the Golden Path ahead of cognitive load. He's also keen to turn AI into real value in his everyday work.

speakers.name: Michael Ameling
speakers.title: President SAP Business Technology Platform, Member of the Extended Board
speakers.bio: Michael is the President of SAP Business Technology Platform and Member of the Extended Board. His team prioritizes security, reliability, and next-generation user experience for SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). As SAP’s leading platform, SAP BTP offers the technical foundation essential for driving customer success and enabling Generative AI-powered applications. Additionally, Michael’s team is responsible for the success of core products such as SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, SAP HANA Cloud and SAP BTP ABAP environment. He joined SAP in 2006 as a Research Associate and has since advanced through various technical leadership roles, including Development Manager and Senior Vice President and Managing Director of SAP Labs Dresden. He has a strong passion in for software architecture and fosters close collaboration across all boundaries. Michael holds a Ph.D. in computer science and a diploma in media computer science from Technische Universität Dresden. A father of four, Michael lives with his family in Dresden.

speakers.name: Florian Deter
speakers.title: General Manager EMEA Strategic Partnerships
speakers.bio: Florian Deter currently serves as General Manager EMEA Strategic Partner Sales, He has been responsible before for Microsoft's enterprise business in Germany as Managing Director of Microsoft Germany and Enterprise Commercial Lead since August 1, 2022. Florian began his career in consulting and management roles at Oliver Wyman, Accenture, and PwC. He later served as Executive Vice President overseeing the global portfolio of Nordcloud, an IBM company. Prior to that, he led CIO Advisory and the Cloud Applications Service Line at IBM.

speakers.name: Mei Dent
speakers.title: Chief Product and Technology Officer
speakers.bio: Mei Dent has an excellent track record as a technology executive in the software and IT industry. She started her career as a software developer at IBM and spent nearly 25 years in various product and engineering related leadership roles at Canadian software giant OpenText. As part of the senior leadership team of the company’s software development organization, she was responsible for global engineering practices and processes for security, performance, user experience, as well as reusable and shared services. In her most recent role as CTO at customer communications management and engagement software provider Doxim, Mei led the entire technology organization and scaled it to support the company’s growth. Mei holds a Master in Computer Science from the Western University in Ontario.

speakers.name: Clemens Wasner
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Clemens Wasner is CEO of enliteAI, an Austrian venture studio that achieved its first AI exit just 18 months after investment. He is also founder and chairperson of AI Austria, an independent think tank driving Austria’s competitiveness in artificial intelligence. Since 2017 he has published the Austrian AI Landscape, the reference overview of the country’s AI ecosystem. Clemens hosts the weekly “AI Talk” podcast and is an active member of the European AI Forum, a coalition of national AI associations that advises on and shapes EU regulation.

speakers.name: Jörg Tewes
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Joerg Tewes joined Exasol as CEO at the beginning of 2023. He spent the first 12 years of his career at data management software company Poet Holdings supporting the company through its early growth stages to its IPO at the German stock exchange. He relocated to the San Francisco Bay Area in 2007 and continued to pursue his career in general management roles at Avid Technology Inc. and Logitech International. In 2014, he joined video and augmented reality start-up Avegant as CEO, where he raised $50M in venture capital funding and strategically expanded the company’s product offerings. From 2018 – 2022, Joerg Tewes held senior leadership roles at Amazon, driving the development and commercialization of its tablet, e-reader and video communication technologies. Joerg Tewes is an active angel investor and mentor to technology start-ups. He holds a degree in computer science from the Technical University in Berlin, Germany.

speakers.name: Mardu Swanepoel
speakers.title: Head of AI Engineering
speakers.bio: Experienced software engineer focused on AI and product development. Currently leading the development of AI solutions for global medical device manufacturers at Flinn.
