Prior to this, I worked with Apple Music's data engineering team, built a decision support SaaS used by large software organisations such as Apple and Move Inc, and led teams building solutions to monitor supply chain risks across millions of global data points at Prewave. I'm passionate about the intersection of software engineering, data, and product, and at my best when leading teams that build data-centred digital products.

speakers.name: Bernd Datler
speakers.title: Managing Director
speakers.bio: Bernd Datler is managing director of the tolling company of ASFINAG since November 2009 and CIO/CDO of the ASFINAG group. Mr. Datler is responsible for technical development and operation of the tolling systems of ASFINAG covering all highways and expressways of Austria as well as for various ITS activities within ASFINAG. In addition to that, Mr. Datler is responsible for all IT- and digitalisation related activities of ASFINAG.

speakers.name: Daniel Terzenbach
speakers.title: Member of the executive board
speakers.bio: Daniel Terzenbach has been a Member of the Executive Board of the Federal Employment Agency since 2019, where he is responsible for operational business areas. From October 2023 to July 2024, he also served as the Federal Government’s Special Representative for the labor market integration of refugees. At the beginning of his career with the Federal Employment Agency, he held a leadership position at the Jobcenter Märkischer Kreis. In 2009, he moved to the agency’s headquarters in Nuremberg, where he took on various leadership roles and, from 2015 to 2018, was responsible for the labor market integration of refugees.

speakers.name: Manuel Romero
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: Manuel Romero is Co-founder and Chief Science Officer at Maisa AI, a Gartner-recognized leader in enterprise AI. Before building Maisa, he established himself as one of the most prolific contributors in the Hugging Face open-source ecosystem, ranking as the 5th most-downloaded individual contributor worldwide. His work spans multilingual NLP, large language models, and evaluation frameworks that help teams ship reliable AI at scale. At the intersection of research and production, Manu has spent his career turning frontier AI into systems that meet the governance, compliance, and auditability demands of regulated industries. Maisa's Digital Workers now operate in mission-critical environments for global financial institutions, with over 120 use cases running in production. He is a recognized voice in the European AI community on topics including enterprise-grade AI deployment, multilingual model development, and building AI systems that institutions can trust.

speakers.name: Jyoti Bansal
speakers.title: Founder and CEO
speakers.bio: Jyoti Bansal is a multi-unicorn founder and serial technology entrepreneur. He is the founder and CEO of Harness, the $5.5B AI Software Delivery Platform automating everything after code — testing, deployment, security, and compliance — for the largest enterprises in the world. Previously, he founded AppDynamics (acquired by Cisco for $3.7B), launched startup studio BIG Labs, and co-founded VC firm Unusual Ventures. He has been recognized as EY Entrepreneur of the Year, Forbes' Best Cloud Computing CEO, SiliconANGLE's Most Innovative Tech Startup Leader, and ranked No. 24 on Fortune's Most Innovative Companies list. He holds a BS in Computer Science from IIT Delhi and over 25 U.S. patents.

speakers.name: Steren Giannini
speakers.title: Director and Founder
speakers.bio: Steren is a founding member of Google Cloud Run, and is today the product leader for Google Cloud's serverless portfolio (Cloud Run, Cloud Run functions, App Engine, Tasks, Scheduler, Eventarc, Workflows). Steren also notably founded Google Cloud Carbon Footprint. Steren is an engineer turned product manager, he graduated from École Centrale (France). Before joining Google, he was CTO of a startup building connected objects and multi device solutions.

speakers.name: Siddhika Nevrekar
speakers.title: Senior Director Product Management
speakers.bio: Siddhika Nevrekar is senior director of product management at Qualcomm Technologies, where she is responsible for product and AI software development, including Qualcomm AI Hub. Siddhika Nevrekar is a visionary product leader with expertise in AI and machine learning. Siddhika co-founded Tetra AI, which was later acquired by Qualcomm. Prior to Tetra AI, she worked to steer CoreML at Apple and spearheaded the development of a Classification Machine Learning Platform at Microsoft. Her work has revolutionized key technologies across major platforms such as Bing, Siri, and now the Qualcomm AI Hub. Siddhika received an MBA in International Business from the Institute of Technology and Management, India and a Bachelor of Computer Engineering from Vivekanand Education Society’s Institute of Technology.

speakers.name: Guillaume Esnou
speakers.title: Field CTO Europe
speakers.bio: Guillaume Esnou is Field CTO and Head of Technology for Invisible Technologies in EMEA, where he leads AI deployment strategy and technical delivery for enterprise clients. He brings deep expertise in AI and analytics, having built and scaled production-grade AI systems across sectors at McKinsey as an Associate Partner. Most recently, he led Engineering, Insights & Analytics at Surveyor Capital, Citadel hedge fund’s leading fundamental equities strategy. Guillaume holds an M.S. in Operations Research from Columbia University and a Diplôme d’Ingénieur in Mechanical and Industrial Engineering from Arts et Métiers. At Invisible, he focuses on building scalable AI systems that transform operations and drive measurable business impact.

speakers.name: Zohar Fox
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Zohar Fox is the Co-Founder and CEO of Aurora Labs, the company behind LOCI - bringing execution reasoning into modern software development. Awarded the 2021 Artificial Intelligence Excellence Award, Zohar is now leading the transformation of how software is developed, deployed, and maintained, across both automotive and AI factory environments. LOCI by Aurora Labs makes large-scale rollouts more predictable while improving runtime performance and reducing energy consumption in production code. Zohar's vision bridges embedded systems and large-scale inference workloads, delivering observability and optimization directly at the binary and hardware levels, before systems even reach the test bench. Prior to founding Aurora Labs, Zohar spent two decades in embedded hardware/software design, orchestration, and deployment for TELCOs and technology service providers, helping deliver over 10 million connected devices across automotive, banking, and IoT industries. Mr. Fox have also led strategic sales and technology partnerships with Deutsche Telekom, France Telecom, DSP Group, and BOSCH.

speakers.name: Adam Bird
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Adam Bird is a serial technology founder and ex-CTO with a deep product focus. At Cronofy, he's building temporal infrastructure: an enterprise-proven grid that unifies availability control, coordinates the full scheduling lifecycle, and unlocks meeting intelligence for agents, systems, and teams. He seeks to reimagine the plumbing behind business process to effect step changes in productivity. Much of his focus is now on building the frameworks that lets the Cronofy team do their best work. Other than that he's a keen cyclist, clumsy language learner and hates wasting time.

speakers.name: Alex Olivier
speakers.title: Co-founder and CPO
speakers.bio: Alex Olivier is the Co-founder & CPO at Cerbos, an open-source authorization platform, and co-chair of the OpenID AuthZEN working group, which standardizes fine-grained authorization. He specializes in authorization systems, with a recent focus on workload identity and AI security. With over a decade of experience building and scaling authorization systems at Microsoft, Qubit, Zencargo, and multiple startups, Alex has published extensively on securing Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and AI agents. A frequent speaker at events on authorization, AI, security, and identity, including KubeCon, Google Cloud NEXT, Identiverse, and EIC, he combines standards development with practical implementation experience, focusing on the future of authorization for traditional applications, distributed workloads, and emerging AI systems.

speakers.name: Alexander Opalic
speakers.title: Developer
speakers.bio: Alexander Opalic is a Senior Full Stack Developer from Germany. He writes at alexop.dev about Vue, TypeScript, and AI in software. He speaks at international conferences on local first apps, testing, and building better developer experiences.

speakers.name: Alfonso Graziano
speakers.title: AI Tech Lead
speakers.bio: Alfonso Graziano is a Tech Lead with a strong background in software engineering, specializing in JavaScript, and distributed systems. Currently at Nearform, Alfonso leads engineering teams to deliver impactful projects, for governmental institutions and enterprise. With a history of scaling teams and designing innovative solutions across industries like fintech and media platforms, Alfonso brings expertise in AWS, Node.js, and React. Passionate about knowledge sharing, he loves to solve problems and contributes to tech communities. In his free time, he enjoys cooking, learning, and spending quality time with loved ones.

speakers.name: Aliaksandr Valialkin
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Aliaksandr is a co-founder and the principal architect of VictoriaMetrics. He is also a well-known author of the popular performance-oriented libraries: fasthttp, fastcache and quicktemplate. Prior to VictoriaMetrics, Aliaksandr held CTO and Architect roles with adtech companies serving high volumes of traffic. He holds a Master’s Degree in Computer Software Engineering. He decided to found VictoriaMetrics after experiencing the shortcomings of all available time series databases and monitoring solutions.

speakers.name: Alison Cossette
speakers.title: CEO and Founder
speakers.bio: Alison Cossette is founder and CEO of ClariTrace, where she's building graph-based intelligence for AI systems. Her work spans AI governance, physical AI, and retrieval research—she recently won "Most Impressive Project" at Physical AI Hack 2026 for a VLA-powered robot that reads books to children. Alison serves on the US NIST Generative AI Public Working Group and has studied AI and Data Science at Northwestern University at the Master's level. She speaks globally on building AI systems that are capable, controllable, and worth trusting.

speakers.name: Anaëlle Sikich
speakers.title: ML / AI Engineer
speakers.bio: Anaëlle is an ML / AI Engineer at Microsoft, working on large-scale production systems where machine learning is part of critical, user-facing workflows. Her work focuses on designing reliable, scalable AI systems that operate under real-world constraints. With a background spanning software engineering and machine learning, Anaëlle is particularly interested in the intersection of AI, distributed systems, and platform engineering—and in helping teams move from experimental models to production systems they can confidently operate.

speakers.name: Anastasia Simou
speakers.title: Quality Engineering Specialist
speakers.bio: Anastasia holds a diploma in Mathematics and works as a Quality Engineering Specialist at Accenture, where she leads the Test Data Management practice. With a background in mathematics and a passion for problem solving, she specializes in designing strategies that combine effective testing with data safety, security, and compliance. With a strong focus on data protection and a particular interest in AI security, her work focuses on protecting sensitive data in test environments and integrating security into quality engineering practices. She is passionate about helping teams build software that is not only functional, but secure by design.

speakers.name: Aram Hakobyan
speakers.title: Head of Platform Engineering
speakers.bio: Platform Engineering Lead and Infrastructure Expert with over 25 years of experience designing and operating highly scalable, distributed systems. Currently serving as Head of Platform Engineering at zooplus SE, where I lead container orchestration and observability teams, running 40 k8s clusters and 3000 microservices, supporting one of Europe's largest online pet retailers.

speakers.name: Aris Tzoumas
speakers.title: Staff Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Aris is a Staff Software Engineer at RudderStack with over 20 years of experience building scalable, high-performance systems across telecommunications, banking, European institutions, and data infrastructure. A core contributor to the open-source rudder-server, he specializes in Go, PostgreSQL, Kubernetes, and cloud-native architectures. Aris is passionate about designing elegant solutions, driving engineering best practices, and sharing insights on scalable data pipelines, enterprise DevOps, and software architecture.

speakers.name: Artur Naumenko
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: I am a senior software engineer and consultant with 9+ years of experience working in banking, telecom, government, and enterprise domains. A systems thinker and pragmatic generalist. Backend-first, infrastructure-aware and privacy-minded. Currently exploring applied AI direction. Experienced mentor, speaker, and consultant, organizer of "summer academies" and different internal knowledge-sharing sessions with a strong focus on practical learning and software engineering reality.

speakers.name: Aydin Mir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Software Architect and CTO
speakers.bio: Aydin Mir Mohammadi, partner and architect at bluehands, has been creating software for more than twenty years. He specializes in the development of distributed complex business applications based on Microsoft technologies. His focus is on cloud migration, AI integration, software modernization, and twin transformation. Aydin is co-initiator of the .NET User Group Karlsruhe, organizer of the Green Software Development Karlsruhe, Azure MVP, initiator of the CO2-Challenge and host of the #nossued Open Space. He is also involved in the training of young software developers as a lecturer at the Karlsruhe Cooperative State University.

speakers.name: Bastian Heilemann
speakers.title: Lead Cloud Solution Architect
speakers.bio: Studied mathematics and computer science. Software developer roles across multiple industries. Moved on to the Software Architect role and leading role. Since 09/2022 Lead Cloud Native Solution Architect at Zeiss.

speakers.name: Björn Buchhold
speakers.title: Technology Evangelist
speakers.bio: Björn Buchhold is a Technology Evangelist at CID, specializing in AI and Data Science. With a PhD in Computer Science and over 15 years of experience in academia and industry, he helps organizations navigate the full journey from AI strategy and data architecture to hands-on ML implementation.

speakers.name: Björn Stahl
speakers.title: Software Architect
speakers.bio: Björn Stahl is a Software Architect at AVS which is a competence center at Bechtle. He is advising customers on architecture decisions, the tradeoffs and how to leverage the advantages of cloud concepts or gain certain degrees of digital souvereignty. Besides the day to day work he is interested in architectural concepts, high-traffic-low-latency applications and performance optimizations in the Java world. Besides the technical topics he likes to learn and discuss all topics around leadership and how to lead people.

speakers.name: Brian Scanlan
speakers.title: Senior Principal Engineer
speakers.bio: Brian is a Principal Systems Engineer in Intercom's Dublin office. He fixes problems, builds things and grows people. He loves helping teams, and what they build, to be both resilient and delightful, increasingly using AI to do so.

speakers.name: Carl Assmann
speakers.title: Product Engineer
speakers.bio: Proud dad and product engineer at the German Airforce. I have a passion for progressive web apps and local-first. I organize and speak at the Munich TypeScript Meetup and love sharing my passion and excitement about my craft. When not at the desk, coding or with my family, you'll find me working out, playing badminton or cooking pasta in the kitchen.

speakers.name: Charlie Cheever
speakers.title: CEO & Co-founder
speakers.bio: Charlie is the CEO and Co-Founder of Expo. Previously he was an early engineer at Facebook and then became the co-founder of Quora where he realized that mobile apps were too difficult to build. Which is how the idea for Expo was born.

speakers.name: Christian Liebel
speakers.title: Software Developer
speakers.bio: Christian Liebel is a developer with a passion for modern web technologies. He has been recognized as a Microsoft Most Valuable Professional (MVP) and a Google Developer Expert (GDE) and speaks at user groups and conferences worldwide. As a member of the W3C Technical Architecture Group (TAG), he helps push the boundaries of the web.

speakers.name: Christian Weyer
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Christian Weyer is Co-Founder and CTO of Thinktecture AG and a catalyst for new technologies with a strong focus on the practical implementation of Generative AI in modern software solutions. With 30 years of experience in software engineering, he advises ISVs and enterprises across industries on how to efficiently integrate LLMs and advanced AI technologies into real-world applications. As a cross-vertical AI technology advisor, Christian bridges strategy and execution — helping organizations move Generative AI from theory into productive practice. He is a passionate international speaker, sharing hands-on insights at conferences and events worldwide. Christian is a Microsoft MVP for AI and a Google Developer Expert (GDE) for Web AI, maintaining close relationships with the product teams in Redmond and Mountain View.

speakers.name: Christin Pohl
speakers.title: Global Black Belt Solution Engineer
speakers.bio: Christin Pohl is a Global Black Belt Solution Engineer for AI Infrastructure at Microsoft (Switzerland), now in her third year. After building her first chatbot in 2018 and 5+ years at SAP, she helps enterprises worldwide choose the right GPU, run LLM training and inference end-to-end (LLMOps), and optimize performance (latency, throughput, and cost per token). She studied Information Technology and Computer Science in Berlin, Toronto, and at Harvard.

speakers.name: Christoph Kober
speakers.title: Lead Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Christoph is a passionate Software Crafter with a strong focus on software architecture and continuous learning. In his role as Lead Software Engineer for Transformation at SQUER, he helps customers modernize their legacy applications, improve their software architecture, and reshape their software engineering culture. He is convinced that principles and practices from the Domain-Driven Design universe are most helpful for tackling the complex challenges of large transformation initiatives.

speakers.name: Christoph Menzel
speakers.title: Head of Security Excellence
speakers.bio: Christoph Menzel is Head of Security Excellence at inovex. With more than 15 years of experience in software development, he combines practical developer expertise with security know-how from technical, strategic, and business perspectives. His clear goal is to help organizations understand security not as a burden, but as an integral part of software development. As a speaker, trainer, coach, and author, Christoph regularly shares his practical, field-tested knowledge. His hands-on mentality and focus on sustainable solutions make him a valuable partner for companies looking to take their software security to the next level.

speakers.name: Colleen Lake
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Colleen Lavin is a Developer Advocate at GitLab, with a background in embedded systems, software development and product management. She’s worked with NASA and was featured on Gimlet Media’s Startup podcast. When she’s not playing with robots or coding you can usually find her lost in the woods or on twitter (@colleenjokes).

speakers.name: Daniel Drack
speakers.title: Senior DevOps Engineer
speakers.bio: Daniel started his IT career as an administrator for ETL and BI tools. He then worked some time in a data warehousing team before diving into all topics devops and cloud native. In his current role he is a DevOps and cloud-native consultant with FullStackS GmbH. Daniel holds a BSc in Industrial Engineering, a MA in Innovation Management and an MBA. He's a Kubestronaut, CNCF Ambassador, eager learner and teacher, lecturing at two Austrian universities of applied science. Daniel currently lives in Graz with his girlfriend and his tomcat Manfred 🐈. 🌐 drackthor.me 📧 daniel@drackthor.me

speakers.name: Daniel Meilak
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Dr Daniel Meilak is a Senior Software Engineer with a background in Computational Physics. After 9 years of grueling studies, he found that life became a lot more exciting when he was able to write bug ridden lines of C++ at EGYM

speakers.name: Daniel Oh
speakers.title: Senior Principal Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Daniel Oh is a Java Champion and Senior Principal Developer Advocate at IBM, where he leads efforts to advance cloud native innovation through open source technologies. Renowned for his ability to bridge technical and collaborative gaps, he empowers developers and organizations to build transformative solutions from AI-driven applications and serverless architectures to resilient microservices. As a CNCF Ambassador and TAG DevEx co-chair, Daniel actively shapes the cloud native ecosystem, fostering partnerships between enterprise developers, AI engineers, and platform engineering teams to accelerate hybrid cloud adoption. His technical leadership extends beyond code contributions, as he mentors communities and champions strategies that enable businesses to thrive in evolving digital landscapes. A dynamic storyteller and educator, Daniel captivates global audiences through keynote speeches, workshops, and interactive sessions, where he demystifies emerging technologies and inspires the tech community to embrace the future of open source innovation.

speakers.name: Daniel Ostrovsky
speakers.title: UI/UX Architect
speakers.bio: A seasoned Full Cycle Web Development Expert and R&D Manager, brings over two decades of experience, skillfully leading both local and international development teams from concept to delivery. As a recognized public speaker and blog writer, he also makes notable contributions to the open-source community. In addition, Daniel leads the "NG-Heroes" meetup, creating networking and knowledge-sharing opportunities within the tech industry. Beyond his professional endeavors, he values family time above all else, proudly owning the title of "The Best Developer in the World," humorously awarded by his wife. Combining technical leadership, community involvement, and a strong family focus.

speakers.name: Daniel Seybold
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: Daniel started his career as PhD student in the area of cloud computing with a focus on distributed databases in the cloud. Further interests cover cloud orchestration, model-driven engineering, and performance evaluations of distributed systems. After completing his PhD, Daniel has co-founded the Benchmarking-as-a-Service platform benchANT where he is responsible for the product development and large-scale benchmark projects.

speakers.name: David Klemme
speakers.title: Co-founder and CTO
speakers.bio: Hybrid AI and enterprise technology leader with 10+ years designing, implementing, and scaling production-grade AI platforms in complex enterprise environments. Bridges executive vision with technical delivery. From strategy and governance down to architecture, infrastructure, and code. As Enterprise Architect at Rödl & Partner (~7,000 employees), architected a multi-tenant generative AI platform serving 6,000+ users with EU AI Act-aligned access controls. Led enterprise data strategy and Azure Integration Services rollout at enterprise scale. Previous experience includes ITSM transformation for German public sector (VBG) and AWS data architecture for Deutsche Bahn subsidiary.

speakers.name: Dennis Doomen
speakers.title: Principal Consultant
speakers.bio: Dennis is a Microsoft MVP and Principal Consultant at Dutch Microsoft consultancy firm Aviva Solutions. With 29 years of experience under his belt as a software architect and/or lead developer, he specializes in designing full-stack enterprise solutions based on .NET as well as providing coaching on all aspects of designing, building, documenting, deploying and maintaining software systems in an agile world. He is the author of several open-source projects, including Fluent Assertions, Pathy, Reflectify, PackageGuard, and several .NET Solution Templates, and has been maintaining coding guidelines for C# since 2001. You can find him on Twitter, Mastodon and BlueSky.

speakers.name: Dennis Schulz
speakers.title: Senior Consultant
speakers.bio: Dennis Schulz is a Senior Consultant at TNG Technology Consulting. He holds a PhD in low temperature physics from the University of Heidelberg. Besides being a programmer, he organized and hosted the TV show Quasi Klar for RNF, published a book that was translated to Korean and Russian, and won Science Slam competitions all over Germany. As a part of the Innovation Hacking team at TNG, he worked on different AI showcases, fine-tuning embeddings, and data mining.

speakers.name: Dmitry Yanter
speakers.title: Director Technology, Data and Innovation
speakers.bio: Dmitry Yanter is an engineering manager with 13+ years at Deutsche Bank, 20 years experience in the industry, holding multiple roles in research, start ups and enterprise. He is member of International Chamber of Commerce working group that defines digital standards for Supply Chain Finance.

speakers.name: Dominik Schmidle
speakers.title: Group Product Manager
speakers.bio: Dominik is a Group Product Manager for Platforms at Giant Swarm with the mission of simplifying developers' lives through intuitive developer platforms. He started as a Full Stack Software Engineer, discovered his passion for DevOps and product organizations, and transitioned into Product Management for cloud technology. He actively shares his experiences through talks, articles, and publications.

speakers.name: Dominik Srednicki
speakers.title: Principal Architect
speakers.bio: For 20 years Dominik has worked in software development, covering roles from hands-on developer to architect to technical leader. For the last 5 years he's been deeply immersed in AI technologies - building real systems, running experiments, and figuring out what survives contact with production.

speakers.name: Edward Smyshliaiev
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Edward is the Chief Technology Officer at GR8 Tech and a hands-on technology leader with 23+ years of building, scaling, and rescuing complex systems in production. His sweet spot is where high traffic meets hard constraints: legacy platforms, SSR, performance budgets, reliability, and modernization that has to happen without pausing the business. He’s known for turning “this is too complicated” into a clear plan teams can ship — breaking down abstract problems into practical architecture, measurable metrics, and repeatable execution. Over the years, he’s led large cross-functional orgs through digital transformation, improved platform performance and operational efficiency, and delivered outcomes that moved the business needle (from faster user experiences to leaner infrastructure and smoother delivery). Outside work, you’ll find him on a hockey rink, hanging out with his two daughters, or switching gears with chess and guitar. He also mentors engineers and occasionally guest-speaks at universities — because learning (and teaching) is part of the job.

speakers.name: Elaine Dias Batista
speakers.title: Senior Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: Elaine is a Senior Staff Engineer at SFEIR where she works on projects including mobile technologies since 2013. She has worked on several projects in native Android/iOS and Flutter. She's a GDE for the Google Assistant, Flutter and Dart

speakers.name: Emiliano Della Casa
speakers.title: Independent Software Architect
speakers.bio: Born in Modena in 1980, I have been working with Ruby on Rails for over 20 years. I'm a software engineer and consultant, passionate about the Rails framework and its community. Since the early days of Rails, I’ve been helping small and medium-sized businesses build and maintain robust web applications. I enjoy sharing what I’ve learned over the years, and I’ve had the pleasure of speaking at conferences like Modena DevFest and RubyDay 2024 in Verona, where I focus on delivering practical, real-world insights from my experience in the field.

speakers.name: Erasmo Hernandez
speakers.title: Senior Web UI Developer and Tech Lead
speakers.bio: For the last 15 years I have been working on Web Development, projects from scratch, helping clients and agencies to build the entire idea until the final product. Coding on both Front-end and Back-end, depending on the project and the available resources. I have worked with a variety of technologies and frameworks, and I am always looking for new challenges and opportunities to learn and grow. Currently I am working as a Tech Lead for one of the biggest Airlines in Europe @ Globant. Using Next.js, Typescript, Tailwind CSS, Docker, Storybook, and more. I have used Javascript, Typescript, React, Vue, GraphQL, MongoDB, MySQL, CSS, Sass, Docker, PHP, Wordpress, Laravel and more. I also love Data related subjects, and been learning Data Science with R and Python on my spare time.

speakers.name: Erhardt Wunderlich
speakers.title: Member
speakers.bio: Erhardt Wunderlich works as a freelance coach and supports companies in improving the development processes to deliver high quality products. Since 1990, Mr. Wunderlich has dealt with the topic of software testing in various industries (including telecommunications, storage automation, rail vehicles, automotive) in different roles (including developer, tester, test manager, test department manager, quality coach) and has also supported the agile transformation in different organizations.

speakers.name: Evgeniya Sukhodolskaya
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Developer Relations at Qdrant with 8 years of IT experience across software engineering, machine learning, and technical management, and 4 years in Developer Relations. Holds a Master’s in Machine Learning, Data Analytics, and Data Engineering. Passionate about NLP, data-centric AI, and the role of vector search in advancing AI technologies.

speakers.name: Fabiano Amorim
speakers.title: Principal Consultant
speakers.bio: Fabiano Amorim is a Microsoft Data Platform most valuable professional (MVP) since 2011. With over two decades of experience, Fabiano is well known in the database community for his performance tuning abilities and the many conference speaking engagements around the world. He writes articles for Simple-Talk (https://www.simple-talk.com/author/fabiano-amorim/) and his blog is on http://blogfabiano.com.

speakers.name: Fabrizio Lazzaretti
speakers.title: Managing Consultant
speakers.bio: Fabrizio Lazzaretti is a Managing Consultant at Wavestone and CNCF Ambassador who bridges cutting-edge cloud-native technologies with enterprise architecture. As maintainer of the CloudEvents project and co-author of "Crafting Great APIs with Domain-Driven Design," he brings deep expertise in event-driven architecture and microservices to complex challenges. With over ten years in software architecture, development, and DevOps, he currently drives architectural transformation in the insurance industry, bridging the gap between business and IT.

speakers.name: Filip Egeric
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Software engineer with extensive experience building robust backend and frontend applications. Skilled in writing clean, maintainable code, and optimizing performance and scalability of complex systems. Proven ability to lead critical architecture migrations, mentor development teams, and deliver efficient, impactful solutions under pressure. Passionate collaborator and continuous learner, eager to improve and share knowledge.

speakers.name: Florian Bader
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Florian is a Solution Architect at Lunaris Digital Solutions and a Microsoft MVP for Azure IoT and DevOps with many years of experience in DevOps, Cloud, and Digitalization. He helps companies develop efficient and effective solutions that sustainably lead their digital projects to success.

speakers.name: Florian Gather
speakers.title: Associate Partner
speakers.bio: After earning his PhD in Physics, Dr. Florian Gather transitioned to software development and joined TNG, where he currently works as an Associate Partner. He specializes in analyzing software projects - conducting due diligence, architecture reviews, and technical assessments to help organizations improve system resilience and maintainability. Florian founded ArchUnit.NET, an open-source tool for enforcing architectural rules in .NET projects. With TNG’s Innovation Hacking team, he drives robotics initiatives that explore LLM integration and Robotics Foundational Models (RFMs), alongside prototypes like smart office systems and interactive furniture. Passionate about microprocessor electronics and smart home automation, Florian explores practical ways to merge hardware tinkering with modern software practices.

speakers.name: Franz Kiraly
speakers.title: Director
speakers.company: German Center for Open Source AI
speakers.bio: Dr Dr Franz Kiraly is the director for the German Center for Open Source AI, the German non-profit organisation supporting sovereign European open source AI software projects. Dr Kiraly has held technical leadership positions in industry, and academic faculty positions (University College London, Alan Turing Institute), with a career focus on open source and applied AI.

speakers.name: Franziska Höhne
speakers.title: Project Manager
speakers.bio: Franziska Höhne is a Project Management Professional (PMP), industrial engineer, coach, and international speaker specializing in communication during organizational transformation. With nearly 20 years of experience in complex, global environments, she works at the intersection of technology, leadership, and psychology to help leaders and teams navigate change with clarity and confidence. Her perspective combines engineering precision with psychological insight. She focuses on how language, tone, and interaction influence alignment, decision-making, and trust - especially when organizations restructure, adopt new systems, or shift their operating models. She supports and leads transformation projects by designing frameworks that make change understandable, transparent, and actionable. Her work turns uncertainty into shared direction and enables teams to move from reaction to ownership. Her approach is grounded in practical tools, intercultural experience, and nearly two decades of hands-on project and audit work. Franziska serves as Vice President Marketing of the PMI Germany Chapter, part of the global Project Management Institute (PMI), and is a member of the German Speakers Association (GSA).

speakers.name: Gaurav Gahlot
speakers.title: Staff Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Gaurav Gahlot is a Software Engineer passionate about building low-level systems, Rust, and Cloud Native ecosystem. He loves to share his learning via his blog, and is also a maintainer of CNCF’s Akri project.

speakers.name: George Proorocu
speakers.title: IT OPS Manager
speakers.bio: George is a seasoned IT professional with 15+ years of experience, evolving from technical roles to leading engineering teams in security-critical sectors. Passionate about coaching and empowering others, he combines hands-on expertise with strategic leadership. A former professional chess player, George won the "Romanian Chess Champion" title with his club team in 2004. At 16, he founded and grew an ISP company in Bucharest to 600 clients before selling it at 18. He later pursued an Executive MBA at IMT Paris to strengthen his leadership skills. Recognized in the Financial Times' "100 Faces of Innovation" in 2016, George is a sought-after Cybersecurity expert specializing in Fraud and Deepfakes. He has spoken at over 20 conferences all around the world, and his beginner’s Cybersecurity course has helped 150,000+ students start their careers in the field.

speakers.name: Goetz Markgraf
speakers.title: Senior Consultant
speakers.bio: Goetz Markgraf ist Consultant bei der codecentric AG. Als passionierter Softwareentwickler seit über 30 Jahren und Product Owner macht es ihm besondere Freude, zwischen Technik und Fachbereich zu vermitteln und zu "übersetzen". Er beschäftigt sich seit Mitte 2023 mit dem Thema "KI-gestützte Softwareentwicklung". Privat ist er Hobbyautor und stolzer Vater dreier Kinder.

speakers.name: Hajed Khlifi
speakers.title: AI / HPC Architect
speakers.bio: Cloud Architect specializes in the management of large-scale systems and the development of cutting-edge solutions based on platform engineering , Infrastructure and operations best practices. My work is focused on cloud-native architectures , Disconnected cloud and lately I am mainly working on AI Infrastructure and operations. I am an international speaker, researcher and also an active contributor to the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) this involvement led to my official recognition as a Kubestronaut.

speakers.name: Halil İbrahim Kalkan
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: I am a software architect and developer. I love to work on open source projects, producing articles, videos and giving talks on software development. I'm the lead developer of the ABP framework (see https://github.com/abpframework/abp and https://abp.io/) I'm mostly interested in developing reusable frameworks & libraries, distributed systems, multi-threaded and scalable solutions. I'm talking at local and international conferences about multi-tenancy, microservice architecture, domain-driven design, modular systems and developer tools. Visit my website to see a list of my previous talks: https://halilibrahimkalkan.com

speakers.name: Hartmut Armbruster
speakers.title: Software Architect
speakers.bio: Hartmut is a software engineer and tech lead with a strong passion for architecture, data streaming, and distributed systems. He has designed and delivered solutions for mission-critical platforms, working with clients including HSBC, NEX Group plc, Raiffeisen Switzerland, Deutsche Bahn, and eu-LISA. Hartmut is driven by a desire to see the bigger picture and excels at aligning engineering teams through clear, compelling architectural designs.

speakers.name: Holger Hammel
speakers.title: VP of Engineering
speakers.bio: Holger Hammel is Vice President Engineering at HelloFresh, where he leads the Consumer Alliance – overseeing all customer-facing digital products and related services across HelloFresh brands globally. He joined the company in 2024 to further scale and strengthen its consumer technology ecosystem. With more than 15 years of leadership experience at companies such as eBay, Mobile International, Aiven, and OneFootball, Holger has a strong track record in building high-performing engineering organizations. He is particularly known for bridging the gap between engineering and business strategy, driving innovation, and delivering scalable, customer-centric solutions.

speakers.name: Hussein Jundi
speakers.title: AI Engineering Lead
speakers.bio: Hussein Jundi is a Munich-based AI Engineering Lead who enjoys turning ambiguous ideas into real, scalable solutions. At E.ON Digital Technology, he drives AI engineering projects across E.ON’s cloud native software engineering technology platforms, assessing new capabilities and leading initiatives that accelerate modernization and AI utilization. Previously, he led Data & AI squads at E.ON and helped build and scale data platforms that power reliable analytics and product development in the mobility/charging space.

speakers.name: Ilie-Daniel Gheorghe-Pop
speakers.title: Researcher
speakers.bio: M.Sc in Computer Engineering, specialized in advanced computer architectures. Now researcher at Fraunhofer FOKUS in Quality Engineering for Quantum Computing. Works in several industry and research projects in the fields of quantum computing, artificial intelligence, IoT, IT security and telco. Main interest is researching efficient application development and implementation of algorithms especially in the quantum domain (Quantum DevOps). Other areas of interest are quantum computing, quantum networks, IoT, Industry 4.0, 5G/6G, AI, robots and the high-level quantum programming language Qrisp.

speakers.name: Ingo Eichhorst
speakers.title: Engineering Trainer
speakers.bio: Ingo Eichhorst is a tech expert with over 15 years of experience as a developer, solution architect, and CTO. Currently, he works as an engineering trainer at IONOS and as a lecturer for Machine Learning and Generative AI at the University of Applied Sciences Nordhausen. As a solution architect at Monstarlab and KPMG, he developed AI solutions for industry, banking, legal tech, and e-commerce. In addition to his involvement with AI startups and open-source projects, where he is responsible for technical planning and implementation, he works as a mentor and regularly shares his knowledge at tech conferences.

speakers.name: Irina Branovic
speakers.title: Tech Lead
speakers.bio: Dr. Irina Brancovic is a Software Engineer at the Hamburg-based adtech company, adjoe. She earned her PhD in Information Engineering in Italy, where she not only conducted academic research but also taught at the university level in Italian, a language she learned from scratch during her studies. Since joining adjoe, Irina has been instrumental in overcoming challenges to build the backend infrastructure, which handles billions of requests from 600 million daily users. She takes pride in her contributions to creating innovative ad technologies and fostering a collaborative and productive work environment.

speakers.name: Iulia Feroli
speakers.title: Content Creator
speakers.bio: Iulia’s mission is to make tech exciting, understandable, and accessible to the new generation. With a background spanning data science, AI, cloud architecture, and open source she brings a unique perspective on bridging technical depth with approachability. She’s building her own brand, Back To Engineering, through which she creates a community for tech enthusiasts, engineers, and makers. From YouTube videos on building robots from scratch, to conference talks or keynotes about real, grounded AI, and technical blogs & tutorials — Iulia shares her message worldwide on how to turn complex concepts into tools developers can use every day.

speakers.name: Jakub Gaj
speakers.title: Cloud Solution Architect
speakers.bio: I've been building cool stuff on the Internet since 1999. Trigram tech enthusiast (IBM, AWS). I help enterprise companies navigate their digital transformation journeys, specializing in data center migrations, cloud migrations & modernizations, as well as cloud-native development. When I'm offline, you can find me chasing the Endless Summer with my family, exploring surf spots around the world. Currently based in Copenhagen, Denmark.

speakers.name: Jakub Janczyk
speakers.title: Senior Engineer/Tech Lead
speakers.bio: Software Engineer passionate about building products users love. Believes that efficient team collaboration is the key to success. When not coding, he’s probably (once again) reminding everyone why proper automated testing is essential for a happy life. After hours, he takes on most challenging role yet - taming his two kids.

speakers.name: Jan de Vries
speakers.title: DevOps Advocate
speakers.bio: Jan de Vries is a senior trainer, business IT consultant, coach, researcher and public speaker in the fields of Agile, DevOps, Business Information Management, Service Management, Antifragility and Strategy Development. He founded: - BlueOceanRecon.com to facilitate the development of Blue Oceans and Lean Startups. - Antifragility.works to conduct research on the practical application of antifragility in organisations.

speakers.name: Jan Peer Stöcklmair
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: I’m a software engineer working at Sentry and Pebblebyte, where I focus on frontend, DevOps, and making sure observability is everywhere. When I’m not deep in code, you’ll probably find me kitesurfing, road biking, swimming, or hanging off a rock wall. Whether I’m debugging or chasing wind, I’m always looking for that perfect flow.

speakers.name: Jayden Watson
speakers.title: CEO and Founder
speakers.bio: Jayden Watson is the CEO and founder of AION, the Everyday AI Platform. Jayden created AION to make advanced AI infrastructure radically accessible — giving builders, enterprises, and innovators a single platform to go from idea to inference at scale. A self-taught coder, algorithmic trader, and crypto-native founder, Jayden has spent over a decade building at the frontier of AI, blockchain, and systems design. From creating early automated arbitrage strategies to engineering luxury logistics protocols, his work has always focused on turning friction into opportunity. At AION, he’s now turning GPUs into accessible resources-- and AI into a utility-- through a full-stack platform that combines compute orchestration, fine-tuning tools, open-source models, and seamless deployment. By abstracting away complexity and enabling ownership, AION is defining what the future of AI infrastructure looks like: decentralized, user-first, and built for everyone.

speakers.name: Jeff Blankenburg
speakers.title: Principal Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Jeff Blankenburg spent the early part of his career in digital advertising, building websites for Victoria’s Secret, Abercrombie & Fitch, and Ford Motor Company, among others. He also spent 8 years at Microsoft, primarily as an evangelist for any new technology he could get his hands on. For the next 7 years, he worked on the Amazon Alexa team helping developers make Alexa even smarter. Today, he is the Head of Developer Relations for DigitalOcean. Jeff has also spoken at conferences all over the world, including London, Munich, Sydney, Tokyo, and New York, covering topics ranging from software development technologies to soft skill techniques. He also serves as an organizer for the Stir Trek conference.

speakers.name: Jennifer Wjertzoch
speakers.title: Senior Frontend Engineer
speakers.bio: Jennifer Wjertzoch is a Senior Frontend Engineer at DKB Code Factory GmbH, where she builds scalable and accessible web systems in the financial domain. She specializes in accessibility architecture and believes that inclusion is not an add-on but a fundamental principle of software quality and risk management. Her work focuses on integrating accessibility into system design, developer workflows, and CI/CD processes to create products that are resilient, compliant, and user-centered.

speakers.name: John Fletcher
speakers.title: Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: John’s first job was as a professional snowboarder, winning the Junior World Championship in 1999. Since then, he has spent 20 years developing software across Australia, Spain and Germany. He continues to build software, loves to speak at conferences and trains people to accelerate companies from the bottom up, using technical excellence, human interaction and fun.

speakers.name: Jörg Jakoby
speakers.title: Software Developer
speakers.bio: Experienced software developer with 17 years of experience in frontend development. Specialized in the development of web applications with Angular and extensive knowledge of JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. Great emphasis on accessible web content and is an Atos Accessibility Champion as well as a Certified Professional in Web Accessibility (CPWA). Successfully developed web-based user interfaces while considering the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2. Leads frontend teams, plans, and implements microfrontend architectures, thus ensuring structured and modular development.

speakers.name: Julia Koch
speakers.title: Executive Director, CTO and CCO
speakers.bio: As CTO and CCO, Julia Koch is responsible for the strategic direction and execution of software development, guiding her teams in delivering innovative and customer-centric banking solutions for the German Savings Banks Group, which serves over 50 million people in Germany. Her core objective is to lead her teams in shaping the future of modern banking. She’s committed to harnessing AI and other innovative technologies to enhance customer experience.

speakers.name: Julia Kordick
speakers.title: Senior Software Global Black Belt
speakers.bio: Julia is a Software Global Black Belt at Microsoft, helping large-scale organizations accelerate dev workflows through pragmatic, end-to-end platform engineering. With a background as a Software Engineer, Dev Lead, and Cloud Solution Architect, she speaks fluent developer and management—and knows how to bridge the gap without losing either side in translation. She’s a big-picture thinker who also loves to understand the details. Web and frontend will always have a place in her ❤️. Outside work, Julia is a gamer, a proud fahrradmaus, and a former quarterback for a women’s American football team (yes, really).

speakers.name: Julian Burr
speakers.title: Senior Developer
speakers.bio: Senior Developer from Germany with over 15 years of experience building web apps and leading frontend teams, currently living and working down under in Australia. Pragmatic perfectionist, always looking for opportunities to learn and grow.

speakers.name: Juraci Paixão Kröhling
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Juraci Paixão Kröhling is a software engineer at OllyGarden, a maintainer of the OpenTelemetry project, a member of the project's governing board and CNCF Ambassador. He has presented about distributed tracing, OpenTelemetry, and other related topics at conferences like KubeCon, OpenSource Summit, FOSDEM, and a few DevOpsDays, among others. Previously, he was also a maintainer of both the OpenTracing and Jaeger projects, having also delivered talks about those projects.

speakers.name: Kesha Mykhailov
speakers.title: Principal Engineer
speakers.bio: Kesha is an engineer with over 20 years of experience in making customers happy through building product, running distributed systems, tuning socio-technical systems, and more recently building with AI. Kesha is a Principal Engineer at Intercom building the future of AI-centric customer service.

speakers.name: Leon Varga
speakers.title: Senior Software Developer
speakers.bio: Dr. Leon Varga is a Senior Software Developer at Q.ANT, where he works on the software stack and application development for photonic processors. He holds a PhD in computer science from the University of Tübingen, with a research background in computer vision and machine learning. Drawing on both, applied AI research and hands-on software engineering, Leon focuses on turning cutting-edge technology into practical, developer-friendly tools.

speakers.name: Liran Hason
speakers.title: VP of AI
speakers.bio: Liran Hason is VP of AI at Coralogix, where he leads the company’s AI strategy, architecture and execution across autonomous agents and enterprise AI. A builder, founder, and engineering leader with more than 20 years of experience, he has spent much of his career making AI systems more reliable, responsible, and ready for the real world. Before Coralogix, he founded and served as CEO of Aporia, a pioneer in AI observability and guardrails, later acquired by Coralogix, and earlier was an AI Architect at Adallom, acquired by Microsoft.

speakers.name: Lorenzo Petricone
speakers.title: Fullstack Developer
speakers.bio: Lorenzo is a Consultant and Fullstack Developer at codecentric, currently working with TypeScript, Angular, and Kotlin. He’s also tracking trends like Large Language Models and enjoys diving into new fields, with a particular interest in the future of Quantum Computing.

speakers.name: Luca Bianchi
speakers.title: CTIO
speakers.bio: Over the past decade, I've architected production AI/ML systems processing 100M+ daily events through event-driven serverless architectures. AWS Serverless Hero and Cursor Ambassador focused on cost-optimized AI infrastructure and real-time data pipelines. Production track record: - Built production AI Agents for legal domains, managing very long contexts, planning, and dynamic loading through event-driven orchestration - Built production GenAI platform serving 500K+ requests/day with <200ms p95 latency - Scaled cloud SaaS to 10M+ users, reducing p99 latency from 2s to 1.2s through event-driven architecture migration - Migrated 80% of workloads to serverless event-driven patterns, cutting infrastructure costs 10x while improving deployment velocity Led engineering teams of 100+ through cultural transformation toward continuous delivery and experimentation-driven development. Co-founder of the Serverless Italy community, Milano Mongo User Group, and Cursor Meetup Milano.

speakers.name: Luca Trușcă
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Luca Trușcă is a Software Engineer at Adobe, building core font experiences for Adobe Express. He specializes in web performance, developer tooling, and bridging the gap between frontend and backend teams. Before joining Adobe, he was an indie game developer whose work reached over 400,000 players. He is passionate about sharing knowledge and believes that lifting up the whole team is the only way to build great software.

speakers.name: Luise Freese
speakers.title: Architect
speakers.bio: Luise is an Azure & Power Platform Architect based in Germany. She is an awarded Microsoft Most Valuable Professional working with organizations that want to move beyond digital theatre. Her focus is on AI-enabled solutions that are designed to last in real-world environments. She helps teams build accessible apps, robust data models, and automation that respects governance, compliance, and human effort. A recurring theme in her work is making the invisible visible: unmeasured work, emotional load, operational friction, and the real cost of “quick wins”. Her motto "Changing the world one app at a time" stems from her extensive work with non-profit organizations. Alongside client work, Luise writes and speaks about productivity myths, pseudo-agile practices, AI readiness, and why many digital initiatives fail long before technology becomes the problem. She is known for combining clear technical guidance with a sharp eye for organizational patterns; and for asking the uncomfortable questions others tend to avoid. When not working and traveling, she is a runner, a mother of (teenage) dragons, a LEGO-builder and a lover of the number 42. Her favorite color is #ff69b4.

speakers.name: Lutz Huehnken
speakers.title: Fractional CTO, Interim Manager and Architect
speakers.bio: Lutz is an independent consultant working at the intersection of software architecture and organizational design, a frequent conference speaker, and a blogger. Until recently, he was Head of Engineering at Upvest, a Berlin-based FinTech company that makes investing accessible to everyone. Previously, he led engineering teams building software for container shipping, online retail, financial institutions, and even cruise ship entertainment systems. His current focus is on event-driven architecture, highly available and scalable systems, and strategic domain-driven design.

speakers.name: Manfred Bjørlin
speakers.title: Principal Cloud Native Architect
speakers.bio: Principal Cloud Native architect and developer with a passion for integration and automation. But wait, it's more! Passion does not only have to be in tech! There is also a burning passion for neurodiversity and diversity in tech, across ages, genders, identities, etc... Loves integration, automation and making processes more streamlined. A back-end developer for over 20 years. Have worked with building integration platforms in the cloud for several bigger organizations. Worked with .Net since early 2000, and Azure since launch. Have also been a team lead, worked with process streamlining, enterprise architecture and everything between that and made my hands dirty as a developer and cloud native architect.

speakers.name: Manfred Steyer
speakers.title: Google Developer Expert
speakers.bio: Trainer and Consultant with focus on Angular. Google Developer Expert (GDE) for Angular and Trusted Collaborator in the Angular team. Writes for O'Reilly, Hanser and the German Java Magazine. Regularly speaks at conferences.

speakers.name: Marc Müller
speakers.title: Principal Consultant
speakers.bio: Marc Müller is a principal consultant for DevOps and .NET / Azure solutions at 4tecture GmbH and has been recognized by Microsoft as a Most Valuable Professional (MVP) for Developer Technologies (Azure DevOps). He has been able to bring his DevOps expertise and know-how for enterprise architectures and component-based distributed systems to many projects over the past few years. As a trainer and speaker, he focuses on the training and coaching of DevOps and .NET project teams.

speakers.name: Marcin Makowski
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Marcin Makowski is CEO at BeOne and a software architect focused on building production-grade AI and orchestration platforms. With over 20 years of experience in distributed systems, enterprise automation, and large-scale process execution, he specializes in designing deterministic architectures in non-deterministic environments. Marcin works at the intersection of AI infrastructure, workflow orchestration, and decision modeling. His recent focus includes LLM platform engineering, model gateways, hybrid retrieval architectures, and building reproducible AI systems with strong observability and auditability guarantees. He is a strong advocate of open ecosystems and engineering-first approaches to AI adoption. Instead of treating AI as a feature, he designs it as a runtime component that must meet the same standards of reliability, scalability, and traceability as any other production system. Marcin co-authored research on dynamic decision model generation for tax compliance (accepted at ISD 2025) and regularly speaks about distributed systems, AI platform design, and deterministic orchestration in intelligent systems.

speakers.name: Marco Sussitz
speakers.title: Senior Software Developer
speakers.bio: Marco Sussitz is an engineer at Dynatrace who lives at the boundary of Java and C++. He writes JVMTI agents, instruments bytecode, and digs into JIT, memory, and thread behavior. You’ll often find him sorting out class loaders or, reading the spec longer than he should. Before Dynatrace, he worked on cloud video encoding with FFmpeg. Off hours he’s bouldering or in the mountains.

speakers.name: Markus Hacker
speakers.title: Senior Regional Director
speakers.bio: Markus leads the Enterprise Business for NVIDIA in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland. Based in Munich, he oversees the company’s go-to-market activities for enterprises in Central Europe, focusing on advancing AI, visualization, and virtualization technologies. Markus’ career began in Microsoft’s Central European Data Centeroperations. Before joining NVIDIA in 2019, he held leadership roles at Akamai, Lionbridge, T-Systems, and Colt Technology Services. His two decades of experience in sales and business management have equipped him with deep expertise in the IT industry's evolving needs. Markus is committed to fostering innovation and making valuable contributions to the field of AI. At NVIDIA, Markus leverages his knowledge to help businesses achieve their AI and industrial digitalization goals.

speakers.name: Martina Kraus
speakers.title: Application Security Engineer
speakers.bio: Martina Kraus has been active in web development since her early years and has become an expert in web security. As an Application Security Engineer, she focuses on integrating security best practices into all phases of software development. In her role as a Google Developer Expert (GDE) in Angular and Identity, she enjoys sharing her knowledge of Angular security at both national and international conferences. She is also currently writing a German book on authentication and authorization in web applications.

speakers.name: Matic Petrič
speakers.title: Quantum Computing Researcher
speakers.bio: Matic Petrič studied theoretical physics at University of Ljubljana, Faculty of Mathematics in physics. During his studies, Petrič worked at T-Labs, the Deutsche Telekom Laboratories, where he derived a problem formulation for optimizing traffic flow in real time with Quantum Annealing. After finishing his degree during the pandemic, he is now working as a Quantum Computing Researcher at Fraunhofer FOKUS, focusing on hybrid quantum-classical algorithms, and working on Eclipse Qrisp - a high level programming language he'll be presenting on stage together with Raphael Seidel during WAD'24.

speakers.name: Merlin Pahic
speakers.title: Staff Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Staff Software Engineer at EIDU, a social enterprise dedicated to improving learning outcomes for children in low-resource settings worldwide. Experienced in full-stack development, including on the Android platform. As an engineer, I'm passionate about functional programming, static typing and property-based testing. As a human, I am dedicated to improving learning outcomes and opportunities for children all around the world.

speakers.name: Mert Akça
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Hi, I’m Mert! I’m a Software Engineer based in Berlin. I’m a "user-driven" engineer at heart, which means I care just as much about the human at the other end of the screen as I do about clean, type-safe code. Whether I’m closely working with other engineers or designers, my goal is always the same: making the experience faster, more inclusive, and a little more fun. Beyond the day job, I love writing about tech—from deep dives into designs to the joys of programming—and I built a browser-based mystery game to explore the limits of interactive storytelling. I'm a big believer in "learning by doing" and love a good live-coding challenge!

speakers.name: Michal Cyprian
speakers.title: Engineering Manager
speakers.bio: Michal is an Engineering Manager at Kiwi.com, a leading global travel tech company headquartered in the Czech Republic. He is passionate about leading talented teams and solving challenging problems in the travel tech space. Even with all this responsibility, he still makes time for deep-dive tech experiments – even if it means waking up at 4:30 AM to run them. It is this combination of engineering and leadership that he enjoys the most.

speakers.name: Mike Fynes
speakers.title: Head of Engineering
speakers.bio: Michael Fynes is Head of Engineering at Wolt within Doordash. A technical leader who focuses on getting teams to work smarter, not harder. Coaching teams to work together better wherever they are is his passion. Has helped Wolt scale the fintech organisation from just a handful of people to its current size. Working in a fast moving, rapidly changing environment in a global marketplace, solving problems in innovative ways that get the job done, in a scalable way.

speakers.name: Milica Aleksic
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: I’m a senior software engineer with expertise in mobile development, particularly React Native. I focus on building performant, scalable applications and a great user experience. Beyond code, I’m passionate about community and mentorship. As co-founder of Women in Tech Niš, I organize events, mentor aspiring professionals, and advocate for diversity in tech. I’ve also spoken at international conferences and hosted large-scale events as an MC, combining technical knowledge with clear and engaging communication. I am driven, detail-oriented, and always learning. I bring a mix of technical depth, people-centered leadership, and a genuine love for building things that matter.

speakers.name: Moataz Nabil
speakers.title: Software Engineering Manager
speakers.bio: I created the Mobile DevOps Playbook book It focuses on Quality Engineering, test automation, and passion for quality. I started as a Software Engineer and became a Quality Engineer and SDET. Then I became a Developer Advocate. I help mobile and quality engineers improve app delivery efficiency and quality. I currently lead a team that creates automated tests for QA teams. The tests can handle a large workload. Throughout my career, I gained experience and developed skills for supervising. I have 12 years of experience in software development, quality assurance, and testing. I have achieved notable milestones. I made mobile and web automation systems using tools like Selenium WebDriver. I also used Appium, XCUITest, and Espresso. I created CI/CD pipelines using CircleCI, GitHub Actions, GitLab, and Bitrise. My contributions extend to many open-source projects. I help people learn about test automation worldwide. To do this, I teach free online courses using testing tools like Espresso and Appium. I also use TestCafe. I do this through Test Automation University. Besides to my job, I am an AWS Community Builder and a DevNetwork Advisory Board Member.

speakers.name: Mohammad-Ali A'râbi
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Mohammad-Ali A'râbi is a Senior Backend Engineer, Docker Captain, Snyk Ambassador, CNCF Organizer, public speaker, and author of the "Docker and Kubernetes Security" book, with 20 years of experience in software development. As a Docker Captain, Mohammad-Ali has spoken at various conferences and meetups. In 2022, he founded the Docker meetup group in Freiburg, where he has since organized over a dozen events. Mohammad-Ali’s passion for security and containers shines through in both his technical work and community efforts.

speakers.name: Mourjo Sen
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: I am a professional software engineer for over ten years now, I have worked with object oriented and functional programming on large-scale distributed systems. I find joy in speaking and writing about software engineering. Some of my articles and blogs are available here: https://mourjo.me

speakers.name: Natanael Copa
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Natanael Copa is the creator of Alpine Linux and a maintainer of the k0s Kubernetes distribution. He has over 20 years of experience in Linux and open-source infrastructure, with a focus on minimal, secure systems.

speakers.name: Neno Loje
speakers.title: Independent Consultant
speakers.bio: Neno Loje ist selbstständiger Berater und Professional Scrum Trainer. Mit großer Leidenschaft unterstützt er große wie kleine Unternehmen und Teams dabei, agiler zu werden, das Risiko zu minimieren, mehr zu automatisieren und mit pragmatischen Lösungen den Alltag aufzuwerten. Seine Schwerpunkte sind dabei Microsofts Azure DevOps (ehem. TFS) sowie die agile Organisation der Softwareentwicklung (z.B. nach Scrum).

speakers.name: Nnenna Ndukwe
speakers.title: Principal Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Nnenna Ndukwe is a Principal Developer Advocate and Software Engineer, passionate about AI. With 8+ experience spanning across media tech, cybersecurity, and AI, she's an active global AI community architect championing engineers to build in emerging tech. She studied Computer Science at Boston University and is a proud member of Women Defining AI and Reg.exe. Nnenna believes that AI should augment: enabling creativity, accelerating learning, and preserving the intuition and humanity of its users.

speakers.name: Nune Isabekyan
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Nune Isabekyan is a Berlin-based founder and cloud architecture expert with over 15 years of experience building scalable, intelligent systems. With a mathematics background and deep expertise in AWS, AI, and DevOps, she specializes in practical AI implementation and cloud-native architectures. As founder and CTO of OpsWorker, Nune is building AI-powered alert investigation automation. When an alert fires, OpsWorker automatically investigates, pulls logs, checks dashboards, correlates events, and gives on-call engineers a summary of what's wrong and suggested next steps. Previously, as CIO of powerdata GmbH, she led teams building SaaS products and AI-powered customer service automation for the utilities sector. Her earlier career includes engineering roles at Teradata, Amadeus, and high-scale startups handling millions of daily users.

speakers.name: Oren Penso
speakers.title: Global field CTO Tanzu division
speakers.bio: Oren is a seasoned IT executive with over 20 years of leadership across enterprise and financial sectors, now serving as Global Field CTO in the Tanzu division. A long-time platform advocate, he’s helped organizations navigate the evolution from Cloud Foundry to Kubernetes and now toward AI-native platforms. Oren is a recognised expert, author, and frequent speaker at VMware events, sharing insights drawn from real-world platform transformations at scale.

speakers.name: Osmar Matos
speakers.title: UX/UI Design and Frontend Lead
speakers.bio: Designer by degree, frontend dev by passion, manager when necessary. 15+ years in web development. After designing and coding for blockchain stable coin financial infrastructure, shifted to AI SRE agents — helping SREs and DevOps engineers finally get some sleep.

speakers.name: Patrick Koss
speakers.title: Tech Lead
speakers.bio: I'm a dedicated and highly skilled Tech Lead with a strong passion for Distributed Systems and Cloud-Native Development. My key areas of expertise include: - Backend Development (Go, Python, Rust) - Distributed Systems (Microservices, Service Mesh, Message Queues) - Cloud-Native Technologies (Kubernetes, Docker, Serverless) - Scalable & High-Performance Systems - Continuous Integration & Deployment (CI/CD)

speakers.name: Patrick Müller
speakers.title: Enterprise Architect
speakers.bio: Patrick – Expert in Holistic IT Architecture and Change Management Since beginning his career as an IT consultant in 2005, Patrick has become a recognized expert in IT architecture across all levels. His holistic approach emphasizes the importance of interdisciplinary collaboration by creating synergies between IT disciplines and proactively integrating considerations beyond traditional IT boundaries. With a passion for knowledge sharing and a commitment to integrating various architectural layers, Patrick has successfully initiated, guided, and firmly established transformative change processes within organizations. His ability to analyze complex systems and drive innovative solutions is further enhanced by his extensive network and active roles as an iSAQB Board Member and IASA member. In his current position as Managing Enterprise Architect at Allianz Kunde und Markt, Patrick leads strategic IT transformations with forward-thinking approaches. Additionally, he supports organizations as a consultant, helping them leverage his expertise and practical methodologies to achieve their goals. His talks inspire audiences with in-depth expertise, practical insights, and a compelling vision of a future-oriented, interconnected IT architecture.

speakers.name: Patrick Schnell
speakers.title: Managing Director
speakers.bio: Patrick Schnell is a distinguished computer scientist and acclaimed author specializing in the integration of AI into software development and business-processes. With a solid foundation in computer science and software-projects, Patrick has dedicated his career to exploring the transformative potential of AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) within the software industry. His team established Vectense (www.vectense.com) as an AI-automation-platform for small and mid-sized businesses.

speakers.name: Peter Bindels
speakers.title: Staff Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Peter is an experienced C++ software developer with a particular interest in making things simpler. Part of making things simpler is to understand the full complexity of a particular problem and then seeing what the simplest solution that is still sufficient would be. He is a part of the ISO C++ Committee since 2019 and participates mostly in the Tooling (SG15), Unicode (SG16), Contracts (SG21) and Safety (SG23) study groups.

speakers.name: Elisa Schön
speakers.title: Senior HR Business Partner
speakers.bio: Elisa Schön works at the intersection of psychology, leadership, and organizational design - making emotional dynamics visible and usable for leaders navigating complex systems. With 10+ years of People & Culture leadership (Chief People Officer at GotPhoto, CHRO at UMI/Volkswagen, scaling teams from 10 to 100+), she's seen firsthand how cognitive overwhelm breaks high-performing teams. Currently pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Clinical Psychology at Medizinische Hochschule Brandenburg, she bridges neuroscience with practical organizational application. As a certified Systemic Coach and Counselor, Elisa specializes in translating psychological research into actionable frameworks for tech leaders. Her approach: Emotions are data - and in the AI age, brain health is the ultimate competitive advantage. Currently Senior HR Business Partner at Der Tagesspiegel, she also runs a coaching practice focused on helping professionals build resilience in high-change environments.

speakers.name: Pratik Patel
speakers.title: Lead Developer Relations Engineer/Code Hacker
speakers.bio: Pratik Patel is a Java Champion, developer advocate at Azul Systems and has written 3 books on programming (Java, Cloud and OSS). An all around software and hardware nerd with experience in the healthcare, telecom, financial services, and startup sectors. He's also a co-organizer of the Atlanta Java User Group and North Atlanta JavaScript meetup, conference chairperson for Devnexus, frequent speaker at tech events, and master builder of nachos.

speakers.name: Precious Osaro
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Precious Osaro is a Senior iOS Engineer with 8+ years of experience building secure, scalable, high-performance mobile systems across FoodTech, FinTech, HealthTech, and Security industries. He has led engineering at Carbon and Reliance Health, designing modular architectures, enterprise-grade mobile pipelines, and modern authentication systems, including Passkeys and OPAQUE. Precious specializes in on-device AI, mobile performance engineering, and building intelligent app experiences powered by Apple’s Core ML, Vision, Natural Language, and MLX frameworks. He also mentors engineers globally and speaks about leadership, architecture, and the future of mobile-edge intelligence.

speakers.name: Robert Barron
speakers.title: SRE Architect
speakers.bio: Robert works for IBM, helping clients improve their IT Operations. He is an SRE and AIOps evangelist who enjoys helping others solve problems even more than he enjoys solving them himself. Robert has over 20 years of experience in IT development & operations and is happiest when learning something new. He lives in Israel with his wonderful wife and two children. His hobbies include history, space exploration, and bird photography.

speakers.name: Rustam Mehmandarov
speakers.title: Principal Engineer and Cloud Lead
speakers.bio: Experienced Software Engineer | Architect | Keynote & Public Speaker | Java Champion | Google Developer Expert for Cloud | Docker Captain | Oracle ACE Pro | Community Leader | Mentor | Ex-leader of JavaZone and Norwegian JUG – javaBin.

speakers.name: Sasa Fajkovic
speakers.title: AI and DevEx Lead
speakers.bio: I'm Sasa Fajkovic, a technical leader with a product mindset and over a decade of experience in fintech, banking, and platform engineering. I build and lead teams that make developers faster — cutting CI/CD costs by 60%, boosting pipeline speed by 30%, and integrating AI and LLM tooling into everyday workflows. I've architected banking infrastructure handling cash balances, ledger systems, and financial compliance at scale. I've founded a digital agency, built proprietary platforms from scratch, led PSD2 compliance implementations, and modernized legacy systems across some of Europe's biggest financial institutions. As a former lecturer who taught programming to 300+ people, I bring a natural drive to simplify complexity and elevate the people around me. I write about AI agents, autonomous CI/CD, and developer productivity at sasa-fajkovic.com — always with a "progress over hype" approach.

speakers.name: Satej Sahu
speakers.title: Principal Data Engineer
speakers.bio: Satej works as Principal Data Engineer at Zalando SE with over 14 years of experience in the industry. He has worked with renowned organizations such as Boeing, Adidas, Honeywell specializing in architecture, big data and machine learning use cases. With a strong track record of architecting scalable and efficient systems, Satej has successfully delivered data-driven and ML applied solutions. He's also an author of two programming books named "Building Secure PHP Applications" and "PHP 8 Basics: For Programming and Web Development" with Apress / Springer publications with another one in pipeline.

speakers.name: Sebastian Gingter
speakers.title: Developer-Consultant
speakers.bio: Sebastian Gingter has more than 25 years of experience as a professional software developer, but don't let the grey hairs fool you – he's always looking ahead. As a consultant at Thinktecture, he's got his eyes set on the future, focusing on integrating Generative AI in the business world and diving into modern (web) technologies for both client and server sides with C# and .NET, for which he's been awarded as Microsoft MVP. Since 2008, he's a speaker, sharing his wisdom and enthusiasm at international conferences and in technical articles, all while keeping it fun and engaging.

speakers.name: Shachar Azriel
speakers.title: VP Product
speakers.bio: For the past decade, I’ve helped startups and mid-sized tech companies scale teams, establish systems, and launch products that stick. Today, I’m VP of Product at Baz, where we’re on a mission to reinvent code review with AI: making it faster, smarter, and (believe it or not) more fun for developers. We’re working at the bleeding edge of technology, facing unique challenges that many other product and development teams are only beginning to encounter. That’s why I regularly share real stories from building AI-powered features in the wild, and from the journey of building an AI-driven company itself. Beyond the product, I love connecting with people. I co-founded the AI-Dev community in Israel, dedicated to accelerating innovation in AI coding and product development.

speakers.name: Shramish Kafle
speakers.title: Senior Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Shramish is a Senior Solutions Architect with deep experience in cloud native platforms, Kubernetes operations, Observability, and modern delivery practices.
