His work centers on designing scalable architecture patterns, improving deployment reliability, and guiding engineering teams toward maintainable, production grade solutions. He writes regularly on Medium and hosts a podcast focused on DevOps, SRE, machine learning, and the practical use of LLMs in engineering workflows. He brings a strong foundation from earlier roles, including DevOps engineering at KSB and as a cloud consultant at MHP: A Porsche Company. During his time there, he worked closely with teams at Porsche and Volkswagen on large scale DevOps initiatives, CI and CD pipeline design, cloud transformation programs and migration strategies for complex enterprise systems. These experiences shaped his practical, implementation driven approach to platform and architecture work. He has delivered technical sessions and hands on workshops with Amazon Web Services and Technische Universität Berlin, covering topics such as Kubernetes operations, cloud architecture choices and platform reliability. His speaking style focuses on real lessons, clear explanations and practical insights that engineering teams can apply immediately. His work across industry verticals has given him a grounded perspective on what makes platforms reliable, scalable and efficient to operate. Attendees can expect a session rooted in real experience, thoughtful engineering practice and an honest look at what actually works in modern cloud environments.

speakers.name: Simon Auer
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: With more than 15 years in development and the experience of building and working on over 200 projects for startups and multinational companies, Simon has worked professionally with almost every major modern framework, from React, Angular, Vue, Ember and Svelte to Flutter, and has discovered their pros and cons. This experience is used to teach at multiple universities/fhs and to consult companies on frameworks and solutions for their specific needs, as well as train whole tech teams on them in leading companies in Austria, Germany and Switzerland.

speakers.name: Theresa Heine
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Theresa Heine is a Product Owner with a software engineering background, focused on software quality, test automation, and release processes. Passionate about improving reliability in legacy systems while adapting quality practices for AI-driven development. Known for making complex technical topics understandable and bridging the gap between technical and non-technical stakeholders.

speakers.name: Simon Martinelli
speakers.title: Programming Architect
speakers.bio: Simon Martinelli is a Java Champion, Vaadin Champion, and Oracle ACE Pro, with over three decades of experience as a software architect, developer, consultant, and trainer. As the owner of Martinelli LLC, he specializes in optimizing full-stack development with Java using AI and has a deep focus on modern architectures and software modernization. He frequently shares his expertise by speaking at international conferences, writing articles, and maintaining his blog, Keep IT Simple: https://martinelli.ch. His passion for teaching is reflected in his work as a lecturer at two universities in Switzerland.

speakers.name: Stas Lebedenko
speakers.title: Cloud Architect
speakers.bio: Stas Lebedenko is a Microsoft Azure Most Valuable Professional in AI & Azure categories, full-stack cloud architect and platform engineer at Eficode. He is also a co-organizer of the Odesa Microsoft. NET User Group, a speaker and author, and an active member of the international and Ukraine .NET/Azure/Operations communities. He has been building and designing software for the last twenty years and has been the MVP since 2020.

speakers.name: Stefan Bley
speakers.title: Software Architect
speakers.bio: Stefan is a software architect at ZEISS Digital Innovation in Berlin and has worked on various Angular projects in different industries throughout his career. He loves experimenting with new technologies and sharing his knowledge by speaking at conferences and contributing at community events. Recently, he has been leading an engineering design team focused on leveraging micro frontend architecture for performant healthcare digitization.

speakers.name: Suchitra Swain
speakers.title: Senior Frontend Engineer
speakers.bio: I’m a frontend engineer with over five years of experience in building scalable, user-focused web applications. For the past 2.5 years, I’ve been working at Delivery Hero in Berlin, developing modern, high-performance interfaces using React, Next.js, and TypeScript. My journey includes impactful work with Adevinta (mobile.de) and SUSTLabs – IIT Bombay, where I contributed to projects in blockchain, energy informatics, and e-commerce. I’ve published research with ACM e-Energy and explored AR-based ideation tools at IDC School of Design, IIT Bombay. I’m passionate about creating clean, accessible interfaces and integrating design with sustainability and innovation. I’ve attended Google I/O Connect in 2024 and 2025, staying connected to the evolving developer ecosystem and the latest in web technologies.

speakers.name: Szymon Chmal
speakers.title: React Native Expert
speakers.bio: Creator of Rozenite, Harness and Voltra. The kind of person who dives into code and somehow makes the "impossible" happen. Focused on building the next generation of developer tools so you can ship your apps even faster.

speakers.name: Ewa Zachel
speakers.title: People Partnering Manager
speakers.bio: With 15 years of experience in HR, I have worked across global organizations, supporting leadership development and driving organizational growth through culture and engagement. Over the past 8 years at CD PROJEKT RED, I have been part of an amazing HR team, progressing from HR Partner to L&D Manager and now People Partnering Manager, working at the intersection of business, people, and creativity to build resilient and future-ready teams. Outside of work, I’m a mom to my ‘daughter-unicorn’ and I’m passionate about a healthy lifestyle and inspiring others to live well.

speakers.name: Thijs Feryn
speakers.title: Technical Evangelist
speakers.bio: As the Technical Evangelist at Varnish Software, Thijs Feryn focuses on web performance, software scalability, and content delivery. He demonstrates content-driven and technical messaging through presentations, videos, books, blog posts, social media posts, podcasts, and other media. Thijs is a published author and wrote Getting Started with Varnish Cache and Varnish 6 by Example. As a public speaker, he has a track record of over 340 presentations in 23 different countries, where he is often praised for his energetic and engaging presentation style. As an evangelist, Thijs is also active in many open-source communities, most notably the Varnish and PHP community. He has contributed to various communities for over 15 years both technically and as an organizer and facilitator. Prior to joining Varnish Software, Thijs Feryn spent 15 years in the web hosting industry, tackling web performance and scalability issues on a daily basis and evangelizing these topics.

speakers.name: Thomas Endres
speakers.title: Managing Partner
speakers.bio: Thomas Endres is a Managing Partner for TNG Technology Consulting in Munich. Besides his normal work for the company and the management of the customers he is creating various prototypes, he and the the Innovation Hacking Team develop various prototypes, including an Augmented Reality application that displays the world from an artist's perspective, real-time deepfakes, or an AI for generating presentations. He works on applications in the field of AR/VR, AI, and gesture control to e.g. autonomously fly quadcopters or control them without physical contact. Furthermore, he is involved in various Open Source projects. Thomas studied computer science at TU Munich and is a passionate software developer. As an Intel Software Innovator and Black Belt, he presents new technologies such as AI, AR/VR, and robotics worldwide. For this, he has received awards including a JavaOne Rockstar Award and several Best Speaker Awards.

speakers.name: Thomas Froment
speakers.title: Program Manager Development Tools
speakers.bio: Thomas Froment is the Program Manager for Development Tools at the Eclipse Foundation, where he oversees all tooling projects, including Eclipse Theia and Theia AI, as part of the Cloud DevTools Working Group. He also leads the Open VSX and Eclipse IDE working groups.

speakers.name: Thomas Hugle
speakers.title: Senior Consultant
speakers.bio: Thomas Hugle is a Senior Consultant with TNG Technology Consulting, with a focus on AI and frontend development. His PhD thesis at the Max-Planck-Institut für Kernphysik in Heidelberg was mostly about data analysis for the CONUS neutrino experiment and computer simulations of physical systems. Besides his work as a software consultant, he handles data mining projects, to apply his data analysis skills to the Spotify data of his colleagues, and gives talks about how to turn from a physicist into a software developer.

speakers.name: Thomas Saueressig
speakers.title: CCO and Member of the Executive Board
speakers.bio: Thomas Saueressig is a Member of the Executive Board and Chief Customer Officer of SAP SE. His board area, the Customer Value Group (CVG), brings together SAP’s go-to-market and delivery functions to help customers effectively drive their business transformation, fully leverage the benefits of AI, and build long-term customer value in the cloud. Saueressig has a strong track record of delivering customer value. In his previous role, he led the Executive Board area Customer Services & Delivery, where he worked closely with customers to achieve business outcomes through innovation and end-to-end business transformation. Before being appointed the head of SAP Product Engineering, Saueressig served as Chief Information Officer (CIO) and Global Head of IT Services at SAP. As CIO, he was responsible for SAP’s IT strategy and its execution with a cloud-first approach. Saueressig joined SAP in 2004 in consulting and subsequently held various international management positions with increasing responsibility. Saueressig holds a degree in Business Informatics from the Baden-Wuerttemberg Cooperative State University in Mannheim and a joint Executive MBA from ESSEC Business School (France) and Mannheim Business School (Germany). Since April 2022, he has been a member of the Board of Directors of Nokia and serves as vice-chairman.

speakers.name: Thomas Weinschenk
speakers.title: Software Developer
speakers.bio: Thomas Weinschenk is an integration specialist in the process industry and Co‑Lead of the Open Edge Computing (OEC) workgroup at the Open Industry 4.0 Alliance. He focuses on vendor‑neutral edge foundations—making applications and devices discoverable, operable, and interoperable in control‑cabinet environments. Thomas bridges OT and IT with practical patterns for onboarding, app‑to‑app communication, identity/semantics, and reliable operations under intermittent networks. He speaks from hands‑on experience: turning proof‑of‑concepts into repeatable, multi‑vendor deployments without lock‑in, and enabling teams to unlock process data responsibly. As a developer at Endress+Hauser, Thomas values open standards and reproducible engineering over tool marketing. His talks deliver guardrails, checklists, and field‑tested lessons audiences can apply right away.

speakers.name: Chris Redmond
speakers.title: Founder
speakers.bio: Chris is a trained Process Communication Model (PCM) practitioner and will be certified as a PCM coach by June 2026. With over 15 years of experience in talent acquisition, he has helped scale teams across startups and high-growth environments. Today, he works as a fractional Head of Talent Acquisition and leadership mentor, supporting recruiters, founders and managers in building high-performance teams. His journey into leadership began the way it does for many, promoted for strong individual performance, but without formal training on how to actually lead people. That experience led him to PCM, where he discovered a practical, psychology-based framework for understanding how people communicate, what motivates them, and how to spot early signs of stress. Now, he brings those insights to fast-paced teams, helping leaders build not just headcount, but trust, motivation, and sustainable performance.

speakers.name: Tilman Mürle
speakers.title: Managing Director and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Tilman Mürle is Managing Director and Co-Founder of Komplyzen, where he helps organizations turn AI governance from a compliance exercise into an operational capability. He brings over a decade of leadership experience across SaaS, enterprise IT, and regulated environments, including his role as former CEO EMEA at Valiantys. Tilman works with engineering, risk, and leadership teams to design AI systems that are measurable, auditable, and production-ready by design. His focus lies on operational AI governance, AI risk management, and translating regulatory expectations into practical architecture and workflows that scale. He regularly speaks at industry conferences on AI risk, governance, and the realities of bringing AI safely into production.

speakers.name: Manjuri Sinha
speakers.title: VP HR
speakers.bio: Recognised as Global Top 200 Biggest Voices in Leadership alongside names such as Simon Sinek and Marshal Goldsmith by Leaders Hum, Match.io etc. - AI Advisory Board Member for Goodtime.io - Keynote Speaker, Author and StartUp advisor. -Mastermind of Global Talent Dynamics with a proven prowess of pivoting orgs to success by building a culture of high performance and engagement. - Rich International experience in Talent, TA, TD, TM, HR Business Partnering and HR Transformation!! -Leadership Experience, built & nutured high performing HR / Talent teams of 40+ to 140+ globally.

speakers.name: Tim Schade
speakers.title: Software Architect
speakers.bio: Tim Schade is Tech Office Lead, software architect, and software engineer at GFT. With more than 18 years of experience, he designs and delivers complex, scalable software solutions with a strong focus on JVM-based architectures. His areas of expertise include cloud technologies, DevOps, artificial intelligence, and IT security. In addition, he is a recognized expert, speaker, and researcher in sustainable software engineering and green coding, focusing on the development of energy-efficient and low-carbon software systems. In his talks, Tim combines extensive hands-on experience with modern technologies and demonstrates how sustainable software architecture can be successfully applied in real-world projects.

speakers.name: Tom Crecelius
speakers.title: Technology Evangelist
speakers.bio: Who am I? - 10+ years in Software Architecture - 15+ years in .NET development - 20+ years in Software Development - Diploma (M.Sc.) and PhD in Computer Science - with a strong academic research background in the area of big data, information retrieval, search, and distributed systems - applying broad technical knowledge with strategic guidance to develop innovative software solutions - combining advanced technology and efficient algorithms with real business cases - cloud transformation and cloud-native development - lead of multiple development teams - special interest in Software Analysis and Diagnostics, especially in .NET - always eager to learn and share knowledge - Coffee averse, Earl Grey addicted Speaking & Sharing - speaker at company-wide tech events - speaker at company-wide live webinars - creator and speaker in video tutorials - actively supporting teams with software architecture, software analysis and diagnostics, and cloud-native issues For early publications & public speaker presence (PhD related), see e.g.: https://dl.acm.org/profile/81342492383 See also: - https://cid.com/author/tomcrecelius/ - my latest #techTalk @Tom Crecelius on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/tom-crecelius/recent-activity/all/

speakers.name: Tomasz Woznica
speakers.title: IT expert
speakers.bio: 20+ years’ experience in developing .NET. I have experience in designing solution architecture, technically managing development team and deploying software to production environments. The idea is matters, language is just a tool - not afraid to touch explore Python, Node.js, COBOL, JCL, etc. Designing and implementing large-scalable online systems in C#.

speakers.name: Marta Wilmanska-Filec
speakers.title: People Partnering Director
speakers.bio: Global People Partnering Director at CD PROJEKT RED, with 20 years of hands-on experience in HR/People Function. She focuses on helping organizations adapt to future challenges by driving initiatives around engagement, leadership development, future-ready skills, talent strategies, and evolving the way people work.

speakers.name: Tomek Porożyński
speakers.title: Staff ML Engineer
speakers.bio: Experienced AI/ML Engineer and Cloud Architect, specializing in Google Cloud and Generative AI. Distinguished as a Google Developer Expert. He manages a YouTube channel, and runs a technology blog dedicated to promoting Cloud Native development. His projects often involve the integration of various artificial intelligence domains. As a sought-after speaker, he delivers lectures and training on Public Cloud and AI, sharing his extensive knowledge and experience with a wide audience.

speakers.name: Torsten Stiller
speakers.title: Head of Automation Factory
speakers.bio: Torsten Stiller is a seasoned tech leader and speaker, currently heading the Automation Factory Domain at E.ON Digital Technology. A former Microsoft and NVIDIA professional, he brings deep experience in cloud-native platforms, automation, and digital transformation. Torsten is passionate about the responsible use of AI and sustainable software engineering, combining hands-on expertise with strategic vision to drive ethical, impactful innovation.

speakers.name: Tushar Gupta
speakers.title: Engineering Lead
speakers.bio: I am a senior IT professional with extensive experience in the e-commerce domain, specialising in solution architecture and team leadership. I focus on building scalable, high-performance systems, migrating from legacy platforms, and driving innovation in competitive markets.

speakers.name: Utz Bacher
speakers.title: Vice President Software
speakers.bio: Utz Bacher is Vice President Software at Q.ANT, where he leads the development of the software ecosystem for Q.ANT’s photonic processors and drives the exploitation of photonic computing technology. After his studies in Information Technology, Utz spent more than 25 years in various business units at IBM. He started as a Linux kernel developer for high speed I/O and networking code, before leading the Linux on Cell/B.E. development team. After designing the hypervisor management for the hybrid mainframe, he became the lead architect for Linux on IBM Z. Utz also drove adoption of container technology and the concept of confidential computing on the platform and was appointed Senior Technical Staff Member. Later, he moved into IBM’s services organization, shaping Cloud solutions and their delivery for client projects. In 2021, Utz joined IBM Quantum where he was the responsible architect for bringing IBM's quantum capabilities to the Cloud. He also designed the integration of client IT environments and HPC clusters from scientific compute centers with IBM Quantum. Utz joined Q.ANT in 2026.

speakers.name: Uwe Korn
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Uwe Korn is a CTO at the EconAI company QuantCo. His expertise is in building scalable architectures for machine learning services and the teams & culture around them. Nowadays, he focuses on the software engineering infrastructure needed to provide the building blocks for bringing ML/AI projects into production. As part of his work, he became a core committer to the Apache Parquet, Apache Arrow and conda-forge projects.

speakers.name: Vadzim Prudnikau
speakers.title: Architect
speakers.bio: Hands-on Architect, IT Trainer, and Conference Speaker with 20+ years of experience, passionate about building high-quality software, mentoring developers, and sharing knowledge. I've trained over 550 professionals in Architecture, EventStorming, and TDD, built and deployed more than 10 production-ready projects, led multiple development teams, and created a successful Java department from scratch. Along the way, I’ve mentored 20+ developers and conducted 100+ technical interviews. My expertise lies in Java, but I also have experience with Kotlin, Groovy, and other programming languages. I believe that strong technical skills, continuous learning, teamwork, and knowledge-sharing are the keys to success in software development.

speakers.name: Anastasia Pshegodskaya
speakers.title: Director of Talent Acquisition
speakers.bio: Anastasia Pshegodskaya is Director of Talent Acquisition at Remote, one of the most fast-paced unicorn companies in the world. Before this, she built and led the global sourcing team at GitLab, while previously driving recruiting initiatives at Fortune 500 companies Uber and Dell. Anastasia is extremely passionate about people, and how technology can bring positive change for them — it drives her recruiting career and life.

speakers.name: Veit Heller
speakers.title: Technologist at Large
speakers.bio: I’m a technologist at heart, though life often leads me into managerial roles. For the last 15 years, I’ve built products, led and advised companies, published papers, and shared my insights at conferences and in articles on the web.

speakers.name: Vitaly Bragilevsky
speakers.title: Head of Rust Ecosystem
speakers.bio: Vitaly is the Head of Rust Ecosystem at JetBrains. He is passionate about programming languages (all of them!) and works closely with Rust, and other projects at JetBrains. As the author of "Haskell in Depth", he firmly believes that writing code in a functional style and avoiding mutation whenever possible leads to better outcomes.

speakers.name: Vitaly Friedman
speakers.title: UX Lead
speakers.bio: Vitaly loves beautiful content and does not give up easily. Born in Minsk, Belarus, he studied computer science and mathematics in Germany. While writing algebra proofs and preparing for software engineering at nights in the kitchen, at the same time he discovered passion for typography, interface design and writing. After working as a freelance designer and developer for 6 years, he co-founded Smashing Magazine back in 2006, a leading online magazine for designers and developers. His curiosity drove him from interface design to front-end to performance optimization to accessibility and back to user experience over all the years. Vitaly is the author, co-author and editor of Smashing Books (https://www.smashingmagazine.com/books), and a curator of Smashing Conferences (https://www.smashingconf.com). He is the UX lead with the European Parliament and Smashing Magazine (www.smashingmagazine.com) and front-end/UX consultant in Europe and abroad, working with large and small companies and organizations like Haufe-Lexware, Axel-Springer and others. He also runs Measure UX (https://measure-ux.com) and Smart Interface Design Patterns (https://smart-interface-design-patterns.com), friendly video courses on UX and design patterns, along with a live UX training for passionate UX and product designers.

speakers.name: Wekoslav Stefanovski
speakers.title: Head Of Development
speakers.bio: Wekoslav Stefanovski has about two decade of professional developer experience using a variety of development technologies. Has been using C# since the first public beta, and has a long and fruitful love relationship with it. Has been using JavaScript since the previous millennium and has a long and fruitful love/hate relationship with it. Currently, works at Sourcico as Head of development. He is passionate about functional programming, static code analysis, compiler design and code quality metrics.

speakers.name: Wojtek Kurzawiński
speakers.title: IT Senior Engineer
speakers.bio: Over 20 years experience in software engenering During these years I develop standalone applications, mobile applications for Android, IoT and much more For the last 12 years at mbank on the mainframe to x86 migration project where I designing solution architecture and technically managing development team I boldly go where I haven't been before - not afraid to explore strange programming language

speakers.name: Mahmoud Aly
speakers.title: TA Business Partner
speakers.bio: Mahmoud is a Talent Acquisition expert with over a decade of experience recruiting for some of the world's most recognizable brands, including Nike, Semrush, Samsung, and PepsiCo. Throughout his career, Mahmoud has taken on several Talent Acquisition roles, from Tech Recruiting, Talent Sourcing, Executive hiring, to Talent Ops. He currently leads global Go-To-Market Recruitment at Semrush, and previously led the Data & AI business at Nike. He's also the Founder of Egypt Recruiting Community, the first and largest network in Egypt for the People & Culture space. Mahmoud is an international keynote speaker who has delivered keynotes at The Sourcing Summit, HR Leaders Summit, and The Recruitment Conference, among others.

speakers.name: Kent Frederiksen
speakers.title: Vice President Reward
speakers.bio: Kent Hviid Frederiksen. VP Rewards at the LEGO Group Kent is currently Vice President, Reward at The LEGO Group and brings over two decades of global experience in Reward having navigated the complexities of multinational corporations. Throughout his career, his focus has been on creating awesome value propositions aligned with company strategy, purpose and values and adaptation to the evolving employee and diverse employment landscape. Responsibilities include Well-Being, hybrid working, Mobility, Executive Reward, Business Travel, Performance Management, STI/LTIP and overall ability to attract & retain talent.

speakers.name: Damandeep Kochhar
speakers.title: EVP and CPTO
speakers.bio: Damandeep Kochhar is EVP, Chief Platform & Technology Officer at HERE Technologies. In his role, he leads the company’s core engineering activities, including the development and operation of our highly automated mapping system for creating, updating and publishing map content.

speakers.name: Wouter Ligtenberg
speakers.title: Director of Engineering
speakers.bio: Wouter is particularly passionate about CICD and everything that involves automation in the tech industry. In the past years he worked in different countries on projects that involve automating Kubernetes workloads while at the same time speeding up and standardizing the landscape in ING. In his free time you can occasionally find him on a splitboard touring the Romanian mountains

speakers.name: Andreas Klinger
speakers.title: Founder
speakers.bio: Andreas Klinger is the founder of PROTOTYPE, an early-stage VC fund investing in European frontier tech. As former CTO and engineer at Product Hunt and AngelList, amongst others, he backs radical founders with crazy ideas as early as possible. Andreas is convinced Europe can be the best place on earth to build hardware. He is building a community of hyper-ambitious European builders with his fast-growing YouTube channel and is engineering the ecosystem with hyper-ambitious projects like the policy movement EU-INC, the robotics club network ESRA and a few others.

speakers.name: Frederik Gregaard
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: As CEO of the Cardano Foundation, Frederik Gregaard brings over two decades of leadership experience, including his tenure at PwC, where he spearheaded transformative initiatives in digital innovation. Under his guidance, the Foundation has become a catalyst for blockchain adoption, driving impactful solutions in decentralized governance and sustainability. Frederik’s leadership emphasizes transparency, regulatory alignment, and fostering global collaboration to build a more inclusive and decentralized future.

speakers.name: Benjamin Smith
speakers.title: Senior Staff Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: A Developer Relations leader with 15+ years of experience driving global adoption of cloud and fintech platforms. With roles at AWS, Stripe, and Broadcom, I’ve built scalable developer engagement engines, shaped compelling product narratives, and launched hands-on programs that turn complex technology into real business impact. Known for high-energy keynotes, strategic program design, and cross-functional leadership, I bridge developers, product teams, and executives to accelerate innovation and deliver results.

speakers.name: Kamen Petroff
speakers.title: Software Developer
speakers.bio: Software Developer at cycos AG (an Atos company) since 2001, specializing in real-time media streaming, web conferencing, and IVR solutions. Shifted focus to Applied AI in 2017, working on research projects in the fields of Natural Language Processing and Deep Learning. Creator of `kpe/bert-for-tf2`, one of the pioneering implementations of the Transformer architecture for TensorFlow 2, dedicated to bridging the gap between cutting-edge AI research and practical software engineering.

speakers.name: Marcus Wermuth
speakers.title: VP of R&D
speakers.bio: Marcus Wermuth is VP of R&D at Safety, building tools to give security teams visibility into developer workstations. Previously, he held engineering leadership roles at Remote and Buffer, two of the most recognized distributed companies globally. With 15 years of engineering experience, Marcus writes about technology leadership, mentors engineering leaders, and facilitates international workshops on distributed team management.

speakers.name: Sebastian Messingfeld
speakers.title: Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: Sebastian is a Staff Engineer at Eurowings Digital GmbH, building end-to-end solutions across the Eurowings website and mobile app. His current focus is the information experience on eurowings.com — including AI-powered chatbots, on-site search, and smarter contact flows — where he helps teams turn AI features into reliable, production-ready capabilities.

speakers.name: Ash Jones
speakers.title: Founder
speakers.bio: Ash Jones is the Founder of Great Influence, where they act as a Strategic Partner to global brands helping them activate their senior leadership teams on social media to support external comms, employer brand and company culture. Ash holds a decade of experience in helping CEOs and Entrepreneurs build their personal brands including Steven Bartlett of Diary Of A CEO fame, ex-Manchester United footballer Gary Neville, best-selling authors Matthew Syed and John Amaechi and senior leaders at the likes of Booking.com and Santander.

speakers.name: Barbara Wilk
speakers.title: Global Head Talent Acquisition
speakers.bio: I help tech companies built at scale and expand their operations internationally. Seasoned Talent Acquisition and people leader, experienced working with fast growing early stage, late stage, IPO-bound and PE-backed tech organizations.

speakers.name: Daniel Schniepp
speakers.title: Chief Architect
speakers.bio: Daniel is the Chief Architect for Developer and Cloud Infrastructure Platforms at Mercedes-Benz Group AG. In recent years, he has worked and led platform and infrastructure topics and enabled product teams to have an easily accessible runtime environment. Besides that, he invests a lot in cultural topics, like engineering development programs, standardised engineering hiring processes and on community building efforts. Additionally, he has multiple years of experience designing and developing backend and frontend applications across different technology stacks. Before joining the corporate world, he co-founded two startups and worked mostly in the financial services sector as a software engineer.

speakers.name: Hazel Wat
speakers.title: Analyst and Consultant
speakers.bio: Hazel is an experienced Analyst & Consultant with a strong track record of bridging technology, data and business needs. Currently with Schwarz Gruppe - Schwarz IT , an in-house technology powerhouse for Lidl & Kaufland supermarkets. Born & raised in Hong Kong, studied & lived in USA for 15 years; currently residing in Germany for 11 years. Lived and worked globally, bringing a multicultural perspective to engineering soft skills and team dynamics. Hazel is passionate about improving team communication and performance, bridging the communication gap between technical and business colleagues.

speakers.name: Robindro Ullah
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Robindro Ullah is partner and managing director of the leading Talent Intelligence Company Trendence and has been a consultant, author, speaker and podcaster for many years. He is an expert in the fields of employer branding, HR marketing, recruitment and data-driven HR. Previously, he was head of HR marketing and Recruitment South at Deutsche Bahn and then head of global employer branding, recruitment and HR communication at Voith, a technology group based in Heidenheim, Germany.

speakers.name: Bianca Errigo
speakers.title: Founder and CEO
speakers.bio: Bianca Errigo is a wellness expert, global speaker, and founder of HumanOS, a pioneering company integrating health and performance into the workplace. With a background in psychology and over 12,000 coaching hours, she blends science with real-world insight. Once hospitalised from corporate burnout, Bianca now partners with companies such as American Express, Cisco, Snapchat and The University of Cambridge to support individuals and organisations on their journeys of sustainable high performance.

speakers.name: Bastian Eichler
speakers.title: VP Product / Marketing
speakers.bio: With over a decade of experience in B2B leadership, Bastian brings a wealth of insights from both the traditional corporate world and the dynamic startup scene. As VP Product/Marketing at WorkMotion, he is at the forefront of shaping the "Future of Work." Bastian's passion for remote work has grown during the pandemic, leading him to develop expertise in leading decentralized, global, and intercultural teams. His experience and insights make him a valuable speaker on the ever-evolving landscape of work.

speakers.name: Bill Boorman
speakers.title: Strategic Advisor
speakers.bio: Bill Boorman has spent his working life in around recruiting, starting out at the age of 16 in what he saw at the time as a short term gig as a trainee recruiter. He has worked as a third party recruiter, run and owned recruiting firms, been HRD and Trainer for international business, helped some of the worlds largest brands evolve the way people get hired through the age of digital and social media. Boorman advises tech companies and investors on the technology needed for the modern day. He is on the board of tech brands Vonq, PlacedApp and StaffCircle. Boorman has won numerous awards including Recruiter Life Time Achievement, and was recently listed in HR Techs Top 100 influencers. Away from work he is a Dad, Husband, Street Artist and Stand Up Comedian. He is an in demand speaker known for sharing realities to challenge the people people.

speakers.name: Christian Wastlhuber
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: Actually, I'm an Egyptologist and should (probably) be looking for mummies. However, I've preferred to use the last 16 years to find people who are still alive. I started out as a sourcer in a recruitment consultancy, worked for a long time in tech start-ups and scale-ups both as a hands-on recruiter as well as in leadership roles and founded my own company, The UnleashPeople, together with a business partner at the beginning of 2024. My focus is on data-driven recruiting processes and strategies, employer branding and a bit of workforce planning. For me, all of this is always under the banner of an excellent candidate experience.

speakers.name: Claudia Tattanelli
speakers.title: Founder and CEO
speakers.bio: Claudia Tattanelli is the Founder and CEO of C-YourCulture and the Chairwoman of the global think tank Future Talent Council. Formerly, Claudia was the Chairwoman of the Universum Strategic Board, and for over 25 years she has led many culture and employer branding strategy projects, supporting the leadership teams of many of the world's leading companies, including Accenture, Alexander McQueen, Apple, AB InBev, Audi, Barclays, Barilla, Campari, Citibank, Coca-Cola, Coty, Exxon, EY, Facebook, FedEx, H&M, Goldman Sachs, Google, IBM, JPMorgan, Luxottica, Lamborghini, L’Oréal, Mondelēz, Lenovo, P&G, SAP, Siemens, the United Nations, Unilever, YSL, and Zalando. Claudia is a regular keynote speaker at conferences and events worldwide and has been quoted in many leading newspapers, journals, and podcasts, including Businessweek, CNN, The New York Times, Financial Times, Forbes, Fortune, The Washington Post, Il Sole 24 Ore, Corriere della Sera, and The Economist. Claudia has also been a guest on Bloomberg, Fox, and CNBC’s Morning Call, as well as many other media outlets.

speakers.name: Daniel Mühlbauer
speakers.title: Speaker and Impactor
speakers.bio: Dr. Daniel Mühlbauer is an expert in HR-IT solutions and focuses on the meaningful use of modern technologies in personnel management within a global corporation. He shares his knowledge on his blog and YouTube channel called “HR Datenliebe" and as co-host of the HRDataDudes-Podcast. His passion lies in the combination of data analytics, artificial intelligence, and HR tech to pave the way for the future of personnel management. After completing his PhD at the Institute for Human Resource Management at LMU Munich, he co-founded a startup specializing in People Analytics.

speakers.name: Hung Lee
speakers.title: Curator
speakers.bio: I am a recruitment industry professional with over 15 years experience as an agency recruiter, Recruitment manager, Internal Head of Talent, recruitment trainer, founder of award winning online recruiting platform WorkShape and now Editor and Community builder at Recruiting Brainfood - the best weekly newsletter in recruitment.

speakers.name: Jessie Schofer
speakers.title: Founder
speakers.bio: Jessie is the Founder of Stakkd: the go to place to discover HR tech. A strategic leader with 13+ years of unique experience in both corporates and startups. She is known for her unconventional approach to People topics and is passionate about HR tech and leveraging the benefits to build the future of work.

speakers.name: Jürgen Hartwig
speakers.title: CHRO
speakers.bio: Jürgen Hartwig has been a member of the Board of Management of Daimler Truck Holding AG since December 2021 and is responsible for the Human Resources division. Since June 2022, he has also served as Labor Director of Daimler Truck Holding AG, and since 2019 he has been a member of the Board of Management and Labor Director of Daimler Truck AG. Before taking over the leadership of the Human Resources department for Daimler Trucks within Daimler AG, Jürgen Hartwig spent three years in Beijing as Director Human Resources Greater China. Prior to that, he held various positions in the areas of Human Resources, Corporate Organization & Change Management, as well as Process Consulting & Organization. He began his professional career in 1993 at the former Daimler-Benz AG in Gaggenau in the field of Human Resources & Organizational Development, after completing his studies in Business Education at the University of Mannheim.

speakers.name: Kerstin Wagner
speakers.title: EVP Talent Acquisition
speakers.bio: At Deutsche Bahn AG, Kerstin Wagner serves as Executive Vice President of Talent Acquisition, reporting directly to the Chief Human Resources Officer. In this role, she consolidates recruiting, employer branding, global governance, and temporary employment under one roof. Her primary objective is to position DB as a top employer and attract talent to the DB Group. Working with her team, she oversees the recruitment of a workforce equivalent to the size of a small town every year, while ensuring the talent acquisition process remains innovative and modern. Kerstin Wagner is a member of several supervisory boards and chairs the Advisory Board of the Deutsche Bahn Foundation. Additionally, she is a dedicated advocate for New Work, technology, and diversity. In 2021, she was named one of the "100 Women of the Year" by Focus magazine, and in 2024, Personalmagazin recognized her as one of the 50 most influential HR thought leaders.

speakers.name: Meghan Hennessy
speakers.title: Global People Executive
speakers.bio: Meghan is a results-driven leader, passionate about people, culture, and team development and empowerment driving business success. She has experience with some of the world’s best known companies and largest brands - from Uber to Apple - leading internationally distributed teams across talent and HR. She strongly values the creation of people-first culture, and puts vision and focus on operationalising for scale and people development, having developed high-performing, global organisations.

speakers.name: Renita Käsper
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: With 20+ years in Global Talent Acquisition and Employer Branding, I have built high-performing teams across industries and led global remote teams for 6+ years. Passionate about community building, I’ve founded several HR and recruitment groups to foster collaboration and growth. I’ve also served as a jury member for employer branding competitions. As an international speaker and panelist, I share insights on recruitment, sourcing, employer branding, and the future of work, helping organizations attract and retain top talent while strengthening their ability to lead remote teams effectively.

speakers.name: Ute Neher
speakers.title: Principal Talent Intelligence
speakers.bio: Ute Neher is Principal Talent Intelligence at Indeed, driving innovation at the intersection of data, inclusion, and HR strategy. She empowers leaders to reimagine hiring through skill-based approaches and responsible AI. With a strong voice in the HR community and as a Queb board member, Ute challenges the status quo and champions better work for all—globally and locally.

speakers.name: Gonzalo Ortiz Jaureguizar
speakers.title: Performance Engineer
speakers.bio: I am a software engineer specialized in developing databases in Java. I love understanding how libraries and frameworks work under the hood and to design and implement high-performance systems. I have worked on prototypes such as ToroDB, the first Spanish database unicorn Devo and since 2022 I'm working at StarTree as an Apache Pinot contributor.

speakers.name: Guillaume Moigneu
speakers.title: Field CTO
speakers.bio: I'm G/, Field CTO at Upsun, a cloud applications platform dedicated to simplifying application deployment and operations. With a focus on AI for DevEx and Sustainability, I strive to make running apps seamless for every developer.

speakers.name: Jan Mensch
speakers.title: Software Developer
speakers.bio: I am a Software Developer working at ClickHouse. My work touches databases, distributed systems, networking, and cloud infrastructure. In my free time I enjoy learning Spanish, cooking, and windsurfing.

speakers.name: Robert Lehmann
speakers.title: Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: Robert has spent the past decade with Google SRE, building the tools that a majority of Google's incident response uses for production monitoring. In a previous life, he was a Python aficionado and otherwise enjoys teaching.

speakers.name: Simone Desantis
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: For years in internet technologies, he has gained experience in the design, development and maintenance of reliable and stable IT infrastructures. Naturally brought to problem solving, he is passionate about complex systems, starting from their comprehension, through the identification of critical issues, up to their optimization and improvement. Currently involved in the design and development of a new high performance all purpose API infrastructure.

speakers.name: Andrea Della Porta
speakers.title: Mobile Manager
speakers.bio: Born in Rome in 1994, passionate about mobile development since forever. Andrea has been a technology enthusiast for as long as he can remember. He started developing his first Java programs in his early high school years, but with the advent of smartphones, he quickly fell in love with the mobile world. His experience started in Switzerland for an Android project in the medical field and continued for large companies such as NTT Data and Deloitte, until he got to his current Capgemini. Over the years he has developed applications with large user bases for various industries, specializing in native Android development, Flutter and now Kotlin Multiplatform. His life is not only programming, but also football, padel and an obsession with Lego!

speakers.name: Jaipal Singh
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Jaipal Singh is CTO at Prem AI, where he leads the development of Sovereign AI solutions. Prem is focused on allowing enterprises to access frontier intelligence in a privacy-preserving manner. Prior to Prem, he led AI teams at Staple and EY, building large-scale document processing systems and low-code ML deployment platforms. A researcher and engineer at heart, Jaipal has published at The Web Conference (WWW 2019) on multimodal fake news detection and at EAMT 2020 on multilingual sentiment analysis, work that grew out of his thesis on cross-lingual event extraction at IIIT Hyderabad. At Prem, he's focused on making private AI practical - proving that the most powerful AI is the one you own.

speakers.name: Nicolò Robba
speakers.title: Chief Product Officer
speakers.bio: Nicolò brings international experience in product strategy and leadership to Prima. He began his career at Rocket Internet before moving to Lazada and later Alibaba, where he helped shape product strategy across six Southeast Asian markets. In 2018, he co-founded Lifepal, building it into Indonesia’s leading digital insurance marketplace with over one million policies issued. He then led digital transformation at Oona Insurance and served as SVP Product at Shipper, driving major efficiency gains while sustaining rapid growth. Nicolò holds a Master’s degree from ESADE Business School.

speakers.name: Nikhil Gandhi
speakers.title: SVP of Engineering
speakers.bio: Nikhil Gandhi is the Senior Vice President of Engineering for Yahoo Mail, where he oversees global engineering, product, and design for one of the world’s largest consumer communication platforms. He oversees a global team, leading technical strategy and execution across core messaging infrastructure, AI-driven experiences, spam and safety systems, and platform modernization, while driving user growth, engagement, and monetization. His organization also supports critical priorities including privacy, data governance, security, and global infrastructure at scale. Prior to Yahoo, Nikhil was Head of Engineering for Ads Platform at TikTok, responsible for monetization systems powering a significant share of revenue, including creator tools and in-app commerce. He previously held leadership roles at Meta, scaling the Ads Experience organization globally, and spent a decade at Apple helping grow the Apple Online Store into a multi–tens of billions business while leading global engineering for Apple.com. A deeply technical leader, he combines strong product and platform instincts with a track record of building high-performing global teams and delivering complex systems at scale.

speakers.name: Seth Webster
speakers.title: Chief Developer Evangelist
speakers.bio: Seth Webster is a technology executive, software architect, and open source leader serving as Executive Director of the React Foundation and Chief Developer Evangelist at Expo. Formerly the head of React at Meta, he has spent more than 20 years leading engineering organizations and shaping developer platforms used by millions worldwide. Seth is passionate about AI, developer experience, and helping people bring their ideas to life through technology.

speakers.name: Ana Maria Mihalceanu
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Ana is a Java Champion Alumni, Senior Developer Advocate for the Java Platform Group at Oracle, with an extensive background in Java and cloud development, while contributing to Java community growth and events. Her exposure to varied technical challenges involving Java-based frameworks has endowed her with deep insights into the evolution of the JDK tools, practical use, and advanced features. To learn more about/from her, follow her on Twitter @ammbra1508.

speakers.name: Andrei Nutas
speakers.title: Technical Test Architect
speakers.bio: Dr. Andrei Nutas is a seasoned Test Architect and QA Team Lead with over 12 years of professional experience in which he designed, implemented, and supervised the development of automation solutions and transformed the quality process of various clients. In addition, he is an experienced speaker for both academic and business conferences. He holds a PhD in AI Ethics and is currently completing an Executive Master in Digital Transformation at ESSEC Business School.

speakers.name: Peter Caron
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Peter Caron is CEO of 3T Software Labs, the team behind Studio 3T, the leading productivity platform for MongoDB teams. With deep experience spanning software engineering, data platforms, and enterprise technology, Peter works closely with global organisations to help them scale MongoDB securely, efficiently, and with confidence. He is a vocal advocate for practical, responsible technology adoption, cutting through hype to focus on real-world outcomes, strong data foundations, and sustainable innovation.

speakers.name: Anshul Jindal
speakers.title: Senior Solution Architect
speakers.bio: Anshul is a Sr. Solution Architect at NVIDIA's DGX Cloud team, he specializes in assisting customers with deploying their workloads at scale. Anshul has a strong background in SRE and has extensive experience in managing production-grade Kubernetes clusters across various Cloud Service Providers (CSPs). He has received Ph.D. in computer science from TU Munich, graduating summa cum laude.

speakers.name: Mohak Chadha
speakers.title: Solution Architect
speakers.bio: Mohak Chadha is a Solutions Architect at NVIDIA, based in Munich, where he focuses on designing solutions for enterprise AI applications. Before this, he was at Firebolt, a unicorn startup, where he managed their cloud infrastructure and worked on improving security. With a background in distributed systems, Mohak specializes in cloud computing, parallel computing, and high-performance computing. He shares his expertise in distributed systems by presenting at major developer conferences, including KubeCon + CloudNativeCon and the Open Source Summit.

speakers.name: Cansu Kavili Örnek
speakers.title: Principal Architect
speakers.bio: AI Platform Architect, bridging DevOps & MLOps to build scalable AI platforms. Passionate about open source, platform engineering, and driving AI adoption. Focused on delivering real customer impact by aligning architectures with business outcomes.

speakers.name: Erica Weistrand
speakers.title: Developer Lead
speakers.bio: Erica is a developer lead at CloudAMQP, where she's spent several years building and operating message broker infrastructure with LavinMQ and RabbitMQ. She enjoys helping developers get comfortable with messaging patterns and the tooling that surrounds them.

speakers.name: Robbie Jerrom
speakers.title: Senior Principal Technologist AI
speakers.bio: As a Principal Technologist for AI at Red Hat with over 30 years of experience, Robbie works to support enterprise AI adoption through open source innovation. His focus is on cloud-native technologies, Kubernetes, and AI platforms, helping to deliver scalable and secure solutions using Red Hat OpenShift AI and RHEL AI. Robbie is deeply committed to open source, open source AI, and open data, believing in the power of transparency, collaboration, and inclusivity to advance technology in meaningful ways. His work involves exploring private generative AI, traditional machine learning, and enhancing platform capabilities to support open and hybrid cloud solutions for AI. His focus is on helping organizations adopt ethical and sustainable AI technologies that make a real impact.

speakers.name: Taylor Jordan Smith
speakers.title: Senior AI Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Taylor Smith, Senior AI Developer Advocate at Red Hat, is an advocate of open source AI innovation and democratization. She has a background in software development, open source technologies like Kubernetes and linux, and technical partnerships. Taylor loves music, animals, and helping to solve real-world problems with technology. Based out of North Carolina.

speakers.name: Toan Nguyen
speakers.title: Founder and CEO
speakers.bio: Toan Nguyen has been hailed as a "super-nerd" (Meedia), a "wunderkind" (Handelsblatt), a "magician" (RampStyle), and the "rockstar of marketing" (W&V). He began his career as an intern at Jung von Matt and worked his way up to become the youngest equity partner in the agency's history. During his tenure at Jung von Matt, he founded several verticals, including JvM STARS, JvM NERD, and ALFR3D, and established an investment in Eintracht Spandau—a League of Legends and Baller League club co-founded with YouTube star Maximilian Knabe. Toan Nguyen has been featured four times on "Top 40 under 40" lists (Capital, Horizont) and is one of the few Germans ever to be appointed twice to the Cannes Lions jury. Today, he advises executive boards as an advisory board member and senior advisor, while also acting as an investor in the longevity space and hosting a Top 30 healthcare podcast (Die Ärztin und der Nerd / The Doctor and the Nerd).

speakers.name: Brian Whippo
speakers.title: Senior Director
speakers.bio: Brian is the Senior Director of Integrations and Developer Tools at Algorand Foundation, where he helps devs learn, integrate, and contribute to Algorand's open source blockchain technology to solve real world problems. Before joining the Foundation, Brian led global strategic transformation programs at Morgan Stanley, where he led technology change programs for commodities, exchange-traded and OTC derivatives, Prime Brokerage, and digital assets businesses over 15 years. When not on his laptop, he's probably cooking slow food that makes the house smell good.

speakers.name: Max Jacobson
speakers.title: VP of Product
speakers.bio: Max began his career at Bridgewater Associates before moving into startups, including co-founding a company in a second language. He’s built and scaled teams across marketing, operations, finance, engineering, and product, bringing a systems-level perspective to building complex organizations and tools. Outside of work, Max loves traveling, cooking, and extremely long walks through cities and nature.

speakers.name: Justin Mongroo
speakers.title: COO and CRO
speakers.bio: As the COO/CRO of Runpod, Justin Mongroo leads the charge in scaling global AI infrastructure software orchestration, drawing on a deep executive background at high-growth organizations like Salesforce, Slack, Conga, and WP Engine. An AI enthusiast and seasoned team builder, he specializes in bridging the gap between cutting-edge technology and sustainable commercial scale. Outside of the office, Justin is a dedicated husband, father, and outdoorsman who can usually be found skiing, mountain biking, or experimenting in the kitchen.

speakers.name: Benedikt Höck
speakers.title: Head of AI
speakers.bio: Benedikt Höck is Head of Artificial Intelligence (AI) for KPMG in Germany and the EMA region. In this role, he is responsible for the AI go-to-market strategy, the development of service offerings, and the firm’s internal transformation through AI. He supports clients end-to-end on their AI journey - from strategy design and the implementation of high-impact use cases to organizational enablement and ensuring the secure, trusted deployment of AI.

speakers.name: David Tielke
speakers.title: Consultant, Coach and Trainer
speakers.bio: David Tielke is a consultant, coach, and trainer with over 15 years of experience in high-level software development. His expertise focuses on software quality, software architecture, and agile development processes. Having trained more than 30,000 participants, delivered over 150 presentations at renowned conferences, and published numerous articles in industry magazines, he has shared his knowledge and expertise widely. On his YouTube channel, he shares his passion for software development and provides practical insights into current industry topics.

speakers.name: Dominik Krichbaum
speakers.title: Product Manager
speakers.bio: Dominik is a Product Manager at ING, where he works we a team composed of multiple entities on platform capabilities for safer and more scalable software delivery. After leading products around process automation and testing capabilities, he now drives a broader initiative towards Continuous Delivery for a complex banking environment with multiple technologies in use, teams and regulatory requirements.

speakers.name: Duan Lightfoot
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Du’An Lightfoot is a Sr. AI Engineer at Akamai, where he specializes in advancing agentic AI systems and helping organizations leverage AI to solve infrastructure challenges and increase developer productivity. Drawing from his diverse background as a USAF veteran and technical experience at Cisco and Cerner, Du’An brings a unique perspective on implementing AI solutions in enterprise environments. He has delivered presentations at major industry events including AWS Re:Invent, We Are Developers, Code With Claude, and the AI Engineer World Fair, focusing on practical AI applications that transform how technical teams work. Through his hands-on experience with Agentic AI technologies and dedication to mentoring professionals through AI transformation, Du’An provides actionable insights and concrete strategies for developers to embrace AI-powered workflows and become productivity champions within their organizations.

speakers.name: Hendrik Lösch
speakers.title: Management Consultant and Architect
speakers.bio: Hendrik Lösch is a management consultant and architect at ZEISS Digital Innovation. The focus of his work is on the development and evaluation of software, based on Microsoft technologies. He also enjoys writing and talking about his work as well as his enthusiasm for clean code, software evolution and test automation in its various forms.

speakers.name: Joseph Katsioloudes
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Joseph is a leading voice in cybersecurity and AI, developing software and content that shape how developers build securely. His open source game gh.io/scg has helped 10K+ developers gain future-proof security skills. His videos, with 2.8M+ views, simplify complex security topics and deliver actionable tips to a global audience. As a speaker, Joseph has delivered 68 talks across 25 countries over the past four years, captivating audiences with his insights and energetic stage presence.

speakers.name: Lena Hall
speakers.title: Senior Director Developers and AI
speakers.bio: Lena Hall is an expert in practical AI adoption, data engineering, cloud and pragmatic architecture, driving strategic AI integration at scale combined with extensive experience leading large, high-performing technical teams. She is the Founder and CEO of Droid AI, where she helps businesses get real results from AI, defining Data + AI strategies, integrating LLMs into complex systems, connecting AI solutions with custom data and business tools, and optimizing outcomes through proven and innovative architectures. Lena has 15+ years of deeply technical background as a solution architect, technical leader in large-scale data, analytics, machine learning, and cloud computing. Prior to Droid AI, Lena worked as Head of Developer Experience, North America at Amazon Web Services and led Big Data DevRel at Microsoft. She frequently shares practical knowledge on her YouTube channel (youtube.com/@lena-hall) and at industry conferences as an international keynote speaker.

speakers.name: Linda Stauffenberg
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: Linda is a Strategic Advisor specializing in the field of enterprise innovation. She is co-founder and MD at Experience One, a digital agency focusing on innovative technologies for designing and scaling new and better CX for companies like Mercedes-Benz, BOSCH, AOK and FI. She focuses on strategic consulting and positioning of changemakers as well as in the implementation of customer centric change programs to create a sustainable culture of innovation. She benefits from her many years of experience in rollout and architecture consulting for the major digital platforms of large enterprises.

speakers.name: Matthias Niehoff
speakers.title: Head of Data
speakers.bio: Matthias Niehoff works as Head of Data and Data Architect for codecentric AG and supports customers in the design and implementation of data architectures. His focus is not so much on the ML model, but rather on the necessary infrastructure and organization to help data science projects succeed.

speakers.name: Nazeer Saeed
speakers.title: Staff Solutions Engineer
speakers.bio: With over a decade of experience in solution engineering, consulting, and digital transformation, I help organizations design scalable technology solutions that solve complex business challenges. At Apryse, I work with enterprises and development teams on document processing, OCR, SDK integrations, workflow automation, and AI-driven extraction pipelines. My focus is on building reliable, secure, and efficient systems that turn unstructured documents into actionable data. My background spans R&D in AI, knowledge management, and Industry 4.0 across Europe. As a speaker, I share practical engineering patterns that help developers build smarter, more predictable document AI solutions.

speakers.name: Pavel Malyarevsky
speakers.title: Director PCCP
speakers.bio: Pavel Malyarevsky is an engineering leader and infrastructure practitioner with nearly 20 years of experience running large-scale, mission-critical systems. He is currently the Engineering Owner of the Container as a Service part of Private Cloud at Deutsche Bank in Berlin, where he designs and operates Kubernetes-based platforms supporting latency-sensitive and high-throughput workloads. Pavel has led cloud and infrastructure organizations across multiple countries, managing fleets of tens of thousands of servers and network devices in highly regulated environments. His background spans Linux internals, distributed systems, observability, and production engineering, with a strong focus on performance and operational correctness. Having worked extensively with containerized runtimes in real production environments, Pavel focuses on closing the gap between Kubernetes abstractions and the underlying behavior of the Linux kernel and application runtimes—especially where hidden performance costs emerge.

speakers.name: Ramona Schwering
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Ramona is a developer advocate and software engineer with roots in quality assurance. She owns both views of the product - that of a tester and a developer. Ramona primarily uses this to strengthen trust in test automation and support the testers and developers alike, becoming a Google Developer Expert in Web Technologies, Women Techmaker Ambassador, and Cypress ambassador.

speakers.name: Sebastian Kister
speakers.title: Chief AI Architect
speakers.bio: Sebastian Kister is a speaker and transformation strategist who makes the complex simple and inspires action. With a rare blend of authenticity, sharp storytelling, and deep hands-on experience in large-scale IT and cultural change, he connects C-level leaders, tech teams, and innovators alike. His keynotes on People-First Transformation, AI adoption, and platform culture spark clarity, energy, and the drive to shape the digital future. Combining a decade of successful start-ups with in-depth enterprise wisdom, delivering more than 100 keynotes world wide he plays Champions League with premium-speakers.com. Sebastian is building functional and RoI driven Agentic Orgs along Human Orgs, harmonizing both and building a future work environment reducing complexity and enhancing time 2 market.

speakers.name: Tamas Piros
speakers.title: AI Consultant
speakers.bio: I help teams and leaders get genuinely good at using AI. Not the "watched a webinar" kind of good. The "transformed how we work" kind. I have been building software for over 25 years, training technical teams at Fortune 500 companies, and speaking at conferences worldwide. These days I focus on AI fluency: giving people the understanding and hands-on skill to use AI tools effectively in their actual work.

speakers.name: Zaid Zaim
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: As a Developer Advocate at Neo4j, I help developers harness the power of graph technology and AI. A Microsoft MVP and global speaker at events like TED, Microsoft Build, and WeAreDevelopers, I focus on making complex tech accessible through content, community, and hands-on support. My mission is to connect, empower, and inspire developers to build impactful solutions.

speakers.name: Alexander Gerfer
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: As Chief Technology Officer, Alexander Gerfer is responsible for establishing the Würth Elektronik eiSos Group as a technology enabler and service leader. The company is one of Europe's largest manufacturers of electronic and electromechanical components and part of the Würth Group. Gerfer began his career at Würth Elektronik eiSos 29 years ago as a technical sales representative. Since then, he has held various positions in the company. In his role as CTO, he particularly promotes technology partnerships in future-oriented areas. With his expert knowledge, he passionately supports and advises innovative start-ups, especially when they encounter unexpected technical problems in the implementation of their ideas. In line with the principle ‘Electronics for Positive Impact’, Alexander Gerfer is committed to sustainable, environmentally friendly and socially forward-looking technologies. For example, the company is researching LED light recipes for optimal photosynthesis and more efficient electronics. Würth Elektronik particularly supports innovative, ecological start-ups, for example in the areas of algae and mushroom cultivation, harvesting robots or biodiversity measurement. During the course of his career, Gerfer has also played a key role in the development and expansion of research and development, as well as product, quality and supply chain management. As an author of specialist books, the electrical engineer and trained radio and television technician presents complex interrelations in an easy-to-understand and practical way. He is also a sought-after keynote speaker at conferences around the world.

speakers.name: Stefan Lemke
speakers.title: CIO
speakers.bio: Stefan Lemke began his career as an IT architect, consultant, and manager of transformation projects at Accenture after completing his studies in physics and his doctorate, ultimately serving as a Partner in Germany and the Netherlands. From 2010, he held leadership positions at Allianz Technology in Munich and Paris before moving to the Zurich Group in Germany as CIO. In 2017, he was appointed to the Board of Management of AXA Konzern AG, where he drove agile organization and culture, technological modernization, and the renewal of the application landscape. Since 2023, Stefan Lemke has been a member of the Boards of Management of the SIGNAL IDUNA Group. He says: "Application development is a core business process, differentiating for our customers and distribution partners - and no longer conceivable without AI, cloud, and an agile setup."

speakers.name: Ariel Shulman
speakers.title: Chief Product Officer
speakers.bio: Ariel Shulman is an accomplished executive with extensive experience in technology management, business development, marketing, and strategy. Since joining Bright Data in 2021, Ariel has leveraged his networking, security, and Internet expertise to drive innovation to access high-quality public web data solutions at scale. He now serves as VP of Product, responsible for Bright Data’s AI-integrated product suite that leads innovation. Fluent in English, French, Spanish, and Hebrew, and with professional experience across multiple countries, Ariel plays a pivotal role in shaping Bright Data's global positioning.

speakers.name: Yanay Sela
speakers.title: Chief Marketing Officer
speakers.bio: Yanay Sela is the Chief Marketing Officer at Bright Data, the world's #1 web data infrastructure for AI. He was the first-ever CMO at Seeking Alpha, and previously served as CMO at Appsee and Galatea, where he led both companies through acquisition. Yanay is a resident professor of marketing in the global MBA program at Reichman University, and a visiting professor at leading business schools across Europe.

speakers.name: Krzysztof Cieślak
speakers.title: Staff Research Engineer
speakers.bio: Krzysztof is a photographer, globetrotter, and public speaker. He works as a Staff Research Engineer at [GitHub Next](https://next.github.com/), where he designs and builds the next generation of developer tools such as GitHub Copilot, NES or [REDACTED] After the work, Krzysztof is an open-source software maintainer passionate about functional programming (especially F#), editor tooling and developer experience - he has created and maintained several OSS projects varying from [language servers](https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete) to [web frameworks](https://github.com/SaturnFramework/Saturn) to [VSCode plugins](https://github.com/ionide/ionide-vscode-fsharp). Additionally, he is a passionate public speaker. You can meet him at many conferences around the world, where he is always trying to meet new people and learn how to improve software developers' lives.

speakers.name: Louis Chen
speakers.title: Chief Strategy Officer and Executive VP
speakers.bio: Louis Chen joined Perfect Corp in 2015 and has since played a key role in planning and executing capital investment rounds and the IPO process, leading Perfect Corp to its successful listing on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) in 2022. Chen spearheaded the transformation of Perfect Corp.’s PR and Communications team into a digital marketing powerhouse. He has also led the implementation of cutting-edge AI and AR technologies to provide beauty and fashion tech solutions to over 860 brands worldwide.

speakers.name: Andrew Funk
speakers.title: Partner Lead
speakers.bio: Andrew is Partner Integrations Lead at Algorand Foundation and has worked on the IT side of the payments industry for the past 20 years and has been an active builder in the Algorand ecosystem for the past 4 years. He has built several public-good products such as AlgoTools, FUNC Node Manager, and Lute Wallet. He now leads partner integrations for the Foundation.

speakers.name: Uli Irnich
speakers.title: CIO Advisor EMEA
speakers.bio: Uli Irnich is CIO Advisor EMEA at Salesforce, based in Düsseldorf. Drawing on over 14 years of experience as a CIO and Managing Director at major telecommunications companies — including Vodafone, Liberty Global, Telefonica, and E-Plus Group — Uli now strategically advises technology buyers and fellow CIOs within the expansive Salesforce ecosystem. His professional background is centered on driving complex corporate initiatives and technological innovation. He is a recognized expert in Digital Transformation and IT Modernization, successfully leading transitions to agile business models and implementing large-scale initiatives like Tech2025. Uli has managed six significant Merger and Acquisition activities, from planning through post-merger integration, notably utilizing MuleSoft for the Unitymedia-Vodafone integration. His focus on Operational Excellence includes Six Sigma expertise and Application Rationalization that delivers measurable financial synergies. As a thought leader, Uli specializes in topics like the Agentic Enterprise and the future of the digital workforce. He actively shares insights through his popular podcast, "Digital Pacemaker," and is a frequent speaker at major industry events.

speakers.name: Daniel Murphy
speakers.title: Head of SRE
speakers.bio: Daniel is a seasoned technology leader with 20 years of experience across diverse industries. He was among the first SRE managers in Ireland, adopting DevOps principles early in his career and demonstrating a forward-thinking approach to technology. As head of SRE at PwC, he led the transformation of business operations through the implementation of well-architected SRE solutions, significantly improving reliability and performance. Daniel's expertise also encompasses multi-cloud architecture, where he designs and implements solutions across various cloud platforms to address complex business challenges. He is passionate about driving AI development and exploring its potential to create innovative solutions for clients. A frequent public speaker, mentor, and active technology advocate,

speakers.name: David Campbell
speakers.title: Head of AI Security
speakers.bio: David Campbell is Head of AI Security at Scale AI, where he leads efforts to secure and stress test the world’s most advanced AI systems. With nearly two decades in Silicon Valley’s startup ecosystem, David has built a career at the intersection of infrastructure, security, and cutting-edge AI. He pioneered one of the industry’s first large-scale AI Red Teaming platforms, now used to probe the limits of generative models and harden them against real-world threats. David’s expertise has been recognized at the highest levels, from briefing the U.S. Congress and U.K. Parliament to advising NATO and the White House on AI risk and resilience. He is a founding member of OWASP AIVSS, helping establish open standards for AI security, and has played a key role in collaborative exercises such as CISA’s JCDC.AI cyber table-top series on AI-driven threats. Prior to his work in AI security, David spent years shaping security and developer experience at Uber, DoorDash, and Nest Labs. His mission today is clear: align AI innovation with business resilience, so organizations can adopt transformative technologies without sacrificing trust or safety.

speakers.name: David Peter Hirsch
speakers.title: Head of Open Source and Community
speakers.bio: David Hirsch is the Head of Open Source and Community at Dynatrace, an ex-CNCF Ambassador, and the founder of open-ecosystem.com . He operates at the intersection of code, community, and strategy. David doesn't just talk about open source; he builds the standards that power it. He shepherded OpenFeature from a rough idea to a CNCF-incubated project, creating the industry standard for feature flagging now used by major enterprises worldwide. Passionate about "Digital Sovereignty" and the developer experience, David brings a unique mix of operational rigor and community spirit to the stage. Whether he's discussing supply chain security or the economics of open source, his goal is always the same: to help developers build sustainable, impactful ecosystems.

speakers.name: Jaroslaw Gajewski
speakers.title: Lead Technical Cloud Architect
speakers.bio: Jarek is holding Lead Technical Cloud Architect position in Atos Cloud Enterprise Solutions practice. During last decade he was mastering his knowledge and expertise in virtualization and cloud related domains. He is holding multiple technology professional certificates and is recognized as an Atos Senior Expert. Jarek is passionate about sharing knowledge on meetups and conferences. In his free time he loves to play board games with his family and is constantly unraveling new cloud mysteries.

speakers.name: Jon Nordby
speakers.title: Head of Data Science
speakers.bio: Jon has been working as a Software engineer on various Embedded, web and IoT systems since 2010. He has a Master in Data Science, and a Bachelor in Electronics Engineering. In 2020, he co-founded Soundsensing, a leading European provider of Predictive Maintenance solutions for HVAC in buildings. Jon also maintains the emlearn open-source machine learning library for microcontrollers, since 2018.

speakers.name: Rabieh Fashwall
speakers.title: Technical Lead
speakers.bio: A distinguished Software Engineer with a rich 15-year trajectory in the computer software landscape, I specialize in architecting robust Cloud Native and Web applications through a strategic blend of technical expertise and innovative problem-solving. My technical arsenal spans Golang, .NET, and React (TypeScript), complemented by comprehensive proficiency in C#, JavaScript, and a versatile range of contemporary development technologies. With a proven track record of delivering high-performance solutions, I excel in designing scalable applications across multiple cloud environments, including AWS, Google Cloud Platform, and DigitalOcean. My approach transcends conventional coding—I view each project as an opportunity to push technological boundaries and deliver transformative digital solutions.

speakers.name: Andreas Kleinbichler
speakers.title: Engineering Manager
speakers.bio: 2005 - 2007 UVD Business Consulting 2007 graduation at Technical University Vienna (Technical Mathematics) 2006 - 2008 Developer at Admiral Sportwetten GmbH (ASW) 2008 - 2021 Software-/Enterprise Architect/ Principal Developer at ASW 2021 - Now: Head of Portal Engineering Unit at ASW Additional: 2019 Lektor at Technikum Wien (Software Engineering/Architecture) 2022 ISAQB Foundation Level Trainer at Technikum Wien Academy

speakers.name: André Behrens
speakers.title: Senior Solution Architect
speakers.bio: André is a Senior Solution Architect at Atos (located in Hamburg), where he designs architectural concepts and software change requests in the automotive sector. As SPOC for the Atos architects community he hosts TecSwaps and performs architectural reviews to drive cross‑project alignment. Previously he served as System Architect & Squad Lead at DB Schenker, leading cross‑functional scrum teams and shipping urgent country rollouts, and as a Senior Consultant at IBM. He began his career as a Java/Java EE/Cobol developer at SIGNAL IDUNA. With 15+ years of experience across automotive, logistics and insurance, André brings practical expertise in system architecture, agile delivery and software development.

speakers.name: Bernardo Belchior
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Bernardo is a software engineer working on the MUI X Charts team to build the next-generation of React charts. He likes performance work and data visualization in general (charts, maps, etc.) and is very excited to have the opportunity to improve the ecosystem of charting in the web.

speakers.name: Christian Bachler
speakers.title: Frontend Architect
speakers.bio: Christian Bachler has been involved in various frontend-related projects at Finanz Informatik since 2016. Currently, he is working as a software architect implementing a state-of-the-art and cross-platform frontend architecture. To this end, he is primarily concerned with web technologies, but also with completely new approaches like Kotlin Multiplatform.

speakers.name: Christian Wenz
