speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Christian Wenz is a consultant, software architect and entrepreneur specializing in web technologies and security. A prolific writer, he has authored or co-authored over 100 books for various publishers. Christian’s work spans both open-source and proprietary web technologies, a unique combination that has earned him recognition as a Microsoft MVP for Developer Technologies and the distinction of being the lead author of the Zend PHP certification. He is also credited in several open-source projects for his contributions. In addition to his writing and technical endeavors, Christian is a frequent speaker at developer conferences worldwide, where he shares his expertise in web technologies.

speakers.name: Clemens Vasters
speakers.title: Principal Architect
speakers.bio: Clemens Vasters is Lead Architect in Microsoft’s Azure Messaging team that builds and operates a fleet of hyper-scale messaging services, including Event Grid, Service Bus, Event Hubs, Stream Analytics and Microsoft Fabric Eventstreams. Clemens represents Microsoft in messaging standardisation in OASIS (AMQP, MQTT) and CNCF (CloudEvents, xRegistry) and writes too much code for being an "Architect". He looks back at nearly 30 years in professional software development and has seen the same fashion come and go a few times.

speakers.name: Dagmar de Haan
speakers.title: Software Developer and IT Architect
speakers.bio: Dagmar de Haan is a freelance software architect and software developer. Her focus is on the design and development of backend systems in the Java environment. Since her beginnings in software development a good 20 years ago, she has been concerned with the question of what makes good software, how it is created and, above all, how it is maintained. She is not only interested in the technical aspects, but also the domain-specific and methodological ones.

speakers.name: Daniel Bodky
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Daniel currently works as a Senior Platform Advocate for NETWAYS Web Services, a public cloud provider and MSP from Germany. In the past, he has helped clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies to SMBs with adopting cloud native solutions like Kubernetes, GitOps, and Observability as a consultant and systems engineer. His work and personal interests have led him to become a Grafana Champion as well as a CNCF Kubestronaut, and he enjoys sharing his experiences at meetups, conferences, and through blog posts on his website.

speakers.name: Daniël van Eeden
speakers.title: Technical Support Engineer
speakers.bio: Daniël has been working on TiDB and related projects. Before that he worked on scaling MySQL for a large company that sells hotel rooms. One of the side projects he has been working in is the MySQL protocol dissector in Wireshark.

speakers.name: Dr. Damir Dobric
speakers.title: Lead Architect and CEO
speakers.bio: CEO and Lead Architect of DAENET GmbH – ACP Digital, Microsoft's long-term ADvanced Certified Partner and a leading technology integrator specialised in software technologies, with a strong focus on AI, Cloud Computing and IoT. Dr. Damir Dobric is a highly skilled and experienced Lead Software Architect specialising in delivering innovative, highly customised software solutions and consulting services. In addition to his role at DAENET, Damir is also a Microsoft Most Valuable Professional (MVP). The Microsoft MVP Award is a prestigious recognition given to exceptional technical experts. Damir is also a part of an elite group of professionals known as Microsoft Regional Directors who actively offer guidance, support, and expertise in various Microsoft technologies across industries. Damir's commitment to excellence in software development and his dedication to helping others have earned him a reputation as a thought leader in the industry. He is a well-known international speaker and keynote speaker at conferences, inspiring people, showcasing his expertise and passion for technology. With a keen eye for innovation and a deep understanding of cutting-edge technologies, Damir Dobric is a valuable asset to both DAENET, ACP Group and the broader Microsoft ecosystem. His work continues to inspire and support other professionals, young people and students in their pursuit of technical excellence and innovation. Furthermore, he serves as an external professor for Software Engineering and Cloud Computing at the Frankfurt University of Applied Sciences, teaching AI, Cloud and Software Development. Damir holds a PhD in Computational Intelligence from the University of Plymouth, UK.

speakers.name: Eberhard Wolff
speakers.title: Head of Architecture
speakers.bio: Eberhard Wolff has 20+ years of experience as an architect and consultant - often on the intersection of business and technology. He is Head of Architecture at SWAGLab. As a speaker, he has given talks at international conferences and as an author, he has written more than 100 articles and books e.g. about Microservices and Continuous Delivery. His technological focus is on modern architectures – often involving Cloud, Domain-driven Design, and Microservices.

speakers.name: Ekaterina Kapranova
speakers.title: Backend Engineer
speakers.bio: I am a backend engineer at SoundCloud with a passion for solving technical problems and delivering results through fast, incremental progress. I transitioned into tech from social sciences by earning a computer science degree in Germany. My previous background gave me an edge in soft skills and communication, which I believe are often the biggest challenges in software engineering—not technical complexity, but aligning teams and ensuring clear communication. I was fortunate to be mentored by exceptional women and now mentor amazing women, hoping to help pave the way for greater diversity.

speakers.name: Emil Sorensen
speakers.title: CEO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Emil Sorensen and kapa.ai has been on the forefront of AI-based documentation assistants since they launched in June 2023 and completed the YCombinator S23 batch. Now Emil works closely with top tech companies including OpenAI, Docker, Mapbox and OSS projects to help provide instant AI-answers to developers worldwide.

speakers.name: Jannis Eickenroth
speakers.title: Head of Analytics and Customer Insight
speakers.bio: Jannis is Head of Analytics and Customer Engagement at Finanz Informatik, the central IT service provider of the Savings Banks Finance Group. He is responsible for the strategic use of data across the entire value chain – from data platforms and advanced analytics to AI-driven customer engagement. His goal is not just to make data available, but to turn it into tangible business value through integrated engagement management, data-driven decision-making, and a strong platform and architecture strategy. Previously, he led the Data Platform department and served as a project manager for the introduction of modern software technologies at Finanz Informatik. His technical background spans both mainframe and Java development. Jannis stands for pragmatic innovation, strategic thinking, and collaborative delivery

speakers.name: Katharina Löhrke
speakers.title: Frontend Architect at
speakers.bio: Katharina Löhrke has been working at Finanz Informatik since 2015 as a software engineer and frontend architect. She designs and develops frontend and backend applications using Java and JavaScript in the context of the OSPlus core banking system, with a focus on payment transactions. She has a strong interest in Java and its programming language concepts, with a particular emphasis on elegant, high-quality code. To share her knowledge and passion for Java, Katharina also lectures on both fundamental and advanced Java programming topics at a university of applied sciences.

speakers.name: Krasimir Tsonev
speakers.title: Senior Engineer
speakers.bio: Krasimir Tsonev is a coder with over ten years of experience in web development. Author of books on Node.js and React he works at Antidote.me where he helps people reaching clinical trials. Loves React and its ecosystem.

speakers.name: Kristiyan Velkov
speakers.title: Principal Frontend Developer
speakers.bio: Hi, I’m Kristiyan Velkov, and I’m excited to be here! I’ve been a front-end developer for over a decade, specializing in React.js, Next.js, and modern JavaScript frameworks. I’m passionate about building scalable and efficient solutions and sharing knowledge with the tech community through writing, speaking, and mentoring. I’m an active Medium author with nearly 6,000 followers in the tech niche and 30,000 connections on LinkedIn. I hold multiple professional certificates and have authored books, including Core TypeScript Utility Types and the upcoming Mastering React JS Interviews. My work focuses on topics like TypeScript utility types, SOLID principles, and React.js best practices. Beyond development, I enjoy organizing workshops and contributing to the growth of developers worldwide. It’s a privilege to share my journey and ideas with you, and I’m looking forward to connecting and learning together. My blog: https://medium.com/@kristiyanvelkov LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/kristiyan-velkov-763130b3/

speakers.name: Kritika Murugan
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Kritika Murugan is a passionate full-stack developer with expertise in Java, Python, and machine learning. Alongside her role as a Java Developer at Finanz Informatik, she teaches Distributed Applications and is quickly mastering German through daily practice. Before she started at Finanz Informatik, she has worked with top clients like Google and Disney.

speakers.name: Lucía Conde-Moreno
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Lucía Conde-Moreno is Head of the AI Research Center at Info Support, where she also works as a consultant software engineer specializing in data and AI applications. She is known as a Jack Of All Trades by her colleagues, having worked in varied roles ranging from .NET or Java developer to data scientist or machine learning engineer. She has worked for different national and international clients, in diverse fields such as finance, health care, energy, or education. She is part of the AI Champions chapter for promoting AI-augmented engineering tools, and she is responsible for supervising internal research in subfields of AI like explainability or computer vision. When she is not working, she is busy switching across random hobbies, from filmmaking to DJing. She holds a MSc in Computer Science, and a BSc in Telecommunications Engineering.

speakers.name: Markus Stauffenberg
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: Markus Stauffenberg is leadership coach, change management advisor, and entrepreneur with over 20 years of experience helping leaders to create impact in their organizations. As co-founder of Experience One, founder of Aseus, and the creator of the 'How to Pitch CX'-Community, he has assisted numerous companies in navigating successful digital and customer-centric transformations. As expert in effective change management and leadership communication, he is also a passionate mentor, having trained teams in startups, universities, and corporate settings.

speakers.name: Mehdi Mohseni
speakers.title: Tech Lead and Lead Cloud Engineer
speakers.bio: Mehdi is a senior software developer and system architect. Since 2000, he has been responsible for the design, implementation, and development of several different systems in the field of office automation, CRM, healthcare management, and automation in industry and logistics solutions. Mehdi specifically works in the field of software development based on .NET technology. Mehdi is a staunch fan of object-oriented programming. Since 2016, Mehdi has worked more or less with cloud computing systems.

speakers.name: Mina Golesorkhi
speakers.title: Founder
speakers.bio: The StartUp Headhunter. Recruited for +150 startUps and Scale Ups for various roles from Tech to Commercial. After years of supporting founders in building their teams Mina has turned to StartUp founder herself: building Hi Remi - a AI Sidekick for recruiters while leading a headhunting agency

speakers.name: Nikos Delis
speakers.title: Founder and CEO
speakers.bio: Microsoft Certified x6, MVP for Azure & IoT, Senior Cloud Architect and tech enthusiast. Passionate about software architecture and solution design. During my 10+ years of hands-on experience in Software Engineering, I have primarily focused on .NET-based solutions and Azure as a cloud-services provider. I have worked with pretty much all kinds of applications, from small Windows Forms tools to large-scale enterprise web-based solutions. The more I code, the more I realize that my true passion is solution design and software architecture, regardless of tech-stack and platform. Sharing knowledge and insights is the finest thing I know and the one thing I love the most in the IT community.

speakers.name: Robert Jeutter
speakers.title: Software Architect
speakers.bio: Robert is an embedded software engineer and software architect with 15+ years of hands-on experience in Rust and C++. His work lives at the intersection of reliable systems and real-world constraints—often in robotics. He regularly speaks at Hackerkiste Augsburg, CCC meetups, and Nerdnight, and runs workshops on software architecture and social robotics. In RoboCup, Robert contributes as a referee and organizer, and he has partnered with the University of Augsburg on robotics projects. His talks focus on clear engineering trade-offs, actionable patterns, and a playful maker spirit that turns ideas into working systems.

speakers.name: Shivay Lamba
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Shivay Lamba is a software developer specializing in DevOps, Machine Learning and Full Stack Development. He is an Open Source Enthusiast and has been part of various programs like Google Code In and Google Summer of Code as a Mentor and is currently a MLH Fellow. He has also worked at organizations like Amazon, EY, Genpact. He is a Tensorflow.JS SIG member and community lead from India.

speakers.name: Stan Bühne
speakers.title: Managing Director
speakers.bio: Stan Bühne is Managing Director at IREB, with over 20 years of experience in Requirements Engineering, business analysis, and project management. During 15 years in IT consulting, he led international digitalization and transformation projects, particularly in the telco sector. In addition to his role at IREB, Stan is an active board member of the Bitkom Working Group for Software Engineering and Architecture and a frequent speaker at international conferences, sharing insights on digital trends and best practices.

speakers.name: Tessel Haagen
speakers.title: Consultant Data AI
speakers.bio: Tessel is a consultant in data & AI at Info Support, specialized in interpretable AI. In her daily work, she applies AI-augmented engineering to build and evaluate intelligent systems. She is known as an enthusiastic knowledge source and an insatiably curious lifelong learner. Outside of work, she channels her strategic thinking into board games (with a soft spot for complex strategy games) and escapes into fantasy novels. With an MSc in Artificial Intelligence, an MSc in Computer Science, and an MA in Linguistics, she combines a strong technical foundation with a deep interest in language and reasoning.

speakers.name: Thomas Coopman
speakers.title: Software Consultant
speakers.bio: Thomas Coopman is a senior consultant at Aardling. He trains and consults teams working in complex environments to bring meaningful change to their organisations. He has built deep expertise in Domain-Driven Design and optimising for team productivity and efficacy. Thomas excels with organisations that recognise that high-performing teams need both technical excellence and a relentless focus on process, practices, and environment. His childhood curiosity with computers took him to the University of Leuven to study computer science. While there, he grew an enduring love for learning new programming languages, with a current proclivity for all things functional programming. Thomas teaches several public classes on topics ranging from Strategic Design, EventStorming, and Tactical Patterns at DDD Academy. He’s also a regular public speaker, with recent well-received talks at DDD Europe and KanDDDinksy. Outside of software, Thomas is a keen parent and a triathlon hobbyist.

speakers.name: Thomas Fricke
speakers.title: Cloud Security Architect
speakers.bio: Thomas Fricke has been working with containers and Kubernetes for 7 years, with Linux and networks for 30 and with computers for 40. His focus is on security in KRITIS environments (energy, health care, government, international transport) and the agile transformations that are necessary in these environments. He is a freelancer, founder (Endocode, Mitigant), and volunteered on various boards, including the German IT Planning Council. He designed the security architecture for the cloud native OpenDesk application.

speakers.name: Tom Asel
speakers.title: Software Architect
speakers.bio: Tom Asel is the founder of tangible concepts. He has been working as a developer, architect and trainer in software development for over 20 years. Agile architecture work is a focus of his work. He supports teams and organisations in aligning development processes, skills, technologies and architectures.

speakers.name: Abdelfettah Sghiouar
speakers.title: Senior Cloud Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Senior Developer at Google Cloud. Co-host of the Kubernetes Podcast by Google and CNCF Ambassador. My focus areas include High-Scalable distributed systems on Kubernetes, Service Mesh, and Serverless. With a background in datacenter scale architecture, operations, and consulting. I spend most of my time working on optimizing GenAI Apps for large-scale operations using Cloud Native Technologies and producing content targeting developers and ops professionals.

speakers.name: Angelika Shvets
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Angelika Shvets, a software developer based in Israel. I've been in the IT industry since 2004. Over the last 7 years, my work has primarily involved software development, encompassing research, implementation, integration, and testing of systems of varying complexity. Prior to my current role, I spent 8 years as a full-stack web application developer. In the early stages of my career, I gained a solid foundation with 5 years of experience as a system administrator. I also served as a system administrator in the military. I hold a Masters in Mathematics and Computer Engineering.

speakers.name: Boy Baukema
speakers.title: Senior Principal Security Consultant
speakers.bio: Boy Baukema is a Senior Principal Application Security Consultant at Veracode with over twenty years of experience in the software security industry. Specializing in Software Composition Analysis (SCA), static analysis, and secure CI/CD pipeline integrations, Boy helps organizations build robust defenses against modern threats. He brings deep practical knowledge of vulnerability remediation and supply chain security to his workshops, empowering developers to effectively balance speed and security in their daily workflows.

speakers.name: Cedric Clyburn
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Cedric Clyburn (@cedricclyburn), Senior Developer Advocate at Red Hat, is an enthusiastic software technologist with a background in Kubernetes, DevOps, and container tools. He has experience speaking and organizing conferences including Devoxx, WeAreDevelopers, The Linux Foundation, KCD NYC, and more. Cedric loves all things open-source, and works to make developer's lives easier! Based out of New York.

speakers.name: Cyrille Visser
speakers.title: Customer Engineer EMEA
speakers.bio: Cyrille is a customer engineer in EMEA and he specializes in cloud databases like Firestore and their crucial role in building high-impact Generative AI applications. He architects database solutions that provide the scalability, performance, and availability necessary for successful GenAI deployments.

speakers.name: Daniel Strebel
speakers.title: Solution Lead EMEA
speakers.bio: Daniel is a member of Google Cloud's Application Modernization practice in EMEA. He focuses on bringing best practices in the areas of developer productivity and the effective use of cloud native development practices to enterprise customers. He works closely with the Google Cloud product teams to scale new technologies and share best practices in the form of webinars, blog posts and open source assets.

speakers.name: Javier Cañadillas
speakers.title: Senior IT Professional
speakers.bio: Javier is a member of Google Cloud's Application Modernization practice in EMEA. He focuses on bringing best practices in the areas of developer productivity and the effective use of cloud native development practices to enterprise customers. He works closely with the Google Cloud product teams to scale new technologies and share best practices in the form of webinars, blog posts and open source assets.

speakers.name: Julian Wood
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Julian Wood is a Principal Developer Advocate at AWS helping developers and builders discover how serverless architectures can transform the way they build and run applications at any scale. He loves exploring how things work and bringing technical topics to life so developers can apply them in their own applications. He also spends time learning from developers in the community and bringing their real-world insights back to AWS product teams to make the best products and services.

speakers.name: Laura Tacho
speakers.title: Senior Principal Technologist
speakers.bio: Laura Tacho is a subject matter expert in measuring the efficiency of engineering organizations. Currently, she is a Senior Principal Technologist at AWS focusing on developer experience and AI-native development. Laura was previously the CTO of DX, where she led the company’s executive advisory and research programs. She is the co-author of the Core 4 Framework of Developer Productivity, used by companies like LEGO, Netflix, Airbnb, Pinterest, and Snowflake to measure and improve developer experience. In 2025, she was awarded Austrian Innovator of the Year. Laura’s been working on developer tools for 15 years, including building out some of the first developer tools for the Docker ecosystem.

speakers.name: Maish Saidel-Keesing
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Maish Saidel-Keesing is a Senior Developer Advocate at AWS. He's been working in tech for over 20 years, and if there's one thing that's been consistent throughout his career, it's this: if something can be automated, it should be. He's an automation junkie. Always has been. He helps developers build in the cloud. Not by talking at them, but by building with them. He live-codes at meetups. He writes. He speaks. He creates. He's got a blog with over 700 posts because he genuinely believes that when you share what you've learned, everybody wins. What gets him excited right now is the intersection of GenAI and developer workflows. Not the hype. The practical stuff. How do you actually use these tools to ship faster, automate your infrastructure, and focus on the code that matters? That's what he helps people figure out. He works with everyone from students writing their first lines of code to senior engineers architecting complex distributed systems. Dev communities. Academic institutions. Because making cutting-edge tools accessible to everyone is the whole point. His mission is simple. Equip developers with the knowledge, tools, and confidence to build anything they can imagine in the cloud.

speakers.name: Marcin Wawryszczuk
speakers.title: Head of AI
speakers.bio: Marcin Wawryszczuk, PhD, is Head of AI at Andersen and a seasoned AI strategist, solution architect, and IT project manager with 15 years of industry expertise. He has delivered over 50 successful projects for leading financial institutions across financial markets, real estate, and tech startups. Marcin serves as an assistant professor of AI research and advisor to national AI bodies, driving generative AI adoption, digital transformation, and enterprise AI strategy. He leads transformational and strategic initiatives where senior stakeholder management, vendor management, and technology intersect.

speakers.name: Marina Coelho
speakers.title: Developer Relations Engineer
speakers.bio: Marina is a Developer Relations Engineer at Google, focused on building native Android apps. Her mission is to help developers excel in building and growing their apps using Firebase, GCP and our suite of AI tools! Before joining the Firebase team in 2022, Marina worked as a software engineer for 8 years, 5 of which were dedicated to Android app development. She loves all things Jetpack Compose, Firebase and AI!

speakers.name: Matheus Guimaraes
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Matheus Guimaraes (@techwithmatheus) is a software architect and technical educator focused on designing scalable, production-grade systems. With over 25 years in tech, he has worked as a developer, architect, CTO, and tech co-founder, helping organizations modernize legacy platforms and build reliable systems. Today, he shares practical architecture insights through talks, articles, videos, and demos. He currently works as a Senior Developer Advocate at AWS.

speakers.name: Miguel Toscano
speakers.title: Data and AI Specialist
speakers.bio: Miguel Toscano is a Data and AI Specialist @Google, focusing on architecting foundational data systems and enabling AI-Agentic applications for high-performance enterprise solutions. Bringing a strong background in building and operating mission-critical data infrastructure, he has a deep appreciation for the rigorous reliability and scale demanded by modern computing. He spends most of his time working on optimizing the data layer for large-scale GenAI systems and sharing practical strategies on how modern data architectures can fuel the future of AI.

speakers.name: Salih Gueler
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Salih is a Senior Developer Advocate at AWS with a strong focus on frontend and mobile app development, developer experience, and serverless architecture. With a background in building scalable mobile and web interfaces, he brings hands-on expertise to developer communities around the world. He’s passionate about creating intuitive developer tools and open source projects, often blending frontend craft with cloud infrastructure. Outside of work, Salih is a dedicated runner, comic book enthusiast, and a dad who balances code, curiosity, and creativity—always exploring new skills, from making music to architecture.

speakers.name: Zahhar Kirillov
speakers.title: Delivery Manager
speakers.bio: During 20+ years spent in ICT industry, I had a privilege to wear all possible hats, acting as a software developer, UX designer, university lecturer, startup entrepreneur, CIO and finally as a delivery manager at S&P500 engineering company. Creating software is my biggest passion. I stay hands-on by delivering PoC and MVP to clients, diving into all aspects of modern technology, be it cloud computing, blockchain or AI. Originally from Estonia, since 2016 I live in Switzerland and in free time I enjoy doing yoga and snowboarding.

speakers.name: Arnaud Jean
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Helping Devs build cool stuff, learn faster, and laugh along the way — one bug at a time. Senior Developer Advocate at AWS, speaker, and agentic AI enthusiast turning complex cloud patterns into fun, hands-on demos

speakers.name: Celeste Horgan
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Celeste is a Sr. OSS Developer Advocate at Snowflake, focusing on open source data communities. She is a previous member of Kubernetes SIG Docs, founded the Inclusive Naming Committee at the Linux Foundation, and is a regular speaker on open source data topics for the past 3 years. Her work on inclusive language in tech has been featured in the New York Times. She currently resides in London, UK.

speakers.name: Joël Mesot
speakers.title: President
speakers.bio: Joël Mesot has been President of ETH Zurich since 2019. At ETH he studied physics and obtained his doctorate with a thesis on high-temperature superconductors. He spent several years researching quantum materials in France, the USA and above all at the Swiss Paul Scherrer Institute (PSI) where he was promoted to head the Laboratory for Neutron Scattering in 2004. In 2008 he became director of the PSI and was promoted full professor at ETH Zurich. Since taking office at ETH Zurich, Mesot has deepened dialogue with political actors both in Switzerland and internationally and driven forwards strategic fields such as AI, energy, space and cyber security.

speakers.name: Stuart van der Lee
speakers.title: DevOps Engineer
speakers.bio: Stuart is a Microsoft Azure & AI MVP and software engineer with a passion for cloud technologies, AI and modern programming languages. He also keeps a close eye on the evolving world of DevOps and the tooling, practices and patterns that surround it. Stuart closely follows F1 in his free time, likes to do several sports such as cycling, golf and throwing weights around in the gym, and he tries to keep up with the endless stream of series and movies (with varying degrees of success).

speakers.name: Adele Carpenter
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Adele is a Software Engineer and Consultant at Trifork Amsterdam where she is working on systems for the educational sector. Most of her work day is spent in the JVM/Spring and React ecosystems, although increasingly she plays a pivotal role as trusted advisor to Trifork’s customers. Adele is an experienced international speaker, having spoken at multiple editions of NDC, goto, Devoxx and JavaZone. As a speaker, she uses her exposure to real-world customer projects, experiences outside of tech and passion for story-telling to distill complex ideas into their essential parts. All with an air of good humour. When she’s not at her computer or on stage, you can find her in the gym pumping some serious iron as she pursues the sport of powerlifting.

speakers.name: Alessandro Stefouli-Vozza
speakers.title: Solution Engineer
speakers.bio: Community leader and CNCF ambassador, Alessandro has spent the last few years building cloud native infrastructures for Microsoft customers, animating the Dutch community, and training others to pass the CKx exams. He has passion for all things cloud native, he's been around open source for 25 years and recently moved to a new Developer Relations role. Twitter handle: @bongo

speakers.name: Alexander Günsche
speakers.title: Senior Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Alex is a Senior Solutions Architect at AWS with 20 years of IT experience in expert and leadership roles. He is a strong advocate of agile and DevOps practices, and he enjoys seeing serverless, cloud-native and event-driven architectures deployed at scale. He has delivered large transformation projects and successfully developed own and customers’ businesses. As an international speaker, he has held advanced technology sessions at a wide range of events.

speakers.name: Alexander Schwartz
speakers.title: Principal Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Alexander Schwartz is a Principal Software Engineer at IBM working full time as a Keycloak maintainer and technical team lead. At work and in his spare time he codes for Open Source projects. In previous jobs he worked as a software architect and IT consultant. At conferences and user groups he talks about JavaScript front ends, Java back ends, Kubernetes and single-sign-on with Keycloak.

speakers.name: Andrew Demczuk
speakers.title: Chief Revenue Officer
speakers.bio: LIVE arena.angel-serv.com (the talk's subject; world leaderboard, 24/7) TALKS (chronological, last 90 days) 20 Mar 2026 | "Don't Hack Me, Bro" | DLT Austria Vienna meetup https://www.meetup.com/dltaustria-vienna-meetups/events/313505411 25 Mar 2026 | "Exploit Arena" | AI Austria meetup https://www.meetup.com/ai-austria/events/313701184/ 08 Apr 2026 | "Roadblocks for AI" | KEYNOTE at ClawCon London https://luma.com/clawconlondon Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6rSEOzWY08U&t=4502s 29 Apr 2026 | "Agentic Economy" | DLT Austria at Fiskaly Vienna https://www.meetup.com/dltaustria-vienna-meetups/events/314094914 19 May 2026 | "Claws Across the Internet" | Vibecoding Vienna https://www.meetup.com/vibecoding-vienna/events/314180303 Slides: ademczuk.github.io/presentations/claws-across-the-internet/slides.html 21 May 2026 | "Step 4: Profit?" | ClawCon #2 at ViennaUP Startup Festival 5-min lightning, ~600 people; won the WWC26 congress pass Slides: ademczuk.github.io/presentations/step-4-profit/slides.html Youtube: https://youtu.be/Ca8ndi06SLw?si=pE3nR8o4CxhEbQX9 REPOS / DECKS github.com/ademczuk/presentations (all decks public) github.com/openclaw/openclaw (ex-maintainer; 21 PRs merged by me, 7 cherry-picked, 54 reviewed) github.com/nimbalyst/nimbalyst (contributor) RESEARCH "Live Adversarial Behaviour in AI-Component-Rich Multi-Agent Systems" | research proposal v2.1, May 2026 Partners: JKU LIT AI Lab, TU Wien, Austrian Institute of Technology Prepared for AI Factory Austria; OSF pre-registration Target venues: NeurIPS 2027, AAMAS 2027, USENIX Security '27 LoRA adapter: ademczuk/Bubbles-Arena-Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-LoRA-v3 HuggingFace; fine-tuned on arena combat data

speakers.name: Andrey Abramov
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Andrey Abramov is the Founder and CTO of SereneDB, where he is building a real-time analytical search and OLAP database. With over 15 years of experience in C++, Andrey specializes in production-grade search engines and database kernel internals. Prior to SereneDB, Andrey was the mastermind behind ArangoSearch, a search engine natively integrated into ArangoDB's distributed multi-model database core. Earlier in his career, Andrey held senior engineering roles at EMC and Quest Software, where he managed teams and led the development of enterprise-scale systems.

speakers.name: Anemari Fiser
speakers.title: Founder
speakers.bio: Anemari Fiser is a tech leadership coach and trainer who helps engineers grow into confident, people-centered tech leads. She is the author of O’Reilly’s book ‘Leveling Up as a Tech Lead’ and the creator of the O’Reilly course ‘Soft Skills for Tech Leads’. With over a decade of experience in the tech industry, she has held roles from software engineer to engineering leader. As an independent coach and trainer, Anemari has worked with 500+ engineers and trained 400+ tech leads across different organisations worldwide. She also shares practical leadership insights with a community of 30,000+ tech professionals on LinkedIn.

speakers.name: Barbara Kryslak
speakers.title: Associate Director
speakers.bio: Barbara Kryslak is a talent leader with extensive experience in both strategic recruiting and the operational deployment of HR technologies. She has led cross-functional teams through AI adoption initiatives, driving measurable improvements in hiring quality and operational efficiency across diverse talent markets.

speakers.name: Brian Lovin
speakers.title: Product Designer
speakers.bio: Brian Lovin is a software designer making AI products at Notion. He previously co-founded Campsite and Spectrum, spent four years designing GitHub's mobile apps, and designed payments experiences at Facebook. Outside of work you'll find him tinkering on side projects and writing about software design.

speakers.name: Corinna John
speakers.title: Principal Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Corinna lives in Hannover and works in renewable energies, always trying to connect coding with sustainability ideas. She ues C# since the year 2002 and has recently jumped into TypeScript for an LCIA project.

speakers.name: Cristina Musceleanu
speakers.title: IT Application Development Expert
speakers.bio: Cristina Musceleanu is a software developer in large-scale enterprise systems, with a strong focus on software architecture, modular design, and sustainable modernization. She is currently involved in evolving a legacy alert and notification platform from a centralized scheduler-based model to a more modular, event-driven architecture, applying principles from Clean Architecture and Onion Architecture to improve resilience, maintainability, and team ownership. Cristina is passionate about turning complex systems into clear, understandable designs and believes that great architecture is built through small, thoughtful steps rather than big rewrites. She enjoys sharing real-world lessons from ongoing projects and helping teams modernize responsibly in regulated and high-impact environments.

speakers.name: Dani Coll
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Dani began his tech journey at a startup 10 years ago as a mobile developer. Over the years, he worked at several consulting companies, gaining some experience across multiple stacks. Eventually, he specialized in frontend development and is now focused on advocating for the field by writing technical content, conducting workshops and giving talks.

speakers.name: Daniel Evenschor
speakers.title: MLOps Engineer
speakers.bio: Daniel Evenschor is ML Ops Engineer at Finanz Informatik, specializing in deploying, scaling, and monitoring LLMs and ML models in the banking sector. With a background as a Data Scientist at Evicon, he bridges data science and production systems to drive real-world impact. His expertise spans MLOps, CI/CD, Kubernetes, OpenShift, and advanced model observability using tools like Prometheus, Dynatrace, and OpenTelemetry.

speakers.name: Diana Gehrke
speakers.title: Talent Lead
speakers.bio: Diana is a global Talent Intelligence and Talent Acquisition leader currently working at Stellantis, where she focuses on skill-based hiring, workforce analytics, and competitive talent intelligence. She has built and scaled talent strategies in complex, high-growth environments, with prior experience at Meta, Nubank, and HelloFresh. Her work sits at the intersection of HR, data, and leadership decision-making, helping organizations anticipate capability risk and turn market signals into strategic workforce action.

speakers.name: Eduard Dücker
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: For over a decade, Eduard has been working as an application developer at Finanz Informatik, currently working as a Data Engineer in the Data Analytics division. He designs data-driven solutions for analyzing and optimizing business processes, with a strong focus on efficiency, customer value, and technological innovation. He is especially passionate about working with modern database and analytics technologies and continuously explores new ways to turn complex data into actionable insights.

speakers.name: Gabriel Kuettel
speakers.title: Engineering Lead
speakers.bio: With over a decade of experience spanning blockchain, cybersecurity, and web development, Gabe brings deep technical expertise and entrepreneurial spirit to the Algorand ecosystem. As a Senior Developer Relations Engineer at the Algorand Foundation, he bridges the gap between emerging technology and practical implementation. Previously, he co-founded TxnLab and helped build NFDomains, the leading identity solution on the Algorand blockchain. Having worked with both early-stage startups and established enterprises, Gabe leverages his diverse background to help builders navigate the full spectrum of challenges in blockchain development. He is committed to empowering developers through hands-on technical education and community building.

speakers.name: Georg Dresler
speakers.title: Principal Software Architect
speakers.bio: Georg studied computer science and decided to become an app developer when the first iPhone was released. He learned Objective-C, then later Swift and Kotlin. Today, he focuses on architecting and developing apps with Kotlin Multiplatform, Flutter, and native technologies. With over a decade of experience, he specializes in application architecture, data modeling, testing, and code quality. Georg has spoken at WeAreDevelopers, InfoQ Dev Summit, and Almato DevCon, as well as several meetups. He has also been a guest on a podcast and taught a course on mobile app development.

speakers.name: Harald Nezbeda
speakers.title: Python Technical Lead
speakers.bio: Hi, my name is Harald and I'm a passionate Python developer interested in development, DevOps and AI. I'm currently located in Austria working as a Senior Software Developer and Python Technical Lead for Anexia. I also work on Open Source Projects and write Articles and Tutorials on my blog.

speakers.name: Hellmar Becker
speakers.title: Solution Architect
speakers.bio: Hellmar is a Solution Architect at ClickHouse. He has 20+ years experience in Analytics, Big Data, and Event Streaming. Hellmar worked at Hortonworks and Imply and helped implementing the first Data Lake at ING Bank.

speakers.name: Hendrik Ebbers
speakers.title: Director of Open Source
speakers.bio: Hendrik Ebbers is the director of open source at Hashgraph and founder of Open Elements. He is a Java champion, a member of JSR expert groups and a JavaOne rockstar. Hendrik is a member of the Eclipse JakartaEE working group (WG) and the Eclipse Adoptium WG. In addition, Hendrik Ebbers is a member of the Board of Directors of the Eclipse Foundation. At Linux Foundation Hendrik is leading the Hiero project, that is the base of Hedera, one of the biggest public DLTs. He leads the technical steering committee for the Hiero project and is a member of a Technical Advisory Board of LF. He gave several sessions about decentralized ledger technology at conferences and even lectures about that topic at universities.

speakers.name: Henriette Brune
speakers.title: Chief of Staff to the CTO
speakers.bio: Henriette Brune is Chief of Staff at FINN, one of Germany’s fastest-growing mobility startups. In her role, she works at the intersection of technology, business strategy, and organizational design, driving initiatives that accelerate innovation, scale tech effectiveness, and unlock hidden business value. Her path into tech leadership wasn’t traditional: starting her career in HR, Henriette now partners closely with FINN’s CTO and engineering leadership. This cross-disciplinary background allows her to connect dots that are often siloed, linking engineering, product, and business departments to deliver measurable impact. At FINN, she has led projects such as the capitalization of R&D efforts and designing innovation frameworks that helped the company move quickly on frontier technologies like Agentic AI. She is passionate about transforming tech organizations from “cost centers” into profit multipliers, and about building structures where ambitious goals, fast iteration, and even failure become catalysts for growth. Henriette speaks on automation, innovation, and organizational scaling, sharing practical playbooks for leaders who want to combine speed with resilience in fast-moving industries.

speakers.name: Irina Terekhova
speakers.title: Co-founder and CPO
speakers.bio: Irina Terekhova is Co-founder and Chief Product Officer at Banalytics, an open edge orchestration platform for cameras, sensors, and industrial devices. At Banalytics she owns the product strategy, use-case definition, and roadmap across four customer segments: industrial sensing teams, AI data collection pipelines, SMB operations, and homelab builders. Her focus is on making edge infrastructure observable and deployable without requiring teams to build the operational layer from scratch. Before Banalytics, Irina spent nearly two decades in product management and business analysis across AI, security, and financial services — including launching an AI line of business at an industrial hardware company and leading product teams at a global software consultancy.

speakers.name: Jeremy Murray
speakers.title: Founder and CEO
speakers.bio: Jeremy Murray is the Founder and CEO of Stack8s, a UK-based company on a mission to commoditise GPU compute on Kubernetes and make AI infrastructure as accessible and interchangeable as electricity. With over a decade of experience in enterprise cloud transformation, Jeremy has led large-scale migrations from legacy platforms to modern cloud environments across highly regulated industries. He holds a PhD from the University of Edinburgh, has authored more than 12 peer-reviewed research papers, and is the inventor of three patents spanning AI, IoT, and advanced computing technologies. Before founding Stack8s, Jeremy worked across cloud, data, and infrastructure programmes, helping organisations modernise mission-critical systems while balancing performance, security, and compliance requirements. Today, he is focused on solving two of the biggest challenges facing AI adoption: GPU scarcity and cloud lock-in. Under his leadership, Stack8s has built a sovereign-by-design AI compute platform that enables organisations to access and orchestrate GPU resources across multiple cloud providers, data centres, and on-premises environments through a unified Kubernetes control plane. The company works with research institutes, enterprises, and startups to accelerate AI innovation while maintaining control over data sovereignty and compliance. Jeremy is a regular speaker on Kubernetes, AI infrastructure, sovereign cloud, and multi-cloud computing, advocating for a future where compute becomes a true commodity and innovation is no longer constrained by infrastructure availability.

speakers.name: Jan Curn
speakers.title: Founder and CEO
speakers.bio: Jan Curn is the founder and CEO of Apify (https://apify.com), a full-stack web scraping and automation platform that powers (not only) AI agents with up-to-date data. He has a lifelong passion for software engineering, which earned him an MSc and PhD in computer science and eventually led him to founding Apify. Jan lives between SF and Prague, is active in the tech community in both cities, organizes meetups, and talks about software, startups, or AI.

speakers.name: Jasmin Azemović
speakers.title: Associate Professor
speakers.bio: Cybersecurity executive, university professor, and former Dean of the Faculty of Information Technologies in Mostar, Bosnia and Herzegovina. Throughout his career, he has designed, implemented, and led security functions within Mistral and HTEC, where his expertise has expanded across both offensive and defensive security domains. His professional focus includes leading Security Operations Center (SOC) teams, managing incident response operations, and driving the implementation and continuous improvement of ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II compliance frameworks. With more than 25 years of professional experience, he has been actively engaged in the fields of information security, privacy, digital forensics, and database systems. He is the author of three books: Writing T-SQL Queries for Beginners Using Microsoft SQL Server 2012, Securing SQL Server 2012, and SQL Server on Linux. Jasmin is a Microsoft Security MVP and a frequent speaker at international IT conferences, industry events, and expert panels. Over the past 18 years, he has delivered more than 170 presentations, sharing practical insights from both industry and academia.

speakers.name: Jawed Sahebjan
speakers.title: Software Engineer and Technical Lead
speakers.bio: Ahmad Jawed Sahebjan is an IT professional with over a decade of experience as a lead developer and senior IT consultant, having worked with organizations such as Volkswagen, Deutsche Bank, Postbank, R+V, and Deutsche Telekom. Over the years he has worked alongside numerous IT freelancers, giving him a clear picture of what separates those who thrive from those who stay stuck competing on hourly rates. This experience led him to found IT-Freelance-Forge, a platform helping IT freelancers and entrepreneurs in the DACH region build strategic client relationships and financial independence. He regularly shares insights on his YouTube channel: youtube.com/@JawedSahebjan

speakers.name: Jenny Speelman
speakers.title: Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: Jenny Speelman is a Staff Engineer for Developer Relations at Edge Impulse, where she is a technical speaker, developer evangelist, and technical content creator. She's co-author of the O'Reilly book "AI at the Edge: Solving Real World Problems with Embedded Machine Learning" and has presented workshops and tech talks for major tech conferences such as the Grace Hopper Celebration, Edge AI Summit, Embedded Vision Summit, Embedded World, and more. In addition to maintaining the Edge Impulse documentation, she has also created developer-facing resources for Arm Mbed OS and Pelion IoT. Jenny previously worked as a software engineer and IoT consultant for Arm Mbed and Pelion. She graduated with a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from The University of Texas at Austin.

speakers.name: Jens Posingies
speakers.title: Engineering Manager
speakers.bio: Jens Posingies is an Engineering Manager at Redcare Pharmacy with 25 years of experience in software development, architecture and engineering leadership. Born and raised in East Germany, he studied Business Information Systems at the University of Lüneburg and Media & Education at the University of Rostock. Over his career, Jens has worked in roles ranging from Java and Node.js developer to Solution Architect and Engineering Manager, including positions at Zalando, TUI, HRS and Redcare Pharmacy. His current focus is on lateral leadership, end-to-end ownership and modular software architectures such as microservices, microfrontends and Kotlin Multiplatform. He is also a certified nerd with deep expertise in Back to the Future and Star Trek.

speakers.name: Jordan Tigani
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Jordan is the CEO and Chief Duck Herder of MotherDuck, a data warehouse built on DuckDB. He was previously a founding engineer of Google BigQuery, Director of Product at Google, and Chief Product Officer at SingleStore.

speakers.name: Jorrin Bruns
speakers.title: Integration Specialist
speakers.bio: Jorrin is a former web developer and web development instructor turned blockchain builder and educator. He has spent the past 8 years in the blockchain industry and has been fully committed ever since, making the leap full-time. He is driven by a passion to help build the next layer of the internet — and to equip other builders with the tools, mindset, and conviction to do the same.

speakers.name: Legare Kerrison
speakers.title: AI Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Legare Kerrison is an Open Source Engineer and Developer Advocate on Red Hat's AI team. She focuses on open source tools for building and deploying AI. She aims to make technical complexity digestible. She’s a tree hugger and avid matcha fan. Based in Boston.

speakers.name: Manuel de la Peña
speakers.title: Staff Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Manuel is an OSS Software Engineer at Docker where he works in the Clients & SDKs team, also maintaining "Testcontainers for Go". Since 2003, he has held various roles in different parts of the development process, starting in 2003 working for the regional public administration in Castilla-La Mancha (Spain) until 2007, then worked at more traditional consulting firms. In 2011, he transitioned to product-oriented and Open Source companies, where he has served as a support engineer, trainer, and Core Engineer at Liferay, as QA Tech lead at Liferay Cloud. From 2019 to 2022 he was involved in Engineering Productivity at Elastic as part of the Observability product, and since 2022 doing pure OSS at AtomicJar, which was acquired by Docker in Dec' 23. In every job he tries to improve the quality of the software products and processes from the automation and testing point of view. He has also founded and managed a couple of small web development and systems consulting companies. Additionally, he organises the Google Developers Group in Toledo, Spain (GDG Toledo), where they run monthly discussions about software in its various aspects, serving as a small community outside the bustling Madrid. Manuel has delivered talks at different national and international events too. Manuel holds a BS in Computer Science (UNED Spain), and a master degree in Research in Software Engineering and Information Systems (UNED Spain). You can find him on Internet as "mdelapenya" everywhere.

speakers.name: Manuela Locarno Ajayi
speakers.title: SVP Engineering
speakers.bio: As TomTom’s SVP Product Engineering, Manuela leadsTomTom's efforts to create game-changing location products that integrate our technical offerings and serve a wide range of customer use cases. With over 25 years of experience in software and product roles, Manuela has led organizations through transformative growth, innovation, and operational excellence at companies such as Microsoft, the World Economic Forum, and Amazon. She holds Master’s degrees in Software Engineering from the Politecnico of Milano, and in Business Administration from the University of Washington.

speakers.name: Marc Arndt
speakers.title: VP Engineering and Architecture
speakers.bio: Marc Arndt has over 20 years of corporate development experiance, primarily focusing on Go and Kubernetes over the last 8 years. Currently the technical Leader at Evana AG, Marc Arndt has overseen the digital transformation of the organisation and brought the company forward into an AI 2.0 world.

speakers.name: Marc Plogas
speakers.title: Cloud and IoT Engineer
speakers.bio: Marc Plogas discovered his passion for technology at the age of six when an Atari sparked his interest in programming. He holds a Master's in Computer Science and spent nearly a decade freelancing as a software, mobile, and test engineer, as well as a solution architect, before joining Microsoft. At Microsoft, he played an instrumental role in pioneering technologies such as Windows Mixed Reality, Desktop Bridge, and WinUI 3, and later advised startups worldwide on optimizing their cloud architectures with a focus on reliability, scalability, cost efficiency, and security. Now at RoboTwin, Marc builds reliable cloud and edge IoT backbones for no‑code robot teaching and continues to deliver talks and workshops on IoT, AI, and cloud architecture.

speakers.name: Marcos Placona
speakers.title: Founder
speakers.bio: Marcos Placona is the founder of DevRel Bridge, a Developer Relations consultancy that helps developer-first companies scale through strategy, content, and community building. With over 20 years in tech and 12+ years of DevRel leadership at companies like Twilio and Circle, he treats DevRel as a revenue-driving function, focusing on activation over awareness and delivering measurable business outcomes. Marcos also founded LinkIntel, a SaaS platform providing actionable LinkedIn analytics for content creators and marketers. His contrarian approach challenges industry norms and helps startups build developer adoption infrastructure that drives real growth.

speakers.name: Marcus König
speakers.title: Managing Director
speakers.bio: I’m Marcus, a growth operator and former VC. Over the past decade, I’ve worked with fast scaling B2B software and fintech companies to turn strong traction into repeatable, structured growth. Today, I partner with high-growth teams as a hands-on consultant, interim executive and advisor, aligning marketing, partnerships, sales and success. I focus on building sustainable, defensible go-to-market & engines, while driving commercial and product excellence.

speakers.name: Martin Sakowski
speakers.title: Principal Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Martin Sakowski is a Solutions Architect at Amazon Web Services. He supports enterprise customers, particularly in the retail sector, on their digital transformation journeys. He has a strong background in building digital platforms and serverless stacks and specializes in helping organizations unlock the potential of Agentic AI and improve the Agent Experience (AX). With this experience he loves to dive deep into architectures and development processes to drive performance, operational efficiency, and increase the speed of innovation.

speakers.name: Matthias Kordt
speakers.title: Team Lead LLM Ops Data and AI Platform
speakers.bio: Matthias Kordt is involved in the planning and provision of the AI and data analytics platform for 50 m Sparkasse clients, and has been working for Finanz Informatik since 2019. The focus here is on automation. Matthias coordinates between the various players on the platform, is always keen to accelerate regulation through automation and enjoys advancing new technologies. A clear focus is the automation and monitoring of the container platform.

speakers.name: Mattias Jonsson
speakers.title: Principal Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Mattias is a Principal Software Engineer at PingCAP, where he works on TiDB's SQL layer and currently on the optimizer's statistics management. He has worked with databases, and MySQL in particular, for more than 20 years. Before PingCAP he spent years at Booking.com working with MySQL at scale, and earlier worked on the MySQL Server code at MySQL AB, Sun, and Oracle. Table partitioning is a recurring theme across his career: he built native partitioning in InnoDB for MySQL, and global indexes for partitioned tables in TiDB.

speakers.name: Michael Friedrich
speakers.title: Principal Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Michael Friedrich is a Principal Developer Advocate at GitLab, focusing on production-ready Agentic AI use cases and DevSecOps efficiency. His journey from Hardware/Software Systems Engineering to DNS, monitoring, and GitLab trainings fuels his embedded homelab research on practical AI implementations. Michael educates at industry events through tutorials and live coding. When not traveling, he builds LEGO models and explores new tastes and cultures.

speakers.name: Mihai Faur
speakers.title: CIO
speakers.bio: Mihai Faur is Chief Information Officer at UiPath, where he leads enterprise technology and transformation initiatives focused on automation, AI, and operational excellence. With extensive experience across finance, operations, and technology leadership roles, including Controller, CAO, and CFO positions, he brings deep expertise in enterprise systems, finance transformation, RPA, technical accounting, and business operations. Mihai is passionate about helping organizations harness automation and AI to drive efficiency, innovation, and smarter decision-making.

speakers.name: MIlan Todorović
speakers.title: Apple Certified Trainer
speakers.bio: Milan Todorović is an Apple Certified Trainer, Consultant, and Software Engineer with decades of experience in high-level developer training. A Swift expert since its inception, he serves as an associate lecturer at the Apple Authorized Training Centre in Serbia, specializing in machine learning, AR, and advanced software architecture. With a background in Electrical Engineering, Milan has a long history of IT leadership, including heading Borland’s regional office (2001–2010). Since becoming a certified Apple trainer in 2017, he has been a regular speaker at the WeAreDevelopers world congresses in Vienna and Berlin, focusing on ML and IT education methodology. As a consultant for major European companies and founder of Edulibris (Estonia), Milan currently focuses on leading engineering teams using advanced ALM techniques. His unique approach combines deep technical mentorship with real-world project delivery to solve the global talent shortage in high-end software engineering.

speakers.name: Nazim Ünlü
speakers.title: Strategic Advisor
speakers.bio: Strategic Advisor at allygatr

speakers.name: Oleksandr Korotkykh
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Alex Korotkykh is a fintech engineering leader with over 15 years of experience building large-scale, regulated software systems. Originally from Ukraine, he has been living and working in Germany for more than 10 years. He has spent most of his career in fintech and data-driven products, working at companies such as Kreditech, figo, and Zalando, where he helped design, build, and scale complex platforms at the intersection of finance, data, and software engineering. Alex joined Pliant at a very early stage and now leads its technology organisation of nearly 100 people, focusing on scalable architecture, security, and building reliable AI-powered products for B2B payments and accounting.

speakers.name: Oliver Seitz
speakers.title: Associate Manager
speakers.bio: I've been a software developer at heart for ten years and am interested in everything related to software development, like containerization and orchestration, machine learning, security, and the cloud. I work as an Associate Manager @ Accenture in Munich, Germany. I love fishing, Microsoft Flight Simulator, and TikTok.

speakers.name: Patrick Feige
speakers.title: Senior Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Patrick Feige is a Senior Solutions Architect at Veracode with over a decade of experience across software engineering and cybersecurity. Having transitioned from hands-on engineering into security, he brings an engineer’s perspective to modern application security challenges. His work includes designing secure DevSecOps workflows, integrating application security tooling into CI/CD pipelines, and advising enterprise teams on software supply chain risk. At Veracode, Patrick focuses on helping organisations operationalise security across the SDLC, enabling faster delivery without compromising resilience or compliance.

speakers.name: Paul Graham
speakers.title: Senior Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: At NVIDIA, Paul has responsibility for supporting customers and partners in delivering accelerated solutions for the Higher Education, High Performance Computing and AI communities in the UK. As well as providing advice on making the best use of NVIDIA hardware, Paul teaches on how to program for GPUs, mentors at hackathons and regularly engages with research software engineering groups. Paul is an advocate for using accelerated computing in HPC, and the use of AI as a powerful tool for researchers.

speakers.name: PJ Hagerty
speakers.title: Co-Host
speakers.bio: PJ Hagerty is a well-known figure in the tech industry, particularly within the developer relations and DevOps communities. He is recognized for his work as a developer advocate, community builder, writer, and speaker. PJ founded DevRelate.io, a company focused on helping tech organizations build communities and enhance developer relations. He has a strong presence in the open-source and tech communities, often speaking at conferences and producing content on topics such as software development, DevOps, and community engagement.

speakers.name: Przemysław Ładyński
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Przemysław Ładyński is a founder, technology leader, and software architect with deep experience in distributed systems, enterprise integrations, and multi-language software platforms. As co-founder of SdNcenter and Founder, CTO & CEO of Graftcode, he has spent years building technologies that simplify how complex systems communicate across languages, runtimes, and organizational boundaries. He founded Javonet - a unique integration technology that enables developers to build scalable applications without implementing web services or custom integration layers. Javonet supports interoperability across dozens of programming language pairs and runtimes, including Node.js, .NET, JVM, Go, C++, Ruby, Perl, and Python, helping enterprises reduce development, deployment, maintenance, and cloud computing costs while improving integration performance. Przemysław has also contributed to large-scale digital healthcare platforms, including globally deployed systems connecting healthcare professionals, patients, medical devices, EHR/EMR systems, and regulated medical data flows across Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific. His work focuses on software architecture, enterprise automation, developer tooling, and practical innovation.

speakers.name: Riccardo Carlesso
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: As a Developer Advocate in Google Cloud, I help customers on topics like SRE, reliability, DevOps, and Digital Transformation / Application Modernization though Google Cloud. Since May 2025, I fell in love for Gemini CLI and i've been using it for my projects at home, and preaching its use at work too! As a community person, I actively participate/organize GDG Cloud Zurich, nurture GDEs and GDG Cloud in Italy. I've also co-founded Rubycon.it

speakers.name: Rick Rackow
speakers.title: Senior Professional Engineer
speakers.bio: Rick Rackow is a seasoned professional in the IT industry with a strong background in cloud and container infrastructure. An SRE at heart, he has worked extensively with distributed systems, Linux, and Go, and focuses on building and operating reliable, observable systems in real world production environments.

speakers.name: Robert Pazurek
speakers.title: Team Lead
speakers.bio: I'm developing Software for over 20 years now. I've worked at different companies from small 10 men startups up to huge enterprises operating world wide both freelance and as an employee. For a while I ran my own small personal fitness training studio and dabbled in many side projects that had nothing to do with software development but focused on communication and working with people. Over time I acquired a lot of experience and knowledge regarding learning and skill development. Be it as a personal trainer, father to two kids (and one dog) or husband to a great agile coach and systemic organizational developer. How people learn and how systems evolve and develop in positive ways has become a passion topic of mine.

speakers.name: Roelant Dieben
speakers.title: Cloud and AI Architect
speakers.bio: With over 20 years of experience developing software on the Microsoft stack, Roelant Dieben has a lot to share about stuff that has been obsolete for years. At Sopra Steria he is helping companies with their Azure cloud and AI challenges, he is a Microsoft Azure & AI MVP, and has a passion for machine learning & AI and application lifecycle management.

speakers.name: Roman Zhukov
speakers.title: Principal Architect
speakers.bio: Roman is a cybersecurity expert, engineer, and leader with over 17 years of hands-on experience securing complex systems and products at scale. Currently Principal Architect at Red Hat, he leads open-source security strategy, upstream collaboration, and cross-industry initiatives focused on building trusted software ecosystems. He has built and scaled programs across security architecture, threat modeling, secure development, vulnerability management, incident response, and security education - for both engineers and senior leadership. His work spans trusted AI, privacy, compliance, and secure software supply chains. Previously, Roman led Product Security & Privacy for Data Center and AI software at Intel. He is a Security Champion for several open-source projects and an active contributor to working groups under the OpenSSF, Eclipse Foundation, and other global initiatives. Roman is also a university lecturer, startup advisor, and mentor - advocating for a practical, collaborative, and responsible approach to open-source and cybersecurity. He is an official member of CEN/CLC and ETSI standardization working groups, contributing directly to the EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA).

speakers.name: Rosário Pereira Fernandes
speakers.title: Senior Developer Relations Engineer
speakers.bio: Rosário P. Fernandes (@thatfiredev) is a Developer Relations Engineer on the Firebase team at Google, focused on helping developers integrate Firebase and Generative AI into their mobile apps. With a passion for developer communities, he can often be found hanging out with other Android, Firebase, or Flutter developers. In his free-time, he enjoys playing videogames, reading comic books and watching movies.

speakers.name: Sabrina Engling
speakers.title: AI Lead
speakers.bio: Sabrina Engling leads the AI Solutions Team at Trusted Shops and drives AI adoption and innovation across the entire company. Her focus is on scalable AI solutions, AI operations, and agentic commerce. She combines technical expertise with strategic business acumen, bringing together both perspectives - from the architecture of scalable AI systems to business value. Beyond her corporate role, Sabrina is a dedicated advocate for tech education and serves as Chairwoman of the NGO TechLabs, helping thousands of people build digital skills. As an AWS Commmunity Builder for AI Engineering, alumna of the AWS She Builds mentoring program, a certified AI Manager, and a recent AI hackathon winner, she is committed to empowering more women to take the leap into AI and cloud technologies.

speakers.name: Sandhya Menon
speakers.title: Lead Data Engineer
speakers.bio: Sandhya Menon is a passionate Data Engineer with over a decade of experience wrking with data. Her journey spans Data warehousing, Statistical data modelling, Analytics with a strong focus on SQL, dbt, data modeling and scalable architectures. She enjoys turning complex, messy data problems into reliable solutions that teams can trust and that scale with both data and people

speakers.name: Sebastian Klenke
speakers.title: Head of AI and Data Analytics
speakers.bio: Sebastian Klenke heads the AI & Data Analytics Platform team at Finanz Informatik and has been working for the company since 2011. His enthusiasm for using technology to create added value for people's everyday lives motivated him to teach at the FOM University of Applied Sciences as a lecturer for Big Data and Data Science and to work there as a research assistant on publications with a research focus on text generation. He sees the provision of the necessary technologies to make artificial intelligence accessible to the masses in various facets as an exciting challenge with great potential.

speakers.name: Sebastien Stormacq
speakers.title: Developer Experience Engineer
speakers.bio: As Developer Experience Engineer @ AWS, Seb is inspiring builders to unlock the value of the AWS cloud, using his secret blend of passion, enthusiasm, customer advocacy, curiosity, and creativity. Seb is focusing on  communities and technologies on AWS. He is a regular author ✍️ on the AWS News Blog, a public speaker 📣 at international conferences, and a podcaster with the weekly "podcast 🎙AWS en 🇫🇷" and "AWS Developers Podcast". Before joining the AWS Developer Experience team, he built & lead the AWS Technical Training team in EMEA, then he built & lead the Alexa Solution Architecture team in UK, France, Italy and Spain.

speakers.name: Sinduri Guntupalli
speakers.title: Program Manager
speakers.bio: Results-oriented Product Manager with background in web development, configuration management, web analytics and SEO. Passionate about open-source, particularly Drupal, and actively engaged in the community through volunteer work. Experienced in managing full product life-cycles and leading cross-functional teams. Known for strong organizational skills, clear communication, and optimize processes for efficient product delivery.

speakers.name: Sophie-Theresa Kleinke
speakers.title: Data Engineer
speakers.bio: Sophie-Theresa Kleinke is a Data Engineer and Business Analyst at Finanz Informatik, the IT service provider and digitalization partner of the Savings Banks Finance Group. With an academic background in mathematics, she focuses on the analysis and optimization of business processes, specializing in process mining methodologies. Her expertise spans the entire data value chain—from working with SQL and backend data structures to designing advanced reporting and data visualization solutions using modern business intelligence tools. Combining data engineering with business analysis, she enables data-driven decision-making and drives efficiency across complex financial systems.

speakers.name: Susanne Bach Ladefoged Hou
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Susanne (She/Her) is a C# backend engineer in the .NET stack who loves clean, maintainable code, automating the boring stuff, and letting tools like Renovate do the heavy lifting. She started her career as a full-stack developer, but these days she’s all about backend magic—automation, productivity, and building software for critical infrastructure that is reliable, robust, and built to keep things running. As an international conference speaker, Susanne shares clear, relatable stories about building software, working smarter with automation, and boosting developer productivity, all delivered in a practical, approachable, and yes, sometimes fun way. She also champions inclusive, neurodiverse-friendly environments in tech. When she’s not coding or speaking, Susanne enjoys exploring creative projects and slaying demons in Diablo IV. Her approach to work and life is rooted in curiosity, creativity, and finding joy in the process—whether that’s writing code, telling stories, or leveling up her character in-game.

speakers.name: Thomas Jung
speakers.title: Head of Developer Advocacy
speakers.bio: I lead a team focused on making life a little easier for SAP developers. We create tutorials, blog posts, videos, sample code, and show up at in-person events—all aimed at cutting through the noise and helping you actually build things. No fluff. Just tools and content that make you more effective.

speakers.name: Tolga Sümer
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: I’m a Senior Software Engineer based in Berlin, currently working at Pliant, where I build internal and customer-facing AI features. My focus is on designing end-to-end AI workflows that combine multiple agents, tools, and data sources — from BI-style database interactions to RAG-based systems. Before moving into AI, I spent several years working on integrations, building APIs and connecting Pliant with accounting and PayOps platforms like DATEV, Lexoffice, and Circula, as well as helping shape our Partner API. Before Pliant, I worked on large-scale backend systems in telecom and enterprise software, mainly around integrations, and high-throughput data pipelines. Earlier on, I explored game development and computer vision through internships and student roles, which helped shape my interest in building things end to end. I enjoy working on complex systems, turning ideas into production-ready solutions, and learning new technologies along the way.

speakers.name: Trecilla Logo
speakers.title: SVP People and Culture
speakers.bio: As SVP, People & Culture at Flip, Trecilla Lobo specializes in building workplace cultures that drive high performance. She brings over two decades of expertise in scaling global tech firms and is responsible for Flip’s end-to-end people strategy, ensuring that inclusivity and engagement remain at the heart of the company’s growth.

speakers.name: Vivek Kuppenhalli
speakers.title: Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: I am a Staff Engineer in the Mobile team at Redcare Pharmacy, specializing in Android application development and large-scale architecture design. I lead architectural transformations that enable teams to adopt multi-module strategies and take ownership of domain-driven development within a shared codebase. I advocate clean architectural patterns that improve scalability, build performance, and team autonomy. Recently, I have been exploring a feature-driven “micro frontend” approach across mobile and backend systems, leveraging Kotlin Multiplatform to share business logic while preserving native UI independence on Android and iOS.

speakers.name: Iurii Marymonskyi
speakers.title: Head of Engineering
speakers.bio: Engineering leader with 20+ years experience across iGaming, Investment Banking, and FinTech. Specializes in implementation of high-load web applications, distributed calculation systems, and large-scale data platforms. Brings end-to-end delivery experience - from architecture and requirements through team leadership, budgeting, and engineering best practices.

speakers.name: Predrag Radenkovic
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Staff engineer and technical lead with 15 years of experience at Silverfin, Toptal, and FIS. Two-time founding engineer, contributing to startups from early stage through unicorn valuation and acquisition.

speakers.name: Žan Jonke
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Žan Jonke is a software engineer at *codeplain, a company rethinking how software gets built through spec-driven AI code generation. With hands-on experience across machine learning, full-stack development, and leadership, he has built production AI systems across national security and medical imaging. At *codeplain, Žan is focused on scaling integration development through AI-powered specification-driven workflows eliminating boilerplate, automating implementation, and helping engineering teams build and ship integrations faster than ever before.

speakers.name: Carey Liu
speakers.title: Product Security Operation Specialist
speakers.bio: Carey Liu is a Product Security professional on TikTok's Global Security team, focusing on product security, AI-SDLC operations, and AI-native security governance. As part of the team, he is currently contributing to SEVA (Spec-driven Engineering Validation Agent), a product designed to bring security validation into product and technical design reviews earlier in the SDLC. With 10 years of experience across security product operations, technical program management, and DevSecOps solution pre-sales, Carey is passionate about making security more scalable, actionable, and developer-friendly. His work focuses on shift-left security and emerging spec-driven AI coding workflow, helping engineering teams build securely by design before code is written. Carey holds a Computer Science degree from the University of British Columbia and a Master’s degree in Information Security from UC Berkeley.

speakers.name: Haoqi Wu
speakers.title: Research Engineer
speakers.bio: Haoqi Wu is a research engineer at TikTok, where he works on building production-grade solutions for AI-powered services with regulatory compliance and user trust. His current work focuses on designing and deploying high-performance invisible watermarking algorithms and systems for AI-generated content provenance, ensuring robust content authentication that meets global regulatory requirements. He has contributed to projects spanning invisible watermarking for AIGC compliance, privacy-enhancing technologies for LLM inference/training services. His research interests lie at the intersection of deep learning and responsible AI. He is dedicated to building practical systems that balance technical performance, regulatory compliance, and user experience.

speakers.name: Lindsey Kelt Zikry
speakers.title: Business Development Lead
speakers.bio: Lindsey is the Global User Growth & Product Partnerships Lead at TikTok. In her capacity as Lead, Lindsey is responsible for innovating and scaling the TikTok app and product features through partnerships with the company's most strategic technology, telecommunications, and content partners. Lindsey has worked for TikTok across the New York and London offices.
