Before joining TikTok, Lindsey was based in NYC and led multi-billion dollar fundraising campaigns, growth strategy, and product management for the $420 billion Alternative Investments platform within the $14 trillion AUM asset manager, BlackRock. Lindsey received a MBA from The Wharton School at the University of Pennsylvania and a BA from Vanderbilt University. Lindsey is an avid squash and padel player and skier and enjoys traveling around the globe.

speakers.name: Mingshen Sun
speakers.title: Research and Engineering Lead
speakers.bio: Mingshen is leading application and innovations of the trusted/confidential computing technologies at TikTok. Previously, Mingshen worked on multiple projects towards building safe, secure and trustworthy systems. Notably, he was fortunate to initiate the Apache Teaclave open-source project. Mingshen got his PhD from the Chinese University of Hong Kong, and has broad research experience on topics at the intersection of privacy and security, operating systems, and programming languages. He also serves on Technical Advisory Council of Confidential Computing Consortium.

speakers.name: Anni Chen
speakers.title: Developer Evangelist
speakers.bio: Anni is an AI nerd, productivity hacker and Developer Evangelist at Twilio. Brewing rich developer experiences, blending AI with APIs, and serving the Python community across the UK and EMEA with a perfect mix of code and community. She's always hungry for the next great idea (and meal).

speakers.name: Jovana Dunisijevic
speakers.title: Senior Technical Evangelist
speakers.bio: Jovana is a Senior Technical Evangelist and a Software Engineer at Atlassian, where she bridges the gap between product engineering and the developer community. Based in Sydney, Jovana specialises in showing, not just telling, how modern development teams can work smarter with AI-native tooling. With a background spanning and developer advocacy and hands-on engineering in platform engineering, performance and scalability, she's passionate about making complex workflows tangible through live demos and practical, real-world use cases. She's a 3 times finalist for Tech awards in ANZ region.

speakers.name: Justin Reock
speakers.title: Deputy CTO
speakers.bio: Justin Reock is the Deputy CTO of DX (getdx.com) and is an engineer, speaker, writer, and software practice evangelist with over 20 years of experience working in various software roles. He is an outspoken thought leader, delivering enterprise solutions, numerous keynotes, technical leadership, various publications, and community education on developer experience and productivity.

speakers.name: Rafael Levi
speakers.title: Developer Relations Lead
speakers.bio: Rafael Levi is the Developer Relations lead at Bright Data, the world's #1 web data infrastructure for AI. With 8+ years at Bright Data, spanning sales, solution architecture, and infrastructure design, he brings rare full-stack perspective to developer advocacy. He builds SDKs, APIs, and MCP-based agent integrations that help developers and AI systems collect real-time public web data at scale, and channels community feedback directly into product improvements.

speakers.name: Alberts Jekabsons
speakers.title: Senior Developer Evangelist
speakers.bio: Alberts Jekabsons is a Developer Evangelist at HERE Technologies, the world’s leading mapping company, with over 12 years of experience working with location data. With a background in foundation engineering, he has built a strong foundation in geospatial data and production-ready pipelines on AWS, working with services like Lambda and S3 to process and manage location data at scale. His invested interest in data visualization led him to develop spatial analytics solutions as a React frontend developer, before shifting his focus to supporting developers in HERE’s growing developer ecosystem. At WeAreDevelopers, Alberts will help participants learn how to identify common failure points, design for reliability, and ship robust location-driven systems. About HERE Technologies HERE is the global leader in mapping and location technology. For over 40 years, we’ve been powering innovation for the world’s most recognizable companies: from launching our first digital map in 1985, to shaping the future of software-defined vehicles today. With the industry’s freshest and richest unified map and a portfolio of products, services and solutions that serve the needs of multiple industries, HERE reveals opportunities that drive progress and unlock new possibilities for every moving vehicle. Discover more at here.com.

speakers.name: April Yoho
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: April is a Senior Developer Advocate at GitHub, where she helps organizations modernize how they build, deploy, and operate software at scale. She specializes in application transformation and DevOps ways of working, with a strong focus on enabling enterprises to adopt modern, code-first practices using GitHub. April is actively involved in technical content creation and education, producing enterprise-focused blogs, videos, workshops, and presentations. Through speaking engagements, executive briefings, and community events, she shares practical, real-world insights to help teams successfully adopt modern DevOps practices. Outside of work, April enjoys spending time outdoors hiking, skiing, and scuba diving. She is also a competitive triathlete, participating in Ironman and Half Ironman events.

speakers.name: Ben De St Paer-Gotch
speakers.title: Director of Product
speakers.bio: Ben De St Paer-Gotch is the Director of Product at GitHub, where he oversees the strategic vision and development for core developer tools including GitHub Actions, Packages, Codespaces, Microsoft Dev Tunnels, and npm. Driven by a mission to improve developers' lives, Ben leads his teams in building seamless solutions that automate day-to-day workflows. In addition to his leadership role, Ben is a published author and an avid technologist who thrives on collaborating with passionate, forward-thinking people. Outside of the tech world, he enjoys spending time with his family and immersing himself in his love for all things Warhammer and Warhammer 40k.

speakers.name: Christoph Lohrmann
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Dr. Christoph Lohrmann is Senior Software Developer at Q.ANT, a deep-tech scale-up developing photonic processors that compute with light to enable energy-efficient AI and high-performance computing. At Q.ANT, he contributes to the development and application of next-generation computing architectures based on photonics and advanced algorithms. Previously, he conducted research in computational physics and complex systems, focusing on the behavior of self-propelled agents, bio-inspired motion strategies, and reinforcement learning approaches for micro-robotics. Christoph Lohrmann studied physics at the University of Stuttgart, where he developed a strong interdisciplinary background bridging physics, machine learning, and emerging computing technologies.

speakers.name: Thinzar Lin
speakers.title: Head of Developer Relations
speakers.bio: Ed is an engineer and marketer experienced in distributed ledger technologies (DLT), model-based design, and advanced vehicle systems. He specializes in translating complex systems into scalable, user-centric solutions. At Hashgraph, Ed helps grow the Hedera network by onboarding developers and partnering with organizations building the next generation of Web3 applications. He is passionate about leading interdisciplinary teams, fostering developer communities, and turning emerging technologies into practical impact. Ed holds a Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from Virginia Tech and a Master’s in Management from Harvard.

speakers.name: Ellen Jones
speakers.title: Customer Success Leader
speakers.bio: Ellen Abarr (Jones) is a Customer Success Manager at DX, Atlassian's developer intelligence platform, where she partners with enterprise engineering organizations to measure, benchmark, and improve developer productivity. Based in Tennessee, Ellen works directly with engineering leaders to identify workflow friction, quantify the impact of AI investments, and build data-driven strategies that improve developer experience at scale. She brings a practitioner's perspective on what it actually takes to shift engineering culture through measurement.

speakers.name: Evgenij Renke
speakers.title: Solutions Engineer
speakers.bio: Evgenij is a passionate technologist at the heart of GitHub's field organisation, Evgenij specialises in bridging the gap between technical complexity and real business value — helping companies navigate everything from GitHub Copilot adoption to security compliance and AI governance. Known for his hands-on approach and deep technical expertise, he brings both credibility and energy to the stage.

speakers.name: Isha Salania
speakers.title: AI Solution Engineer
speakers.bio: Isha Salania is a Cloud & AI Solution Engineer at Microsoft, with over six years of experience driving AI/ML innovation across the technology, automotive, and insurance sectors. Throughout her career, Isha has established a proven track record of delivering data-driven solutions and deploying transformative enterprise AI applications. Beyond her corporate engineering role, she is an active content creator dedicated to bridging the knowledge gap by sharing tech insights and practical study strategies. Isha is deeply passionate about demystifying artificial intelligence and leveraging it to create tangible, real-world impact.

speakers.name: Julia Kordick
speakers.title: Senior Software Global Black Belt
speakers.bio: Julia is a Software Global Black Belt at Microsoft, helping large-scale organizations accelerate dev workflows through pragmatic, end-to-end platform engineering. With a background as a Software Engineer, Dev Lead, and Cloud Solution Architect, she speaks fluent developer and management—and knows how to bridge the gap without losing either side in translation. She’s a big-picture thinker who also loves to understand the details. Web and frontend will always have a place in her ❤️. Outside work, Julia is a gamer, a proud fahrradmaus, and a former quarterback for a women’s American football team (yes, really).

speakers.name: Klaus-M. Schremser
speakers.title: CRO and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Co-founder of OtterlyAI — the AI search monitoring & GEO platform. Previously Head of Growth at Usersnap (acquired by saas.group), has.to.be ($300M exit), Wikidocs (acquired by Atlassian) and Gentics (acquired by APA). Building the category for how brands show up in ChatGPT, Perplexity & Google AI Overviews.

speakers.name: Luis Bitencourt-Emilio
speakers.title: SVP Product
speakers.bio: Luis is the SVP of the Copilot Agents Product, focused on making GitHub the place where humans and agents collaborate to develop software. With a career spanning leadership roles at Microsoft, Reddit, Rappi and Loft, he has a proven track record of scaling high-growth engineering, product, and data organizations. A lifelong builder, Luis started programming when he was eight years old and today also serves on the Board of Trustees for the Wikimedia Foundation.

speakers.name: Luke Forrest
speakers.title: Developer Relations Engineer
speakers.bio: Luke is a Developer Relations Engineer at Hashgraph, working directly with projects building on Hedera to make sure they have the tools, guidance, and support they need to build. Luke’s day-to-day is spent alongside builders: unblocking integration issues, feeding their requirements back into our SDKs and docs, and connecting them with the right people across the ecosystem. He has been working in DLT since 2020, across multiple projects tokenising real-world and financial assets, which keeps him close to the practical challenges teams hit when value starts moving on-chain.

speakers.name: Manuel Weichselbaum
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Manuel Weichselbaum is co-founder and CTO of anyconcept, a research company building a world model for software interfaces. His background spans physics, telematics, and computer science, and he has spent over a decade doing AI research before that was the obvious thing to do. In 2020 he began exploring a question that the industry had largely ignored: what if a model could learn to understand software purely through visual interaction data, the way a human does, without ever reading a line of code. That question became the Vision Action Model, a domain-specific foundation model trained on pixels, mouse, and keyboard data alone. Manuel leads all VAM research and architecture at anyconcept. GHOSTS, the first product built on the VAM, is already running in production for enterprise customers. He is based in Graz, Austria.

speakers.name: Marie Theresa Brosig
speakers.title: Strategic Solutions Engineer
speakers.bio: Marie Theresa has spent the past decade helping developers thrive by bridging the gap between technology and people — drawing on her background in both education and computer science. She has a proven track record of making complex tools feel usable and human, and now works with some of the largest enterprise customers in EMEA to make AI a true peer programmer: one that supports developers everywhere. Marie once helped run a cybersecurity book club, believes good documentation is a form of kindness, and rarely leaves the house without a paperback (or two) in tow.

speakers.name: Moe Sani
speakers.title: Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Moe is an Edge AI Solutions Architect at Edge Impulse (A Qualcomm Company), where he empowers partners and customers to bring cutting-edge machine learning into a diverse range of industries. Prior to Edge Impulse, Moe was leading the research and implementation of the first ML based navigation system for Dyson robots. With over 10 years of experience in robotics, machine learning, and embedded systems, Moe has a proven track record in architecting, leading, and implementing state-of-the-art AI and IoT solutions. His expertise spans predictive maintenance to smart automation, turning complex technologies into practical, real-world applications

speakers.name: Neena Thomas
speakers.title: Head of Platform
speakers.bio: Leading Enterprise platform focused on AI Governance, Data Security, Agent permissions & authorizations, observability & Analytics for AI AppGen to build secure apps, and create intelligent workflows & agents

speakers.name: Ondřej Kukla
speakers.title: Product Manager
speakers.bio: Ondřej Kukla runs product development of StellarBase, an AI-powered knowledge management platform currently in early access. He has spent over ten years in enterprise software, moving from data analytics and system integration into product ownership and team leadership. He is a Certified LeSS Practitioner trained by Craig Larman, co-creator of Large-Scale Scrum, and holds Scrum.org certifications in Product Ownership, Agile Leadership, and UX integrated into product discovery and the Scrum cycle. At StellarBase, he leads a team building something he believes the market is missing: a platform where organizational knowledge actually comes together instead of staying scattered across tools that don't talk to each other.

speakers.name: Raphael De Lio
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: My name is Raphael De Lio, a curious mind who can’t help but to try to improve whatever I touch. I’m a 30-something (and counting) software engineer, with over eight years hopping across roles, industries, and countries. Born under Brazilian skies, I spent six years in Portugal before landing in the Netherlands in 2022. My craft has been code: Java and Kotlin on distributed systems are my bread and butter, and I’ve also took part as organizer and host of the Dutch Kotlin User Group in Amsterdam. These days I’m a Developer Advocate at Redis, a role where I mix code, community, and creativity. I get to build, teach, and share sparks that help developers everywhere solve problems faster (and hopefully have more fun along the way). If you’re building something cool and need a hand, or just want to talk about Kotlin, Redis, databases, or community magic, I’m always up for a chat.

speakers.name: Samuel Agbede
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Hey there! 👋🏾 I’m Samuel, a Developer Advocate at Redis focused on building and explaining AI systems that actually work in the real world. I spend my time exploring how to design reliable AI agents, especially around memory, state, and retrieval. In practice, that means helping developers understand how to move beyond demos into systems that are scalable, observable, and useful in production. My background is in Applied AI and software engineering, with experience across machine learning, backend systems, and developer education. I’m particularly interested in how tools like Redis can act as the memory layer for modern AI applications, making agents more consistent, context-aware, and performant. I care a lot about clarity. Not just what AI can do, but how it behaves under real constraints like latency, cost, and failure. Most of my work is about breaking these ideas down into something practical that engineers can actually use. Alongside this, I host Stories That Shape Us, where I speak with professionals and creatives about the experiences and decisions that shape their lives and careers. At the core, I believe good technology should be understandable, dependable, and built to serve people.

speakers.name: Tugdual Grall
speakers.title: Strategic AI GTM Lead
speakers.bio: Tug helps organisations across the EMEA region unlock the full potential of AI-powered development. Tug is passionate about bringing GitHub Copilot to developer communities — from enterprise teams to local meetups. He regularly speaks and runs hands-on workshops on topics like agentic AI, vibe coding, and building custom Copilot agents, skills, and MCP integrations. Based in France, Tug combines deep technical expertise with a genuine love for sharing knowledge and empowering developers to build smarter and faster.

speakers.name: Elena Lucarelli
speakers.title: Principal AI DevX Lead
speakers.bio: Elena is a seasoned program manager with over 13 years of experience leading complex cross-functional initiatives in engineering organizations. Her career took a transformative turn during a collaboration with SAP Sustainability, where she led the creation of the SAP AI ethics policy, aligned with UNESCO’s Recommendation on the Ethics of AI—a project that sparked a deep passion for artificial intelligence and its broader ethical and societal implications. This enthusiasm brought Elena to the forefront of AI innovation. Most recently, Elena founded and built the AI.ccelerate program at SAP Signavio, an initiative designed to empower developers by integrating agentic coding practices into their daily workflows. Through this program, Elena bridges cutting-edge AI with practical, responsible implementation in enterprise environments.

speakers.name: Kiran Panchal
speakers.title: AI Solution Engineer
speakers.bio: Kiran is an AI & APPS Solution Engineer at Microsoft, working with enterprise customers, especially in the automotive industry EMEA to design and scale AI-driven applications. She focuses on translating complex business challenges into practical, production-ready solutions using Microsoft Foundry, AI Services , frameworks and modern agent architectures. With deep hands-on experience across architecture design, workshops, and real-world deployments, she operates at the intersection of business and engineering bridging the gap between strategy and execution without losing pace on either side. When she isn’t debugging runtime telemetry or configuring Foundry Agents, Kiran channels her focus into martial arts, music, and canvas art. She tackles rogue bugs and heavy enterprise scale with the same grit, curiosity, and zero-sugarcoating mindset she used to navigate moving across the globe from India to Germany.

speakers.name: Lisa Becker
speakers.title: Solutions Engineer
speakers.bio: Lisa Becker is a Solutions Engineer at GitHub, where she helps corporate customers across EMEA evaluate, adopt, and get the most out of the GitHub platform. She specializes in translating real-world technical challenges into clear solutions, partnering with sales, product, and engineering teams to drive platform adoption at scale. Her background is in machine learning and applied AI. She holds a master’s degree in Cognitive Systems from the University of Potsdam, with hands-on industry experience in natural language processing, large language models, and recommendation systems. This technical foundation gives her a unique perspective on the increasingly AI-driven conversations shaping how organizations build software today. Alongside her work at GitHub, Lisa develops on-demand courses and facilitates live webinars on NLP and LLMs for O’Reilly Media. She is a Google Women Techmakers Ambassador, a certified #IAmRemarkable facilitator, and a passionate advocate for diversity, equity, and inclusion in tech.

speakers.name: Ricardo Niepel
speakers.title: Senior Solution Engineer
speakers.bio: Ricardo Niepel is a Senior Solution Engineer at Microsoft, covering software development topics at Azure for large enterprises in Germany. Being 13+ years at Microsoft he worked with a lot of different development teams across enterprises, distributed worldwide, helping enterprise AI developers unlock the full potential of Microsoft’s AI-powered stack across every stage of the development lifecycle. I collaborate closely with engineering leaders and platform teams to be successful with Microsoft Foundry, Azure AI, and Responsible AI, through hands-on engagements.

speakers.name: Anastasija Removic
speakers.title: Senior Talent Manager
speakers.bio: Senior Talent Manager at Liberty Global

speakers.name: Daniel Branski
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Daniel Branski is a Lead Engineer at Finanz Informatik, the central IT service provider of the German Savings Banks Finance Group, responsible for their Data & Intelligence Platform. He led the architectural transformation of their central data warehouse from mainframe-based infrastructure to a modern, decentralized architecture. His expertise and enthusiasm range from enterprise-wide system design all the way down to query-level optimization.

speakers.name: Edvin Teskeredzic
speakers.title: Senior AI Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Edvin Teskeredzic is a Senior AI Software Engineer at Infobip, working on developer tooling, streaming data, ML system design, and AI agents. With a background in Electrical Engineering and experience across both academia and industry, he focuses on building systems that make complex technical workflows easier, faster, and more observable. Edvin works on improving developer experience through practical tooling, inference optimization, and observability for AI-powered systems. Outside of work, he enjoys pub quizzes, science fiction, and probably optimizing things that did not need optimizing.

speakers.name: Hareesha Narayana Shirankallu
speakers.title: Managing Director
speakers.bio: Hareesha Narayana Shirankallu is the Managing Director at Bosch Global Software Technologies GmbH, leading the European business. With over 22 years at Bosch, he brings extensive experience across automotive software, engineering, and global delivery models. He has led multidisciplinary teams across geographies and played a key role in building large-scale nearshore and offshore delivery centers, including in India and Vietnam. Starting his career as a software engineer, he has contributed to innovation initiatives and holds patents that are in production within Bosch products. He is passionate about building high-performing teams and believes people-centric leadership drives sustainable business success.

speakers.name: Jemiah Sius
speakers.title: Senior Director Developer Relations
speakers.bio: I am a Full-stack Engineer turned DevRel Engineer at New Relic. With a background in software engineering and marketing direction, my experience involves: designing and developing user interfaces, addressing customer/client pain points, marketing automation, and community development.

speakers.name: Josh Hobson
speakers.title: Senior Engineer
speakers.bio: Josh is a developer at AG Grid, where he's been building AG Studio — a new dashboard library with multi-agent AI baked in. He's a mathematician by training and a maker by instinct, with a particular love for developer tooling, type systems, and intelligent interfaces. Outside of work, Josh can be found tinkering with 3D printers, ski touring in the Alps, or out on long walks with his dog.

speakers.name: Jovana Dunisijevic
speakers.title: Senior Technical Evangelist
speakers.bio: Jovana is a Senior Technical Evangelist and a Software Engineer at Atlassian, where she bridges the gap between product engineering and the developer community. Based in Sydney, Jovana specialises in showing, not just telling, how modern development teams can work smarter with AI-native tooling. With a background spanning and developer advocacy and hands-on engineering in platform engineering, performance and scalability, she's passionate about making complex workflows tangible through live demos and practical, real-world use cases. She's a 3 times finalist for Tech awards in ANZ region.

speakers.name: Karin Krobath
speakers.title: Founding Partner
speakers.bio: Dr. Karin Krobath is founding partner of identifire® – Austria's first agency for Employer Branding and Corporate Culture. Her focus for 20 years: start from the inside. She turns brands into movements, employees into ambassadors, and "yeah-whatever" sayers into genuine fans. With sharp positioning and strong formats – including Learning Journeys for innovation enthusiasts to Nairobi and Kigali. identifire.at – 20 years of corporate culture. Now also prompt.

speakers.name: Leandro Gomes da Silva
speakers.title: Founder
speakers.bio: Leandro Gomes da Silva is the founder of The AI Laundry and Head of AI Operations at a European recruitment firm, where he built a talent intelligence platform for candidate outreach, job descriptions, and interview optimization. No engineering team. No enterprise software contract. His AI outreach assistant doubled candidate response rates for a 3-person team. He works with HR and recruitment teams across Europe to fix broken processes and build AI tools they run themselves. He speaks on AI implementation for non-technical teams, vibe coding, and the future of talent operations. Returning speaker at WeAreDevelopers World Congress, with previous stages including HREX Vienna, TNW, and Recruiting Brainfood. He's the person to find if your team has tried AI and it isn't sticking.

speakers.name: Leszek Włodarski
speakers.title: IT Deputy Director
speakers.bio: Hi, my name is Leszek Włodarski, you can find more about me at https://www.linkedin.com/in/leszekwlodarski/. I am passionate enigineering manager/director, still with hands-on experience. I feel that proper architecture, efficient SSDLC pipeline and development processes are curcial to make our developers life better. I am not afraid of big things and challanges. 23 years of work in one company makes my experience and perspective on Software Development unique or simply special.

speakers.name: Nimrod Kor
speakers.title: Co-founder and CTO
speakers.bio: Nimrod Kor is a software engineer specializing in code analysis, cloud security, and developer tooling. As the #1 contributor to Checkov, he has helped shape best practices in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) security. His work on open-source projects like Checkov, Yor, and Airiam—focused on creating and analyzing code—has been central to his past talks.

speakers.name: Roland Odorfer
speakers.title: Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Roland is a Solutions Architect at Amazon Web Services (AWS), based in Berlin, Germany. He works with German industry and manufacturing customers, helping them architect secure and scalable solutions on AWS.

speakers.name: Sagar Sharma .
speakers.title: Research Scientist
speakers.bio: Sagar Sharma is a Research Scientist with TikTok Privacy Innovation Lab with expertise on differential privacy and privacy measurement. He is the research lead for the PET ARENA program - series of privacy technology competitions focused on stress-testing privacy-enhancing technologies (PETs) through adversarial red teaming.

speakers.name: Steven Mi
speakers.title: Senior MLOps Engineer
speakers.bio: Steven is a Software Engineer with 5+ years of experience building and productionizing machine learning systems that solve high-impact business problems. He has worked across domains including user acquisition, customer retention, content generation, supply optimization, and lead generation. Currently at GetYourGuide, he is part of the Machine Learning and AI Platform team, where he focuses on enabling teams to independently build and operate production-grade LLM systems. Outside of work, Steven co-organizes the AI in Production meetup in Berlin, a community dedicated to showcasing real-world AI applications across industries. In his free time, he enjoys exploring food and building tools he'd otherwise have to pay for.

speakers.name: Szymon Kulec
speakers.title: Lead Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: I believe that simplicity strongly resonates with a good architecture and results in an amazing performance of both: the engineering and the product itself. I like my data to be served fast. I don't allocate before evening.

speakers.name: Tom Wheeler
speakers.title: Principal Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Alternating between software engineering and technical education roles, Tom Wheeler's career spans more than 25 years in the financial, healthcare, defense, and tech industries. Prior to joining Temporal, he created training courses at Cloudera, developed aerospace engineering software at Object Computing, Inc. (OCI), helped design and implement a distributed system for high-volume data processing at WebMD, and wrote code for some of the earliest web applications at A.G. Edwards. Tom enjoys learning new things and teaching them to others, which has led to presentations at tech conferences around the world. When Tom manages to step away from the computer, you can probably find him cooking, traveling, or playing guitar.

speakers.name: Alexander Schwarz
speakers.title: Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Alexander Schwarz is a Robotics Solutions Architect at NVIDIA, based in Zurich, Switzerland, where he helps customers and developers accelerate AI training and robotics simulation with NVIDIA Cosmos and Isaac. Alexander holds an M.Sc. in Robotics, Cognition, and Intelligence from the Technical University of Munich, and conducted research in computer vision and robot learning at ETH Zurich, Johns Hopkins University, and Delft University of Technology. He is passionate about guiding developers and product teams to build next-generation Physical AI workflows. At NVIDIA, Alexander specialises in the Cosmos World Foundation Model platform — enabling developers to generate diverse synthetic data at scale, train robust robot policies, and validate them in simulation before real-world deployment. He regularly delivers hands-on DLI workshops and technical talks at leading developer conferences including WeAreDevelopers, GTC, and SIGGRAPH, covering topics from physical AI simulation pipelines to industrial digital twins with Omniverse.

speakers.name: Amit Kushwaha
speakers.title: Principal Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Amit Kushwaha is a deep learning solutions architect at NVIDIA, specializing in inference optimization and production deployment of LLM and agentic AI systems. Previously, he served as Director of AI Engineering at SambaNova Systems, architecting Generative AI solutions on novel accelerator hardware, and as Principal Data Scientist at ExxonMobil, applying machine learning to complex physical and industrial systems. He holds a Ph.D. in Engineering from Stanford University, where his research focused on scientific computing and large-scale simulations.

speakers.name: Antonio Rueda-Toicen
speakers.title: Senior Developer Relations Manager
speakers.bio: Senior Machine Learning Engineer with experience in industry and academia. I have led teams that built data products with millions of users. I have mentored over 300 professionals in the field of machine learning. Skilled in Computer Vision, Data Science, and Software Engineering.

speakers.name: Mohak Chadha
speakers.title: Solutions Architect AI
speakers.bio: Mohak Chadha is a Solutions Architect at NVIDIA, based in Munich, where he focuses on designing solutions for enterprise AI applications. Before this, he was at Firebolt, a unicorn startup, where he managed their cloud infrastructure and worked on improving security. With a background in distributed systems, Mohak specializes in cloud computing, parallel computing, and high-performance computing. He shares his expertise in distributed systems by presenting at major developer conferences, including KubeCon + CloudNativeCon and the Open Source Summit.

speakers.name: Sergio Perez
speakers.title: AI Solution Architect
speakers.bio: Sergio Perez specializes in conversational AI. He has experience in optimizing training and inference of LLMs, building retrieval-augmented generation systems, and working with companies in different sectors to leverage AI. His area of expertise at NVIDIA is quantization and inference serving. Previously, he worked as an AI engineer at Graphcore and Amazon, and got a Ph.D. in computational fluid dynamics from Imperial College London.

speakers.name: Ed Marquez
speakers.title: Head of Developer Relations
speakers.bio: Ed is an engineer and marketer experienced in distributed ledger technologies (DLT), model-based design, and advanced vehicle systems. He specializes in translating complex systems into scalable, user-centric solutions. At Hashgraph, Ed helps grow the Hedera network by onboarding developers and partnering with organizations building the next generation of Web3 applications. He is passionate about leading interdisciplinary teams, fostering developer communities, and turning emerging technologies into practical impact. Ed holds a Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from Virginia Tech and a Master’s in Management from Harvard.

speakers.name: Shilpa Shankar
speakers.title: Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Shilpa works as Developer Advocate empowering developers with cutting-edge technologies across SAP Business Technology Platform, including ABAP Cloud, RAP, Agentic AI and Generative AI. Her work focuses on bridging the gap between engineering teams and the global developer community through technical enablement, hands-on learning, and real-world innovation. Shilpa has delivered keynote sessions at SAP TechEd and actively contributes to global developer communities through deep-dive workshops, technical content, and developer programs across the world.

speakers.name: Adrian Mouat
speakers.title: Technical Community Advocate
speakers.bio: Adrian has been involved with containers from the early days of Docker and authored the O’Reilly book “Using Docker”. He works at Chainguard whose mission is to make the software lifecycle secure by default. His current focus is on improving the standard of security and provenance guarantees in container images.

speakers.name: Seth Webster
speakers.title: Chief Developer Evangelist
speakers.bio: Seth Webster is a technology executive, software architect, and open source leader serving as Executive Director of the React Foundation and Chief Developer Evangelist at Expo. Formerly the head of React at Meta, he has spent more than 20 years leading engineering organizations and shaping developer platforms used by millions worldwide. Seth is passionate about AI, developer experience, and helping people bring their ideas to life through technology.

speakers.name: Ross Kukulinski
speakers.title: VP of Product
speakers.bio: Ross Kukulinski is Vice President of Product at Tailscale, where he leads product strategy and execution across the company's secure connectivity platform. Ross is a technical product leader who builds businesses at the intersection of open source, cloud infrastructure, and developer experience. He has spent nearly two decades working on cloud-native technologies, helping organizations move from early adoption to reliable, large-scale production use. His work has focused on turning complex distributed systems into products that are easier to adopt, operate, and trust—without hiding the underlying engineering or limiting flexibility. Before joining Tailscale, Ross was VP of Product Management at Kong, where he led the transformation from packaged on-prem software to a cloud-first platform. Prior to Kong, he spent four years at VMware following the acquisition of Heptio, where he was an initial employee. At Heptio and VMware, he led product for Kubernetes platforms including Tanzu Kubernetes Grid, working closely with engineering teams and customers to shape how modern infrastructure is packaged, delivered, and operated. Ross is passionate about building products that balance strong technical foundations with clear customer value, mentoring future product leaders, and earning developer trust through great experiences.

speakers.name: Ainhoa Arruabarrena Ortiz
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Ainhoa is a Senior Software Engineer at Twilio specializing in cloud infrastructure. Over the past four years, she has driven initiatives to modernize Twilio's public and internal edge infrastructure, executing a long-term vision that has systematically elevated security and performance standards. As an infrastructure owner, she and her team build the tooling that abstracts away complexity, enabling internal teams to seamlessly deploy their own ingresses while ensuring a reliable, high-performance experience for end users. When she isn't modernizing edge routing, Ainhoa can usually be found away from the terminal—either hitting the basketball court or out on a trail hiking.

speakers.name: Alejandro Albuerne
speakers.title: Cofounder & CPO
speakers.bio: I’m Alex, CPO and co-founder at Resizes, building infrastructure that removes operational friction for scaling companies. We help teams ship faster, control cloud costs, and scale confidently without vendor lock-in.

speakers.name: Alija Nuredini
speakers.title: Founder and CEO
speakers.bio: Alija Nuredini is the Founder and CEO of Pistacio and DesignOps Lead at 8reasons Digital, a design agency. He built Pistacio to solve a problem he saw firsthand: service businesses juggling too many disconnected tools for time tracking, task management, expenses, and team coordination. Combining a founder's instinct with a builder's hands-on approach, he shipped a full operations platform serving real service teams. He has the scope decisions, dead ends, and lessons to prove it.

speakers.name: Andrew Boyagi
speakers.title: Customer CTO
speakers.bio: Andrew Boyagi is the Customer CTO and Head of DevOps Evangelism at Atlassian, where he helps some of the world’s largest organizations optimize their developer experience and productivity. With over 20 years of experience leading multidisciplinary technology teams—including a pivotal role at the Commonwealth Bank of Australia, where he built and scaled a platform engineering function supporting 7,000 engineers—Andrew brings a practical, real-world perspective to DevOps and software delivery. At Atlassian, Andrew regularly partners with Fortune 500 companies, sharing insights on how to remove friction, boost engineering velocity, and create high-performing, engaged teams. He is a frequent speaker at major industry events, and his thought leadership explores how AI and modern practices can transform the developer journey. Andrew holds an MBA from Southern Cross University and is passionate about empowering teams to achieve more.

speakers.name: Anton Vedeshin, Ph.D.
speakers.title: Founder
speakers.bio: Anton Vedeshin, Ph.D. is a serial entrepreneur, TEDx speaker, and investor. He holds a Ph.D. in cloud computing and cybersecurity and has authored scientific papers and patents across NLP, blockchain, and data protection (IEEE, ACM, Springer). He co-founded Vespia, a KYB and business verification platform acquired by Veriff in February 2026, and previously taught Cloud Computing and Data Security at TalTech while helping design e-government and security systems for Estonia and the European Commission. Today Anton is focused on Snoika - an AI marketing engine built for a new reality: AI is the first place people discover products. Snoika builds brand context for LLMs and makes companies show up in answers across ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity, and Google AI. Unlike generic AI writers, Snoika produces expert-structured, human-sounding content designed to be cited, trusted, and ranked - reinforced through structured signals and continuous testing. Anton has worked with teams including NASA, Google, Microsoft, Bosch, BMW, Cisco, and 15% of Global 2000 companies. That same systems thinking now powers how Snoika engineers visibility in AI search.

speakers.name: Antonella Mei-Pochtler
speakers.title: CEO and Executive Chairwoman
speakers.bio: Antonella is an Italian executive and multiple board member with many years of strategy consulting experience at BCG, where she became one of the youngest partners and a member of its global executive committee. Her work focus is in media, technology, and consumer goods. She was named amongst the top 25 consultants worldwide by Consulting Magazine and was honored with the Women Leaders in Consulting Lifetime Achievement Award in 2013. From 2018 to 2022, she was Special Advisor to the Austrian Chancellor and headed the ThinkAustria strategy unit. She also co-chaired the Kofi Annan Award for Innovation in Africa. Today, she is CEO of iSi Group, Executive Vice Chairwoman of Pochtler Industrieholding and contributes her in-depth transformation expertise as non-executive director in international supervisory boards (Generali, Publicis, Westwing, and DEAG). Previously, amongst other positions, she was Chairwoman of the Supervisory Board of Wolford and an IFC-nominated director at the Ivorian company SIPRA. She is strongly engaged in the non-profit sector, among others as a Vice-President of the European Forum Alpbach (EFA) and co-founder of the Sovereign Europe Forum, board member at TUM Venture Labs and advisory board member of the Drucker Forum. She has been committed to equal opportunities in education for many years (Teach For All, Business@School, Phorms Schools) and has published essays on leadership and strategy (“Acupuncture for Management”). Antonella studied in Munich and Rome and has an MBA with distinction from INSEAD. She lives with her family in Vienna.

speakers.name: Axel Ebert
speakers.title: Founding Partner
speakers.bio: Mag. Axel Ebert is founding partner of wortwelt® and co-creator of identifire®. With experience across more than 300 wording projects for financial services, industry, telecoms, NGOs and public administration, he has established himself as the "Letter Man" – and, since adopting AI, also as 'Chief Prompting Officer'. The only C-level title you actually have to earn every single day. Motto: from artificial to creative intelligence. wortwelt.at – we craft language. AI pitches in. Jargon-free.

speakers.name: Ben Hutchison
speakers.title: Forward Deploy Engineer
speakers.bio: Ben has a strong background in software security having worn many hats from ethical hacker, penetration tester and security consultant to application security strategist focused on secure software development programs and maturity assessments. He is co-author of multiple BSIMM annual reports and the P-SSCRM framework, with a decade-plus advising security teams on SSDLC, SSDF, and security champions programs.

speakers.name: Boris Hecker
speakers.title: Managing Director
speakers.bio: Boris Hecker is the Managing Director of Atos Germany and in this role, he directs the strategy and operational execution of the business activities of Atos in Germany. He also heads the Public Sector and Defense industry in Germany in addition to his overall responsibility for business growth in Germany, Austria, and Central Europe (Poland and Czech Republic).

speakers.name: Brian Lovin
speakers.title: Product Designer
speakers.bio: Brian Lovin is a software designer making AI products at Notion. He previously co-founded Campsite and Spectrum, spent four years designing GitHub's mobile apps, and designed payments experiences at Facebook. Outside of work you'll find him tinkering on side projects and writing about software design.

speakers.name: Carl Fagerlin
speakers.title: Forward Deploy Engineer
speakers.bio: Carl is a cloud architect who has worked across IoT, smart homes, smart locks, distributed systems, and global cloud governance at IKEA, ASSA ABLOY, and Axis. He has used coding agents since they became useful enough to change how software gets built, and now helps Oplane’s customers apply agentic threat modeling to real systems and real security problems.

speakers.name: Cole Groom
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: Co-founder of Balance Breathing

speakers.name: Daniel Geisel
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Daniel Geisel is CEO of Mercedes‑Benz Tech Innovation and Head of Home of Tech. In this role, he is responsible for the global software organization within Mercedes‑Benz IT, driving technological development and innovation in close collaboration with the business functions. Under his leadership, the organization is evolving from individual tech hubs into a globally integrated software tech organization, combining software engineering excellence, digital solutions and strong execution capabilities. He works closely with international teams across locations including Spain, South Africa, India and Malaysia.

speakers.name: David Soria Parra
speakers.title: Member of Technical Staff
speakers.bio: David Soria Parra is a Member of Technical Staff at Anthropic based in London. He co-created the Model Context Protocol (MCP). He leads its technical direction, governance, and ecosystem development. Before joining Anthropic in 2024, David spent nearly a decade at Meta leading engineering organizations, and served as an Entrepreneur in Residence at Sutter Hill Ventures. He is a long-time open-source contributor with a track record spanning PHP, Git, and Mercurial.

speakers.name: Dominik Krichbaum
speakers.title: Product Manager
speakers.bio: Dominik is a Product Manager at ING, where he works we a team composed of multiple entities on platform capabilities for safer and more scalable software delivery. After leading products around process automation and testing capabilities, he now drives a broader initiative towards Continuous Delivery for a complex banking environment with multiple technologies in use, teams and regulatory requirements.

speakers.name: Enris Nogare von Tein
speakers.title: Team Lead
speakers.bio: Born in Brazil, loving tech since very young! Graduated in Computer Science and worked for ExxonMobil for a bit, afterwards came to Germany to work at e.Solutions and then finally at EGYM SE! Magic the Gathering aficionado and hoping to lead bigger and bigger teams in the future!

speakers.name: Hergy Fongue
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Hergy Fongue is a Lead Developer at Finanz Informatik, the central IT service provider of the German Savings Banks Finance Group. He plays a key role in the transformation of the central data warehouse from mainframe-based infrastructure to a modern, decentralized architecture. His focus lies on building modern cloud-native core components for business-rule-driven data services. This involves ensuring both the semantic integrity of data and the flexible processing of individual records as well as large files.

speakers.name: Kristjan Ulst
speakers.title: Developer Productivity
speakers.bio: Kristjan Ulst leads Developer Productivity and AI adoption at Arrive, where his team supports 800+ engineers across 90+ countries. He builds the platforms, tooling, and AI workflows that help teams ship faster, safer, and with less friction. His career sits at the intersection of AI and engineering productivity. At MindTitan, he led teams that shipped AI products for telecom, government, and education. At Arrive, he applies that experience at scale, driving AI coding tool adoption across the engineering org, from strategy and enablement to guardrails and measurement. Before that, he co-founded two startups (Fanvestory, acquired, and Fairmus) and ran a CTO-as-a-service practice for over a decade. He has built products from zero to one and scaled engineering cultures from startup to enterprise. What he cares about: making engineers' lives easier, building teams with real ownership and autonomy, and treating AI adoption as a culture change, not a tool rollout.

speakers.name: Luisa Rink
speakers.title: Product Engineer
speakers.bio: Luisa Rink is a Product Engineer in ING where she plays a key role in shaping customer‑centric digital products. With a strong foundation in change projects and cross‑functional collaboration, she helps teams move beyond feature delivery toward creating meaningful customer impact.

speakers.name: Malte Lantin
speakers.title: Strategic Solutions Engineer
speakers.bio: Malte Lantin is a Strategic Solutions Engineer at GitHub, where he helps large enterprises adopt modern software engineering practices, AI-powered developer workflows, and platform engineering at scale. Malte is based in Germany and brings more than 15 years of experience spanning software development, technical evangelism, program management, and solutions engineering. He is also the co-host of the German software engineering podcast TodoCast, where he discusses developer productivity, emerging technologies, and industry trends.

speakers.name: Malte Lensch
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Malte Lensch started using Lovable and Claude in spring 2025, and it changed his life. What began as an experiment to rebuild his own consulting website turned into a production-grade workflow now running for multiple clients. Mainly helping companies now kill their WordPress and Webflow setups and replace them with modern AI-native stacks that actually ship. He's Managing Director at Till Freitag Consulting, a Berlin-based consultancy specialising in AI-native workflows and automation. In his spare time, he is spending more and more time in the garden.

speakers.name: Mara Kiefer
speakers.title: Senior Research Engineer
speakers.bio: Mara is a multidisciplinary professional with a background in both the natural sciences as well as the technology industry. She has a passion for helping others to tackle challenging problems and build elegant solutions and is an active advocate for innovation and open source.

speakers.name: Marc Teipel
speakers.title: AI Solution Engineer
speakers.bio: Marc specializes in bridging the gap between technical execution and executive vision by "speaking AI" to everyone from developers to C-suite leaders. Driven by a deep curiosity and a passion for emerging technology, Marc excels at uniting enthusiastic teams to uncover the full potential of artificial intelligence. Outside of his professional life, Marc loves pushing his limits by signing up for intense sports events (even if he occasionally questions that decision the next morning).

speakers.name: Mario Román Dono
speakers.title: Software Engineer
speakers.bio: Mario is a Software Engineer on the Edge Services team at Twilio, where he works on building the company’s high-volume, public-facing edge infrastructure. Previously he worked at EPAM, where he developed large-scale media software for several of the biggest European tier-1 TV operators. Specializing in Infrastructure and Platform Engineering, he focuses on creating developer platforms that help internal customers ship software at a higher pace, while also ensuring a reliable, high-performance experience for the end users relying on those services.

speakers.name: Marius Obert
speakers.title: Developer Evangelist
speakers.bio: Marius' passion is exploring the latest communication technologies – and, even more, sharing his knowledge and learning from others. Originally from the Black Forest in Germany, he started his software development career as a UI developer in Silicon Valley, where he discovered his love for web technologies, especially JavaScript and the entire Node.js ecosystem. After countless creative (and sometimes frustrating) battles with CSS, he relocated to Munich, where he now works as a Developer Evangelist at Twilio. In this role, he inspires and empowers developers around the world to build cloud-native apps with outstanding customer engagement. He has also spent nearly a decade working on enterprise frontend development and even wrote a book on the topic. Whether on stage, in workshops, or through hands-on prototypes, Marius loves exchanging ideas with fellow developers and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. When he’s not doing that, he dives into the world of Web 3.0 – always on the lookout for the next exciting technology.

speakers.name: Mateusz Gren
speakers.title: Head of Software Development
speakers.bio: Mateusz Gren is Head of Software Development at Atos in Austria, where he leads large-scale software and AI initiatives with teams of several hundred engineers. With a background in Business Informatics and more than a decade of hands-on experience as a software architect, developer, and engineering leader, he operates at the intersection of architecture, execution, and business impact. Beyond enterprise systems, he actively builds and operates his own AI-assisted applications using modern web stacks, applying production-grade thinking to personal productivity systems - building and evolving personal software systems for sports performance, finance, and decision-making using generative AI and minimal code. His talks focus on what actually works, what breaks, and how developers can stay in control while leveraging AI as a genuine multiplier rather than a buzzword.

speakers.name: Matthew Brady
speakers.title: Sales Engineering Manager
speakers.bio: A seasoned Presales Engineer with over 30 years of experience, specialising in Application Security (AppSec), Supply Chain, and Performance Engineering. Throughout a long-standing career, I have partnered with organisations to design and deliver secure, scalable, and high-performing solutions that address complex business and technical challenges. Known for bridging the gap between business needs and technical execution, I bring deep domain expertise, strategic insight, and a customer-focused approach to every engagement.

speakers.name: Maximilian Jesch
speakers.title: Outbound Product Manager
speakers.bio: Maximilian Jesch is an AI Engineer and Developer Advocate at IBM, where he focuses on Agentic AI and the watsonx ecosystem. In his role, he helps developers and enterprises understand, build, and scale AI solutions, while also serving as a technical leader for IBM Bob. Beyond his work at IBM, Max is active in the broader AI community, sharing insights through open-source projects. His work reflects a blend of hands-on engineering, advocacy, and community leadership in modern AI development.

speakers.name: Mike Bursell
speakers.title: Executive Director
speakers.bio: Mike Bursell is the Executive Director of the Confidential Computing Consortium. He is one of the co-founders of the Enarx project (https://enarx.dev), and was CEO and co-founder of Profian, a start-up based on Enarx. He has previously served on the Governing Boards of the CCC and the Bytecode Alliance and currently holds advisory board roles with various start-ups. Previous companies include Red Hat, Intel and Citrix, with roles in security, virtualisation and networking. Mike is author of "Trust in Computer Systems and the Cloud", published by Wiley in 2021.

speakers.name: Panley Cooze
speakers.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
speakers.bio: I'm panley, first and foremost I'm a professional hobbyist. I strive to learn about the technologies used in web infrastructure, from beautiful frontends to GPU backends. I endeavour to teach those around me to uplift the developer community, kickstart founders and make a better internet. I have history in Electrical Engineering, all the way to Trust & Safety!

speakers.name: Peter Hock
speakers.title: VP Digital Applications
speakers.bio: Peter Hock is Head of Digital Applications for Germany, Austria, Czechia and Poland. As a senior executive in digital applications and software-driven transformation, he brings more than 20 years of experience in IT services and consulting—on both the customer and provider side—and across international business environments. He leads global delivery teams across on-, near-, and offshore and helps organizations design, build, and operate business-critical digital solutions end to end. His focus is not on technology for its own sake, but on how organizations translate strategy into execution and measurable outcomes. He specializes in structuring complex initiatives across the full lifecycle—from idea and requirements to delivery, operation, and continuous optimization. Peter is particularly driven by challenging implementations—where complexity, scale, and real-world constraints demand clear ownership, strong governance, and tight alignment between business, users, and engineering. ------------ Mateusz Gren is Head of Software Development at Atos in Austria and a driving force behind large-scale digital transformation in enterprise environments. Starting his career as a software developer, he brings a hands-on perspective to leading complex programs that combine modern software engineering, DevOps, and AI. In recent years, Mateusz has been at the forefront of integrating generative AI into software development - from code generation and automated testing to new collaboration models between engineers and AI systems. His focus is not on hype, but on making AI work in real-world enterprise projects with strict requirements for governance, quality, and scalability. As a speaker, he shares practical insights on how organizations can adopt AI in software engineering without losing control. His perspective bridges technology, delivery, and leadership - with one goal: building better software, faster.

speakers.name: Peter Kröner
speakers.title: Supreme Commander
speakers.bio: Peter Kröner is an independent trainer for front-end web technologies. He spends his working days reading dry specifications, fighting buggy browsers, and doing workshops on the latest and greatest web standards.

speakers.name: Rene Schenk
speakers.title: Software Architect
speakers.bio: René is a software architect and lead developer at iQMatix. For more than 20 years, René has been helping clients in the insurance, healthcare and retail sectors with architectural decisions, design and implementation.

speakers.name: Rudi Bauer
speakers.title: CCO
speakers.bio: CCO of WeAreDevelopers

speakers.name: Salih Guler
speakers.title: Senior Developer Advocate
speakers.bio: Salih is a Senior Developer Advocate at AWS and Google Developer Expert with over 10 years of experience spanning fullstack, mobile, and cloud development. He specializes in bridging complex engineering challenges with developer communities through hands-on code, content, and technical strategy.

speakers.name: Sergio Perez
speakers.title: Senior Solution Architect
speakers.bio: Sergio Perez specializes in conversational AI. He has experience in optimizing training and inference of LLMs, building retrieval-augmented generation systems, and working with companies in different sectors to leverage AI. His area of expertise at NVIDIA is quantization and inference serving. Previously, he worked as an AI engineer at Graphcore and Amazon, and got a Ph.D. in computational fluid dynamics from Imperial College London.

speakers.name: Stefanie Manzinger
speakers.title: Senior Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Stefanie Manzinger is a Senior Solutions Architect for Automotive and Manufacturing at NVIDIA. In her role, she helps customers accelerate physical AI development with NVIDIA Cosmos and drives the adoption of generative AI for industrial applications. Previously, as a Development Engineer at MAN Truck & Bus SE, she focused on developing autonomous driving solutions. Stefanie holds a Ph.D. from the Technical University of Munich, where her doctoral research specialized in decision-making and trajectory planning for autonomous vehicles. She is passionate about supporting developers in adopting and improving physical AI systems, driven by her interest in the evolving role of foundation models within industrial AI and robotics.

speakers.name: Xuan Huang · 黄玄
speakers.title: Software Architect
speakers.bio: Xuan Huang (@huxpro) is the Architect of Lynx at ByteDance, an open-source, cross-platform framework powering TikTok and CapCut. He is exploring what app frameworks should look like in the age of AI, and he was previously on the React Core Team at Meta.

speakers.name: Jovana Dunisijevic
speakers.title: Senior Technical Evangelist
speakers.bio: Jovana is a Senior Technical Evangelist and a Software Engineer at Atlassian, where she bridges the gap between product engineering and the developer community. Based in Sydney, Jovana specialises in showing, not just telling, how modern development teams can work smarter with AI-native tooling. With a background spanning and developer advocacy and hands-on engineering in platform engineering, performance and scalability, she's passionate about making complex workflows tangible through live demos and practical, real-world use cases. She's a 3 times finalist for Tech awards in ANZ region.

speakers.name: Christian Heilmann
speakers.title: VP Developer Relations
speakers.bio: Former Yahoo, Mozilla, Microsoft. JavaScript and Accessibility expert. Worked on Chromium Developer Tools. Author of the Developer Advocacy Handbook, LinkedIn Learning and Skillshare Trainer. Cinephile.

speakers.name: Jarek Kutylowski
speakers.title: CEO and Founder
speakers.bio: CEO and Founder of DeepL

speakers.name: Joe Atkinson
speakers.title: Co-founder
speakers.bio: Joe is the Co-Founder of PromptMates, a community helping Recruiting & HR leaders become AI-native. Recently Director of AI at Scede (now AI advisor) leading an AI transformation from scratch for a recruiting services business. Passionate about building the skillset of the future in public!

speakers.name: Julian Teicke
speakers.title: Co-founder and Executive Chairman
speakers.bio: Co-founder and Executive Chairman of The Delta

speakers.name: Markus Eisele
speakers.title: AI Developer Strategist
speakers.bio: I help Java teams build AI-ready, cloud-native systems that are fast to change and boring to operate. For more than 20 years I have worked with large enterprises on modernizing Java platforms. From heavy monoliths to Quarkus-based, container-first architectures. Today I focus on how Java, open source, and local AI models come together in real systems, not demos. I am a Java Champion, author, speaker, and developer strategist at IBM. I write the Enterprise Java and Quarkus Substack, The Main Thread, where I publish deep, hands-on tutorials.

speakers.name: Rasmus Klärck
speakers.title: Founding GTM Lead
speakers.bio: Rasmus Klärck is the Founding GTM Lead at Oplane. He is a repeat founder and operator: he co-founded the fintech startup CasaPay and spent four years as the first employee at the Nordic VC Icebreaker.vc, backing and building early-stage software companies. He is not a security engineer, and that is the point. He builds AI products himself (like Cosos, an AI agent that runs sales pipeline inside Slack), which is how he ran headfirst into the problem Oplane solves: AI now writes and ships code faster than any team can review it, and the risks that matter most are architectural, not line-level. Oplane is an AI security engineer that continuously threat-models your codebase, surfaces the real architectural risks in minutes, and drives the fix inside the developer workflow. It works with AI-native and regulated software companies, including Tandem Health and Miro, and is backed by a $5.2M seed round.

speakers.name: Rishabh Budhiraja
speakers.title: Staff Engineer
speakers.bio: Rishabh Budhiraja is Staff Engineer at idealo, specializing in cloud-native web applications, frontend modernization, performance engineering, and agentic system development. He works on high-traffic customer-facing systems across checkout and offer page experiences, building scalable features and improving user experience through experimentation and modern architecture. His work includes migrating legacy frontend systems to modern micro frontend architectures, optimizing Core Web Vitals, contributing to idealo’s company-wide frontend framework, and exploring MCP-based integrations for AI-enabled engineering workflows. With experience across React, Astro, AWS, Kubernetes, server-side rendering, and AI-assisted development, Rishabh focuses on building robust, scalable systems that help teams deliver faster and create better digital experiences.

speakers.name: Rishi Deorukhkar
speakers.title: AI Engineer
speakers.bio: Rishi Deorukhkar is AI Engineer at idealo specializing in AI-native systems, backend architecture, and cloud platform development. He builds agentic RAG pipelines, MCP applications, AI agents, and evaluation frameworks for automated compliance, audit workflows, document intelligence, and developer productivity. His work spans scalable APIs, vector databases, specification-driven coding agents, enterprise SaaS platforms, and eval-driven AI systems deployed across AWS and Azure. With experience in TypeScript, Python, LangChain, OpenAI, Claude, CDK, Pulumi, Kubernetes, and microservices architecture, Rishi focuses on turning complex business workflows into reliable, automated, AI-powered systems.

speakers.name: Stefan Ganß
speakers.title: Business Trainer
speakers.bio: Stefan Ganß is a journalist, TV reporter, and host with more than twenty years of experience in German public broadcasting, including ARD and MDR. He has won the German Radio Award and knows what it means to communicate under pressure: live on camera, in front of an audience, with no second take. Since 2023, he has also worked as a double-certified business trainer, trained at TAM Academy and certified by BDVT. As a storytelling trainer, he helps leaders, sales teams, researchers, and experts turn complex topics into stories that create clarity, trust, and connection. His clients include Siemens Energy, Techem, Berlitz, and several universities. His core belief: in the age of AI, human connection is becoming a real advantage. Storytelling is one of the most practical tools to build it.

speakers.name: Tolga Elser
speakers.title: Director Technical Architects
speakers.bio: Tolga Elser is Director of Technical Architects at Salesforce, leading the pre-sales technical architecture team across Germany and Austria. Based in Munich, he works at the intersection of enterprise architecture, AI, and developer experience — with a particular focus on agentic systems, headless design patterns, and what it actually takes to make enterprise infrastructure agent-ready. With a background spanning cloud architecture, information security, and enterprise transformation — and previous experience at Cognizant, Dimension Data, and Ramboll Management Consulting — Tolga brings a practitioner's lens to the theory. He's TOGAF certified and holds certifications across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.

speakers.name: Yiyang Lu
speakers.title: Head of R&D and Co-founder
speakers.bio: Yiyang Lu is a self-taught engineer and entrepreneur at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence and robotics. As the founder behind initiatives such as pluto and pdm R&D Labs, he is dedicated to developing advanced technologies that prioritize human-centric design and societal impact. With a multidisciplinary background spanning from quantitative analysis at the London School of Economics to practical engineering, Yiyang advocates for a future where innovation acts as a catalyst for human capability rather than a replacement. Currently based in Berlin, he is active in the tech ecosystem, focusing on methodologies that bridge the gap between complex technical implementation and ethical, real-world application.

speakers.name: Yuval Dvir
speakers.title: Commercial Officer
speakers.bio: Commercial Officer at SandboxAQ

speakers.name: Zaak Chalal
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Zaak Chalal is the founder and CEO of Mobioos, a company pioneering context engineering for software development and AI-assisted delivery. A software engineer and entrepreneur for more than 28 years, Zaak wrote his first programs on a Commodore 64 and later contributed to some of Europe’s earliest large-scale digital initiatives, including Ooshop, Carrefour’s pioneering e-commerce platform. Throughout his career, he has led software engineering, architecture, and digital transformation initiatives across multiple industries. His experience exposed a recurring challenge: organizations struggle to preserve and operationalize the business context behind their software systems. Today, through Mobioos, Zaak focuses on helping organizations transform business and technical context into a strategic asset that can be understood and activated by humans, AI systems, and autonomous agents. His work challenges the traditional assumption that code alone is the source of truth and explores how context can become the foundation for reliable AI-driven software engineering.

speakers.name: Jörg Krall
speakers.title: Senior Business Development Manager
speakers.bio: Joerg Krall is a Senior Industry Business Development Manager for Automotive EMEA at NVIDIA, where he drives business development and strategic partnerships across Europe’s automotive ecosystem. Based in Germany, Joerg works with leading OEMs, Tier 1 suppliers, and technology partners to accelerate the adoption of NVIDIA’s automotive platforms — including NVIDIA DRIVE — across the region. With deep expertise spanning enterprise GPU solutions, XR/visualisation, and autonomous vehicle technologies, Joerg brings a broad technical and commercial perspective to NVIDIA’s automotive go-to-market in EMEA. He is a regular speaker at industry events, having presented at forums such as the XR-Symposium at SRH Hochschule Heidelberg, and has worked extensively with enterprise and industrial customers across Germany and Europe. Prior to his focus on automotive, Joerg held roles within NVIDIA’s Worldwide Field Operations supporting enterprise visualisation and vGPU deployments for customers including Thales, Atos, and science+computing ag.

speakers.name: Peter Swierzy
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: Peter Swierzy is the CEO of BlockyDevs, a R&D software development company specializing in blockchain, Web3, fintech, Agentic Commerce, and AI-driven software development. He helps startups, foundations, and enterprise clients turn complex technical ideas into production-ready products. Peter is especially focused on Agentic Commerce, enabling AI agents to discover services, initiate payments, interact with APIs, and participate in digital transactions autonomously. His work also explores how AI-assisted development, agent-based workflows, and decentralized infrastructure can create practical value beyond the hype.

speakers.name: Giampaolo Casolla
speakers.title: Senior Data Scientist
speakers.bio: Giampaolo Casolla is a Senior Data Scientist at GetYourGuide in Berlin, working at the intersection of machine learning, generative AI, and product development. He builds AI-powered experiences used by travelers at scale, including GetYourGuide’s AI Discovery Agent in ChatGPT and AI Itineraries on Google Search. Previously at Amazon, his work on anomaly detection and forecasting contributed to more than $20M in cost savings. He holds an MSc in Applied Mathematics cum laude, has nine published papers with 380+ citations, and supports the local data community as Chair of the PyData Berlin Video Committee.

speakers.name: Jens Petersohn
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.bio: Jens is CTO of Home-of-Tech and Mercedes-Benz Tech Innovation. His journey into tech began around 2006: debugging Internet Explorer 6, writing PHP that would make any modern linter weep, and genuinely believing that document.all was a reasonable API. Somehow, that did not scare him off. He joined Mercedes-Benz as a software engineer in 2014, and by 2017 had taken on additional responsibility as Principal for Connected Vehicle Services, shaping how cars talk to the cloud and, occasionally, back. Since 2023, he serves as CTO, driving innovation, embedding AI across the product development lifecycle, and steering a 7,500-person engineering organization at the heart of Mercedes-Benz's global software ecosystem. He still has opinions about JavaScript. They've just gotten harder to revert.

speakers.name: Martin Karrer
speakers.title: Senior Solutions Architect
speakers.bio: Senior Solutions Architect with a background as a Software Developer in Advertising Technology, now working at Amazon Web Services helping customers build scalable, large-scale applications on AWS. Elixir Developer and Database Enthusiast.

speakers.name: Jordi Spranger
speakers.bio: Jordi Spranger is an independent AI and robotics engineer based in Munich, bringing agentic AI to mobile robotics. He has delivered production-grade machine-learning solutions for large enterprises and built the intelligence layer for autonomous mobile robots. He is currently develop ing spatial-memory systems for embodied agents on top of knowledge graphs, in line with his aspiring robotics startup for agentic robots.

speakers.name: Chadha Sridi
speakers.title: Developer Relations Engineer
speakers.bio: Chadha is a Developer Relations Engineer at Qdrant focused on vector search, retrieval systems, and AI agents. Her academic background includes a dual engineering degree specializing in cybersecurity and mathematical and computational engineering, along with a master’s degree in computer vision. She is particularly interested in building scalable and reliable AI systems powered by semantic retrieval and generative models.

speakers.name: Ed Huang
speakers.title: TiDB
speakers.bio: Co-founder & CTO at PingCAP (the company behind TiDB), an entrepreneur with a hacker's mind & heart. Full-stack engineer, for real. Pioneer in building cloud-native systems. Building distributed systems and databases with ♥️. Building a company like a distributed system.

speakers.name: Olivier Pomel
speakers.title: CEO
speakers.bio: CEO of Datadog

speakers.name: Sara Jane Kenny
speakers.title: Jr Integration Specialist
speakers.bio: Sara Jane is a Jr Integration Specialist at the Algorand Foundation, focused on integrations, developer tooling, and developer relations. Previously a Technical Ecosystem Specialist, she worked on onboarding founders, builders, and developers through community, education, and content initiatives. With 5+ years of experience in the Web3 space, she holds a degree in Software Development and has been featured in Cointelegraph, Business Post, and more.

speakers.bio: Joerg Krall is a Senior Industry Business Development Manager for Automotive EMEA at NVIDIA, where he drives business development and strategic partnerships across Europe’s automotive ecosystem. Based in Germany, Joerg works with leading OEMs, Tier 1 suppliers, and technology partners to accelerate the adoption of NVIDIA’s automotive platforms — including NVIDIA DRIVE — across the region. With deep expertise spanning enterprise GPU solutions, XR/visualisation, and autonomous vehicle technologies, Joerg brings a broad technical and commercial perspective to NVIDIA’s automotive go-to-market in EMEA. He is a regular speaker at industry events, having presented at forums such as the XR-Symposium at SRH Hochschule Heidelberg, and has worked extensively with enterprise and industrial customers across Germany and Europe. Prior to his focus on automotive, Joerg held roles within NVIDIA’s Worldwide Field Operations supporting enterprise visualisation and vGPU deployments for customers including Thales, Atos, and science+computing ag.
